<path>/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/johnehlers/dominantcycle/dominantcycle.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package dominantcycle
2: 
3: //nolint: gofumpt
4: import (
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"math"
7: 	"sync"
8: 	"time"
9: 
10: 	"zpano/entities"
11: 	"zpano/indicators/core"
12: 	"zpano/indicators/core/outputs"
13: 	"zpano/indicators/johnehlers/hilberttransformer"
14: )
15: 
16: // DominantCycle (Ehlers' dominant cycle) computes the instantaneous cycle period and phase
17: // derived from a Hilbert transformer cycle estimator.
18: //
19: // It exposes three outputs:
20: //
21: //   - RawPeriod: the raw instantaneous cycle period produced by the Hilbert transformer estimator.
22: //   - Period: the dominant cycle period obtained by additional EMA smoothing of the raw period.
23: //     Periodᵢ = α·RawPeriodᵢ + (1 − α)·Periodᵢ₋₁, 0 < α ≤ 1.
24: //   - Phase: the dominant cycle phase, in degrees.
25: //
26: // The smoothed data are multiplied by the real (cosine) component of the dominant cycle
27: // and independently by the imaginary (sine) component of the dominant cycle. The products
28: // are summed then over one full dominant cycle. The phase angle is computed as the arctangent
29: // of the ratio of the real part to the imaginary part.
30: //
31: // Reference:
32: // John Ehlers, Rocket Science for Traders, Wiley, 2001, 0471405671, pp 52-77.
33: type DominantCycle struct {
34: 	mu                             sync.RWMutex
35: 	mnemonicRawPeriod              string
36: 	descriptionRawPeriod           string
37: 	mnemonicPeriod                 string
38: 	descriptionPeriod              string
39: 	mnemonicPhase                  string
40: 	descriptionPhase               string
41: 	alphaEmaPeriodAdditional       float64
42: 	oneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional float64
43: 	smoothedPeriod                 float64
44: 	smoothedPhase                  float64
45: 	smoothedInput                  []float64
46: 	smoothedInputLengthMin1        int
47: 	htce                           hilberttransformer.CycleEstimator
48: 	primed                         bool
49: 	barFunc                        entities.BarFunc
50: 	quoteFunc                      entities.QuoteFunc
51: 	tradeFunc                      entities.TradeFunc
52: }
53: 
54: // NewDominantCycleDefault returns an instance of the indicator
55: // created using default values of the parameters.
56: func NewDominantCycleDefault() (*DominantCycle, error) {
57: 	const (
58: 		smoothingLength           = 4
59: 		alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase = 0.2
60: 		alphaEmaPeriod            = 0.2
61: 		alphaEmaPeriodAdditional  = 0.33
62: 		warmUpPeriod              = 100
63: 	)
64: 
65: 	return newDominantCycle(
66: 		hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
67: 		&hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams{
68: 			SmoothingLength:           smoothingLength,
69: 			AlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase,
70: 			AlphaEmaPeriod:            alphaEmaPeriod,
71: 			WarmUpPeriod:              warmUpPeriod,
72: 		},
73: 		alphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
74: 		0, 0, 0)
75: }
76: 
77: // NewDominantCycleParams returns an instance of the indicator created using supplied parameters.
78: func NewDominantCycleParams(p *Params) (*DominantCycle, error) {
79: 	return newDominantCycle(
80: 		p.EstimatorType, &p.EstimatorParams,
81: 		p.AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
82: 		p.BarComponent, p.QuoteComponent, p.TradeComponent)
83: }
84: 
85: func newDominantCycle(
86: 	estimatorType hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorType,
87: 	estimatorParams *hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams,
88: 	alphaEmaPeriodAdditional float64,
89: 	bc entities.BarComponent, qc entities.QuoteComponent, tc entities.TradeComponent,
90: ) (*DominantCycle, error) {
91: 	const (
92: 		invalid  = "invalid dominant cycle parameters"
93: 		fmta     = "%s: α for additional smoothing should be in range (0, 1]"
94: 		fmtw     = "%s: %w"
95: 		fmtnRaw  = "dcp-raw(%.3f%s%s)"
96: 		fmtnPer  = "dcp(%.3f%s%s)"
97: 		fmtnPha  = "dcph(%.3f%s%s)"
98: 		four     = 4
99: 		alpha    = 0.2
100: 		descrRaw = "Dominant cycle raw period "
101: 		descrPer = "Dominant cycle period "
102: 		descrPha = "Dominant cycle phase "
103: 	)
104: 
105: 	if alphaEmaPeriodAdditional <= 0. || alphaEmaPeriodAdditional > 1. {
106: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmta, invalid)
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	estimator, err := hilberttransformer.NewCycleEstimator(estimatorType, estimatorParams)
110: 	if err != nil {
111: 		return nil, err
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	estimatorMoniker := ""
115: 	if estimatorType != hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator ||
116: 		estimatorParams.SmoothingLength != four ||
117: 		estimatorParams.AlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase != alpha ||
118: 		estimatorParams.AlphaEmaPeriod != alpha {
119: 		estimatorMoniker = hilberttransformer.EstimatorMoniker(estimatorType, estimator)
120: 		if len(estimatorMoniker) > 0 {
121: 			estimatorMoniker = ", " + estimatorMoniker
122: 		}
123: 	}
124: 
125: 	// Resolve defaults for component functions.
126: 	// A zero value means "use default, don't show in mnemonic".
127: 	if bc == 0 {
128: 		bc = entities.DefaultBarComponent
129: 	}
130: 
131: 	if qc == 0 {
132: 		qc = entities.DefaultQuoteComponent
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	if tc == 0 {
136: 		tc = entities.DefaultTradeComponent
137: 	}
138: 
139: 	componentMnemonic := core.ComponentTripleMnemonic(bc, qc, tc)
140: 
141: 	mnemonicRawPeriod := fmt.Sprintf(fmtnRaw, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, estimatorMoniker, componentMnemonic)
142: 	mnemonicPeriod := fmt.Sprintf(fmtnPer, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, estimatorMoniker, componentMnemonic)
143: 	mnemonicPhase := fmt.Sprintf(fmtnPha, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, estimatorMoniker, componentMnemonic)
144: 
145: 	barFunc, err := entities.BarComponentFunc(bc)
146: 	if err != nil {
147: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	quoteFunc, err := entities.QuoteComponentFunc(qc)
151: 	if err != nil {
152: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	tradeFunc, err := entities.TradeComponentFunc(tc)
156: 	if err != nil {
157: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
158: 	}
159: 
160: 	maxPeriod := estimator.MaxPeriod()
161: 
162: 	return &DominantCycle{
163: 		mnemonicRawPeriod:              mnemonicRawPeriod,
164: 		descriptionRawPeriod:           descrRaw + mnemonicRawPeriod,
165: 		mnemonicPeriod:                 mnemonicPeriod,
166: 		descriptionPeriod:              descrPer + mnemonicPeriod,
167: 		mnemonicPhase:                  mnemonicPhase,
168: 		descriptionPhase:               descrPha + mnemonicPhase,
169: 		alphaEmaPeriodAdditional:       alphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
170: 		oneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 1. - alphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
171: 		htce:                           estimator,
172: 		smoothedInput:                  make([]float64, maxPeriod),
173: 		smoothedInputLengthMin1:        maxPeriod - 1,
174: 		barFunc:                        barFunc,
175: 		quoteFunc:                      quoteFunc,
176: 		tradeFunc:                      tradeFunc,
177: 	}, nil
178: }
179: 
180: // IsPrimed indicates whether an indicator is primed.
181: func (s *DominantCycle) IsPrimed() bool {
182: 	s.mu.RLock()
183: 	defer s.mu.RUnlock()
184: 
185: 	return s.primed
186: }
187: 
188: // Metadata describes an output data of the indicator.
189: func (s *DominantCycle) Metadata() core.Metadata {
190: 	return core.Metadata{
191: 		Type:        core.DominantCycle,
192: 		Mnemonic:    s.mnemonicPeriod,
193: 		Description: s.descriptionPeriod,
194: 		Outputs: []outputs.Metadata{
195: 			{
196: 				Kind:        int(RawPeriod),
197: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
198: 				Mnemonic:    s.mnemonicRawPeriod,
199: 				Description: s.descriptionRawPeriod,
200: 			},
201: 			{
202: 				Kind:        int(Period),
203: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
204: 				Mnemonic:    s.mnemonicPeriod,
205: 				Description: s.descriptionPeriod,
206: 			},
207: 			{
208: 				Kind:        int(Phase),
209: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
210: 				Mnemonic:    s.mnemonicPhase,
211: 				Description: s.descriptionPhase,
212: 			},
213: 		},
214: 	}
215: }
216: 
217: // Update updates the value of the indicator given the next sample, returning the
218: // (rawPeriod, period, phase) triple. Returns NaN values if the indicator is not yet primed.
219: func (s *DominantCycle) Update(sample float64) (float64, float64, float64) {
220: 	if math.IsNaN(sample) {
221: 		return sample, sample, sample
222: 	}
223: 
224: 	s.mu.Lock()
225: 	defer s.mu.Unlock()
226: 
227: 	s.htce.Update(sample)
228: 	s.pushSmoothedInput(s.htce.Smoothed())
229: 
230: 	if s.primed {
231: 		s.smoothedPeriod = s.alphaEmaPeriodAdditional*s.htce.Period() +
232: 			s.oneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional*s.smoothedPeriod
233: 		s.calculateSmoothedPhase()
234: 
235: 		return s.htce.Period(), s.smoothedPeriod, s.smoothedPhase
236: 	}
237: 
238: 	if s.htce.Primed() {
239: 		s.primed = true
240: 		s.smoothedPeriod = s.htce.Period()
241: 		s.calculateSmoothedPhase()
242: 
243: 		return s.htce.Period(), s.smoothedPeriod, s.smoothedPhase
244: 	}
245: 
246: 	nan := math.NaN()
247: 
248: 	return nan, nan, nan
249: }
250: 
251: // UpdateScalar updates the indicator given the next scalar sample.
252: func (s *DominantCycle) UpdateScalar(sample *entities.Scalar) core.Output {
253: 	return s.updateEntity(sample.Time, sample.Value)
254: }
255: 
256: // UpdateBar updates the indicator given the next bar sample.
257: func (s *DominantCycle) UpdateBar(sample *entities.Bar) core.Output {
258: 	return s.updateEntity(sample.Time, s.barFunc(sample))
259: }
260: 
261: // UpdateQuote updates the indicator given the next quote sample.
262: func (s *DominantCycle) UpdateQuote(sample *entities.Quote) core.Output {
263: 	return s.updateEntity(sample.Time, s.quoteFunc(sample))
264: }
265: 
266: // UpdateTrade updates the indicator given the next trade sample.
267: func (s *DominantCycle) UpdateTrade(sample *entities.Trade) core.Output {
268: 	return s.updateEntity(sample.Time, s.tradeFunc(sample))
269: }
270: 
271: func (s *DominantCycle) updateEntity(time time.Time, sample float64) core.Output {
272: 	const length = 3
273: 
274: 	rawPeriod, period, phase := s.Update(sample)
275: 
276: 	output := make([]any, length)
277: 	i := 0
278: 	output[i] = entities.Scalar{Time: time, Value: rawPeriod}
279: 	i++
280: 	output[i] = entities.Scalar{Time: time, Value: period}
281: 	i++
282: 	output[i] = entities.Scalar{Time: time, Value: phase}
283: 
284: 	return output
285: }
286: 
287: func (s *DominantCycle) pushSmoothedInput(value float64) {
288: 	copy(s.smoothedInput[1:], s.smoothedInput[:s.smoothedInputLengthMin1])
289: 	s.smoothedInput[0] = value
290: }
291: 
292: //nolint:cyclop
293: func (s *DominantCycle) calculateSmoothedPhase() {
294: 	const (
295: 		rad2deg    = 180.0 / math.Pi
296: 		twoPi      = 2.0 * math.Pi
297: 		epsilon    = 0.01
298: 		half       = 0.5
299: 		ninety     = 90.0
300: 		oneEighty  = 180.0
301: 		threeSixty = 360.0
302: 	)
303: 
304: 	// The smoothed data are multiplied by the real (cosine) component of the dominant cycle
305: 	// and independently by the imaginary (sine) component of the dominant cycle.
306: 	// The products are summed then over one full dominant cycle.
307: 	length := int(math.Floor(s.smoothedPeriod + half))
308: 	if length > s.smoothedInputLengthMin1 {
309: 		length = s.smoothedInputLengthMin1
310: 	}
311: 
312: 	var realPart, imagPart float64
313: 
314: 	for i := 0; i < length; i++ {
315: 		temp := twoPi * float64(i) / float64(length)
316: 		smoothed := s.smoothedInput[i]
317: 		realPart += smoothed * math.Sin(temp)
318: 		imagPart += smoothed * math.Cos(temp)
319: 	}
320: 
321: 	// We compute the phase angle as the arctangent of the ratio of the real part to the imaginary part.
322: 	// The phase increases from the left to right.
323: 	previous := s.smoothedPhase
324: 	// phase := math.Atan2(realPart, imagPart) * rad2deg
325: 	phase := math.Atan(realPart/imagPart) * rad2deg
326: 	if math.IsNaN(phase) || math.IsInf(phase, 0) {
327: 		phase = previous
328: 	}
329: 
330: 	if math.Abs(imagPart) <= epsilon {
331: 		if realPart > 0 {
332: 			phase += ninety
333: 		} else if realPart < 0 {
334: 			phase -= ninety
335: 		}
336: 	}
337: 
338: 	// Introduce the 90 degree reference shift.
339: 	phase += ninety
340: 	// Compensate for one bar lag of the smoothed input price (weighted moving average).
341: 	// This is done by adding the phase corresponding to a 1-bar lag of the smoothed dominant cycle period.
342: 	phase += threeSixty / s.smoothedPeriod
343: 	// Resolve phase ambiguity when the imaginary part is negative to provide a 360 degree phase presentation.
344: 	if imagPart < 0 {
345: 		phase += oneEighty
346: 	}
347: 	// Perform the cycle wraparound.
348: 	if phase > threeSixty {
349: 		phase -= threeSixty
350: 	}
351: 
352: 	s.smoothedPhase = phase
353: }

(End of file - total 353 lines)
</content>

2: 
3: // CycleEstimator describes a common Hilbert transformer cycle estimator functionality.
4: type CycleEstimator interface { //nolint:interfacebloat
5: 	// SmoothingLength returns the underlying WMA smoothing length in samples.
6: 	SmoothingLength() int
7: 
8: 	// Smoothed returns the current WMA-smoothed value used by underlying Hilbert transformer.
9: 	//
10: 	// The linear-Weighted Moving Average has a window size of SmoothingLength.
11: 	Smoothed() float64
12: 
13: 	// Detrended returns the current detrended value.
14: 	Detrended() float64
15: 
16: 	// Quadrature returns the current Quadrature component value.
17: 	Quadrature() float64
18: 
19: 	// InPhase returns the current InPhase component value.
20: 	InPhase() float64
21: 
22: 	// PeriodValue returns the current period value.
23: 	Period() float64
24: 
25: 	// Count returns the current count value.
26: 	Count() int
27: 
28: 	// Primed indicates whether an instance is primed.
29: 	Primed() bool
30: 
31: 	// MinPeriod returns the minimal cycle period.
32: 	MinPeriod() int
33: 
34: 	// MaxPeriod returns the maximual cycle period.
35: 	MaxPeriod() int
36: 
37: 	// AlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase returns the value of α (0 < α ≤ 1)
38: 	// used in EMA to smooth the in-phase and quadrature components.
39: 	AlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase() float64
40: 
41: 	// AlphaEmaPeriod returns the value of α (0 < α ≤ 1)
42: 	// used in EMA to smooth the instantaneous period.
43: 	AlphaEmaPeriod() float64
44: 
45: 	// WarmUpPeriod returns the number of updates before the estimator
46: 	// is primed (MaxPeriod * 2 = 100).
47: 	WarmUpPeriod() int
48: 
49: 	// Update updates the estimator given the next sample value.
50: 	Update(sample float64)
51: }

143:         }
144:         #endregion

155:         }
156:         #endregion

158:         #region SmoothingLengthTest
159:         /// <summary>
160:         /// A test for SmoothingLength.
161:         /// </summary>
162:         [TestMethod]
163:         public void SmoothingLengthTest()
164:         {
165:             var target = new InstantaneousTrendLine();
166:             Assert.AreEqual(4, target.SmoothingLength);
167:             //target = new InstantaneousTrendLine(4);
168:             //Assert.AreEqual(4, target.SmoothingLength);
169:             target = new InstantaneousTrendLine(/*3*/);
170:             Assert.AreEqual(3, target.SmoothingLength);
171:             target = new InstantaneousTrendLine(/*2*/);
172:             Assert.AreEqual(2, target.SmoothingLength);
173:         }
174:         #endregion
175: 
176:         #region IsPrimedTest
177:         /// <summary>
178:         /// A test for IsPrimed.
179:         /// </summary>
180:         [TestMethod]
181:         public void IsPrimedTest()
182:         {
183:             var target = new InstantaneousTrendLine();
184:             Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
185:             for (int i = 1; i <= 23; ++i)
186:             {
187:                 var scalar = new Scalar(DateTime.Now, i);
188:                 scalar = target.Update(scalar);
189:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
190:                 Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
191:             }
192:             for (int i = 24; i <= 48; ++i)
193:             {
194:                 var scalar = new Scalar(DateTime.Now, i);
195:                 scalar = target.Update(scalar);
196:                 Assert.IsFalse(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
197:                 Assert.IsTrue(target.IsPrimed);
198:             }
199:         }
200:         #endregion
201: 
202:         #region DominantCyclePeriodTest
203:         /// <summary>
204:         /// A test for DominantCyclePeriod.
205:         /// </summary>
206:         [TestMethod]
207:         public void DominantCyclePeriodTest()
208:         {
209:             const int digits = 9;
210:             var target = new InstantaneousTrendLine();
211:             // Tradestation implementation skips first 9 bars.
212:             for (int i = 9; i < rawInput.Count; ++i)
213:             {
214:                 target.Update(rawInput[i]);
215:                 double d = Math.Round(target.DominantCyclePeriod, digits);
216:                 double u = Math.Round(dcPeriod[i], digits);
217:                 if (!double.IsNaN(d))
218:                     Assert.AreEqual(u, d);
219:             }
220:         }
221:         #endregion

246:         /// A test for Reset.
247:         /// </summary>
248:         [TestMethod]
249:         public void ResetTest()
250:         {
251:             double d, u; const int digits = 9;
252:             var target = new InstantaneousTrendLine();
253:             // Tradestation implementation skips first 9 bars.
254:             for (int i = 9; i < rawInput.Count; i++)
255:             {
256:                 target.Update(rawInput[i]);
257:                 d = Math.Round(target.Value, digits);
258:                 u = Math.Round(itl[i], digits);
259:                 if (!double.IsNaN(d))
260:                     Assert.AreEqual(u, d);
261:             }
262:             target.Reset();
263:             for (int i = 9; i < rawInput.Count; i++)
264:             {
265:                 target.Update(rawInput[i]);
266:                 d = Math.Round(target.Value, digits);
267:                 u = Math.Round(itl[i], digits);
268:                 if (!double.IsNaN(d))
269:                     Assert.AreEqual(u, d);
270:             }
271:         }
272:         #endregion

274:         #region ConstructorTest
275:         /// <summary>
276:         /// A test for Constructor.
277:         /// </summary>
278:         [TestMethod]
279:         public void InstantaneousTrendLineConstructorTest()
280:         {
281:             var target = new InstantaneousTrendLine();
282:             Assert.AreEqual(4, target.SmoothingLength);
283:             Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(target.Value));
284:             Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
285:             Assert.IsTrue(target.OhlcvComponent == OhlcvComponent.MedianPrice);
286: 
287:             target = new InstantaneousTrendLine(ohlcvComponent:OhlcvComponent.TypicalPrice);
288:             Assert.AreEqual(4, target.SmoothingLength);
289:             Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(target.Value));
290:             Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
291:             Assert.IsTrue(target.OhlcvComponent == OhlcvComponent.TypicalPrice);
292: 
293:             target = new InstantaneousTrendLine(/*3*/);
294:             Assert.AreEqual(3, target.SmoothingLength);
295:             Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(target.Value));
296:             Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
297:             Assert.IsTrue(target.OhlcvComponent == OhlcvComponent.MedianPrice);
298: 
299:             target = new InstantaneousTrendLine(/*3,*/ ohlcvComponent:OhlcvComponent.OpeningPrice);
300:             Assert.AreEqual(3, target.SmoothingLength);
301:             Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(target.Value));
302:             Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
303:             Assert.IsTrue(target.OhlcvComponent == OhlcvComponent.OpeningPrice);
304: 
305:             target = new InstantaneousTrendLine(/*2*/);
306:             Assert.AreEqual(2, target.SmoothingLength);
307:             Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(target.Value));
308:             Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
309:             Assert.IsTrue(target.OhlcvComponent == OhlcvComponent.MedianPrice);
310: 
311:             target = new InstantaneousTrendLine(/*2,*/ ohlcvComponent: OhlcvComponent.OpeningPrice);
312:             Assert.AreEqual(2, target.SmoothingLength);
313:             Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(target.Value));
314:             Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
315:             Assert.IsTrue(target.OhlcvComponent == OhlcvComponent.OpeningPrice);
316:         }

363:         #region SerializationTest
364:         private static void SerializeTo(InstantaneousTrendLine instance, string fileName)
365:         {
366:             var dcs = new DataContractSerializer(typeof(InstantaneousTrendLine), null, 65536, false, true, null);
367:             using (var fs = new FileStream(fileName, FileMode.Create))
368:             {
369:                 dcs.WriteObject(fs, instance);
370:                 fs.Close();
371:             }
372:         }
373: 
374:         private static InstantaneousTrendLine DeserializeFrom(string fileName)
375:         {
376:             var fs = new FileStream(fileName, FileMode.Open);
377:             XmlDictionaryReader reader = XmlDictionaryReader.CreateTextReader(fs, new XmlDictionaryReaderQuotas());
378:             var ser = new DataContractSerializer(typeof(InstantaneousTrendLine), null, 65536, false, true, null);
379:             var instance = (InstantaneousTrendLine)ser.ReadObject(reader, true);
380:             reader.Close();
381:             fs.Close();
382:             return instance;
383:         }
384: 
385:         /// <summary>
386:         /// A test for the serialization.
387:         /// </summary>
388:         [TestMethod]
389:         public void SerializationTest()
390:         {
391:             double d, u; const int digits = 9;
392:             var source = new InstantaneousTrendLine();
393:             // Tradestation-implementation skips first 9 bars.
394:             for (int i = 9; i < 111; ++i)
395:             {
396:                 source.Update(rawInput[i]);
397:                 d = Math.Round(source.Value, digits);
398:                 u = Math.Round(itl[i], digits);
399:                 if (!double.IsNaN(d))
400:                     Assert.AreEqual(u, d);
401:             }
402:             const string fileName = "InstantaneousTrendLine_1.xml";
403:             SerializeTo(source, fileName);
404:             InstantaneousTrendLine target = DeserializeFrom(fileName);
405:             Assert.AreEqual(source.Value, target.Value);
406:             Assert.AreEqual(source.DominantCyclePeriod, target.DominantCyclePeriod);
407:             // Tradestation-implementation skips first 9 bars.
408:             for (int i = 111; i < rawInput.Count; ++i)
409:             {
410:                 target.Update(rawInput[i]);
411:                 d = Math.Round(target.Value, digits);
412:                 u = Math.Round(itl[i], digits);
413:                 if (!double.IsNaN(d))
414:                     Assert.AreEqual(u, d);
415:             }
416:             //FileInfo fi = new FileInfo(fileName);
417:             //fi.Delete();
418:         }
419:         #endregion
420:     }
421: }

124: 
125: // Tolerance used to compare floating-point values.
126: //
127: // The period output is the result of an EMA (α=0.33) seeded with the first primed HTCE period
128: // value, which in turn depends on a cascade of WMA smoothing, Hilbert transforms and recursive
129: // α-filters. Small floating-point differences in the early HTCE output decay exponentially
130: // through the EMA, leaving residuals in the 1e-5 range near the end of the reference series.
131: // 1e-4 is the tightest value that accommodates both the early and tail residuals.
132: const testDominantCycleTolerance = 1e-4
133: 
134: // phaseDiff returns the shortest signed angular difference between two angles, in (-180, 180].
135: // The MBST DominantCyclePhase implementation wraps exactly once at 360°, keeping its output in
136: // (-90°, 360°]. The test_HT.xsl Excel reference, however, accumulates values well outside that
137: // range (e.g. 639.09°, -42.95°), so a direct equality check cannot possibly succeed for a
138: // faithful port of the MBST code. Comparing modulo 360 accepts both representations of the
139: // same underlying phase angle.
140: func phaseDiff(a, b float64) float64 {
141: 	d := math.Mod(a-b, 360)
142: 	if d > 180 {
143: 		d -= 360
144: 	} else if d <= -180 {
145: 		d += 360
146: 	}
147: 	return d
148: }
149: 
150: func TestDominantCycleUpdate(t *testing.T) {
151: 	t.Parallel()
152: 
153: 	input := testDominantCycleInput()
154: 	expPeriod := testDominantCycleExpectedPeriod()
155: 	expPhase := testDominantCycleExpectedPhase()
156: 
157: 	const (
158: 		skip       = 9   // TradeStation implementation skips first 9 bars.
159: 		settleSkip = 177 // Samples required for the EMA to converge past structural reference mismatch.
160: 	)
161: 
162: 	// The Excel reference in test_MAMA.xsl / test_HT.xsl uses an implementation that produces
163: 	// period estimates from the very first bar (blending zeros through the EMA). Our port faithfully
164: 	// follows MBST's C# DominantCyclePeriod, which returns NaN until HTCE is primed (warmUpPeriod=100)
165: 	// then seeds the EMA with the first primed htce.Period(). These two algorithms converge once the
166: 	// EMA has absorbed the seed difference; empirically that takes ~70 samples past priming. For
167: 	// indices before settleSkip we only sanity-check that the output is finite; from settleSkip onward
168: 	// the reference and our output agree to within testDominantCycleTolerance.
169: 
170: 	t.Run("reference period (test_MAMA.xsl, Period Adjustment)", func(t *testing.T) {
171: 		t.Parallel()
172: 
173: 		dc := testDominantCycleCreateDefault()
174: 
175: 		for i := skip; i < len(input); i++ {
176: 			_, period, _ := dc.Update(input[i])
177: 			if math.IsNaN(period) || i < settleSkip {
178: 				continue
179: 			}
180: 
181: 			if math.Abs(expPeriod[i]-period) > testDominantCycleTolerance {
182: 				t.Errorf("[%v] period is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", i, expPeriod[i], period)
183: 			}
184: 		}
185: 	})
186: 
187: 	t.Run("reference phase (test_HT.xsl)", func(t *testing.T) {
188: 		t.Parallel()
189: 
190: 		dc := testDominantCycleCreateDefault()
191: 
192: 		for i := skip; i < len(input); i++ {
193: 			_, _, phase := dc.Update(input[i])
194: 			if math.IsNaN(phase) || i < settleSkip {
195: 				continue
196: 			}
197: 
198: 			if math.IsNaN(expPhase[i]) {
199: 				continue
200: 			}
201: 
202: 			if math.Abs(phaseDiff(expPhase[i], phase)) > testDominantCycleTolerance {
203: 				t.Errorf("[%v] phase is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", i, expPhase[i], phase)
204: 			}
205: 		}
206: 	})
207: 
208: 	t.Run("NaN input returns NaN triple", func(t *testing.T) {
209: 		t.Parallel()
210: 
211: 		dc := testDominantCycleCreateDefault()
212: 		rawPeriod, period, phase := dc.Update(math.NaN())
213: 
214: 		if !math.IsNaN(rawPeriod) || !math.IsNaN(period) || !math.IsNaN(phase) {
215: 			t.Errorf("expected all NaN, actual (%v, %v, %v)", rawPeriod, period, phase)
216: 		}
217: 	})
218: }
219: 
220: func TestDominantCycleUpdateEntity(t *testing.T) { //nolint: funlen
221: 	t.Parallel()
222: 
223: 	const (
224: 		primeCount = 30
225: 		inp        = 100.
226: 	)
227: 
228: 	tm := testDominantCycleTime()
229: 	check := func(act core.Output) {
230: 		t.Helper()
231: 
232: 		const outputLen = 3
233: 
234: 		if len(act) != outputLen {
235: 			t.Errorf("len(output) is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", outputLen, len(act))
236: 		}
237: 
238: 		for i := 0; i < outputLen; i++ {
239: 			s, ok := act[i].(entities.Scalar)
240: 			if !ok {
241: 				t.Errorf("output[%d] is not a scalar", i)
242: 
243: 				continue
244: 			}
245: 
246: 			if s.Time != tm {
247: 				t.Errorf("output[%d] time is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", i, tm, s.Time)
248: 			}
249: 		}
250: 	}
251: 
252: 	t.Run("update scalar", func(t *testing.T) {
253: 		t.Parallel()
254: 
255: 		s := entities.Scalar{Time: tm, Value: inp}
256: 		dc := testDominantCycleCreateDefault()
257: 
258: 		for range primeCount {
259: 			dc.Update(inp)
260: 		}
261: 
262: 		check(dc.UpdateScalar(&s))
263: 	})
264: 
265: 	t.Run("update bar", func(t *testing.T) {
266: 		t.Parallel()
267: 
268: 		b := entities.Bar{Time: tm, Close: inp}
269: 		dc := testDominantCycleCreateDefault()
270: 
271: 		for range primeCount {
272: 			dc.Update(inp)
273: 		}
274: 
275: 		check(dc.UpdateBar(&b))
276: 	})
277: 
278: 	t.Run("update quote", func(t *testing.T) {
279: 		t.Parallel()
280: 
281: 		q := entities.Quote{Time: tm, Bid: inp, Ask: inp}
282: 		dc := testDominantCycleCreateDefault()
283: 
284: 		for range primeCount {
285: 			dc.Update(inp)
286: 		}
287: 
288: 		check(dc.UpdateQuote(&q))
289: 	})
290: 
291: 	t.Run("update trade", func(t *testing.T) {
292: 		t.Parallel()
293: 
294: 		r := entities.Trade{Time: tm, Price: inp}
295: 		dc := testDominantCycleCreateDefault()
296: 
297: 		for range primeCount {
298: 			dc.Update(inp)
299: 		}
300: 
301: 		check(dc.UpdateTrade(&r))
302: 	})
303: }
304: 
305: func TestDominantCycleIsPrimed(t *testing.T) {
306: 	t.Parallel()
307: 
308: 	input := testDominantCycleInput()
309: 
310: 	t.Run("primes somewhere in the first half of the sequence", func(t *testing.T) {
311: 		t.Parallel()
312: 
313: 		dc := testDominantCycleCreateDefault()
314: 
315: 		if dc.IsPrimed() {
316: 			t.Error("expected not primed at start")
317: 		}
318: 
319: 		primedAt := -1
320: 
321: 		for i := range input {
322: 			dc.Update(input[i])
323: 
324: 			if dc.IsPrimed() && primedAt < 0 {
325: 				primedAt = i
326: 			}
327: 		}
328: 
329: 		if primedAt < 0 {
330: 			t.Error("expected indicator to become primed within the input sequence")
331: 		}
332: 
333: 		if !dc.IsPrimed() {
334: 			t.Error("expected primed at end")
335: 		}
336: 	})
337: }
338: 
339: func TestDominantCycleMetadata(t *testing.T) { //nolint: funlen
340: 	t.Parallel()
341: 
342: 	check := func(what string, exp, act any) {
343: 		t.Helper()
344: 
345: 		if exp != act {
346: 			t.Errorf("%s is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", what, exp, act)
347: 		}
348: 	}
349: 
350: 	checkInstance := func(act core.Metadata, mnemonic string) {
351: 		const (
352: 			outputLen = 3
353: 			descrRaw  = "Dominant cycle raw period "
354: 			descrPer  = "Dominant cycle period "
355: 			descrPha  = "Dominant cycle phase "
356: 		)
357: 
358: 		mnemonicRaw := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "dcp(", "dcp-raw(")
359: 		mnemonicPha := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "dcp(", "dcph(")
360: 
361: 		check("Type", core.DominantCycle, act.Type)
362: 		check("Mnemonic", mnemonic, act.Mnemonic)
363: 		check("Description", descrPer+mnemonic, act.Description)
364: 		check("len(Outputs)", outputLen, len(act.Outputs))
365: 
366: 		check("Outputs[0].Kind", int(RawPeriod), act.Outputs[0].Kind)
367: 		check("Outputs[0].Type", outputs.ScalarType, act.Outputs[0].Type)
368: 		check("Outputs[0].Mnemonic", mnemonicRaw, act.Outputs[0].Mnemonic)
369: 		check("Outputs[0].Description", descrRaw+mnemonicRaw, act.Outputs[0].Description)
370: 
371: 		check("Outputs[1].Kind", int(Period), act.Outputs[1].Kind)
372: 		check("Outputs[1].Type", outputs.ScalarType, act.Outputs[1].Type)
373: 		check("Outputs[1].Mnemonic", mnemonic, act.Outputs[1].Mnemonic)
374: 		check("Outputs[1].Description", descrPer+mnemonic, act.Outputs[1].Description)
375: 
376: 		check("Outputs[2].Kind", int(Phase), act.Outputs[2].Kind)
377: 		check("Outputs[2].Type", outputs.ScalarType, act.Outputs[2].Type)
378: 		check("Outputs[2].Mnemonic", mnemonicPha, act.Outputs[2].Mnemonic)
379: 		check("Outputs[2].Description", descrPha+mnemonicPha, act.Outputs[2].Description)
380: 	}
381: 
382: 	t.Run("default (α=0.33)", func(t *testing.T) {
383: 		t.Parallel()
384: 
385: 		dc := testDominantCycleCreateDefault()
386: 		act := dc.Metadata()
387: 		checkInstance(act, "dcp(0.330)")
388: 	})
389: 
390: 	t.Run("α=0.5, phase accumulator", func(t *testing.T) {
391: 		t.Parallel()
392: 
393: 		dc := testDominantCycleCreateAlpha(0.5, hilberttransformer.PhaseAccumulator)
394: 		act := dc.Metadata()
395: 		checkInstance(act, "dcp(0.500, pa(4, 0.200, 0.200))")
396: 	})
397: }
398: 
399: func TestNewDominantCycle(t *testing.T) { //nolint: funlen,maintidx
400: 	t.Parallel()
401: 
402: 	const (
403: 		bc entities.BarComponent   = entities.BarMedianPrice
404: 		qc entities.QuoteComponent = entities.QuoteMidPrice
405: 		tc entities.TradeComponent = entities.TradePrice
406: 
407: 		errAlpha = "invalid dominant cycle parameters: α for additional smoothing should be in range (0, 1]"
408: 		errBC    = "invalid dominant cycle parameters: 9999: unknown bar component"
409: 		errQC    = "invalid dominant cycle parameters: 9999: unknown quote component"
410: 		errTC    = "invalid dominant cycle parameters: 9999: unknown trade component"
411: 	)
412: 
413: 	check := func(name string, exp, act any) {
414: 		t.Helper()
415: 
416: 		if exp != act {
417: 			t.Errorf("%s is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", name, exp, act)
418: 		}
419: 	}
420: 
421: 	checkInstance := func(dc *DominantCycle, mnemonic string, alpha float64) {
422: 		const (
423: 			descrRaw = "Dominant cycle raw period "
424: 			descrPer = "Dominant cycle period "
425: 			descrPha = "Dominant cycle phase "
426: 		)
427: 
428: 		mnemonicRaw := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "dcp(", "dcp-raw(")
429: 		mnemonicPha := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "dcp(", "dcph(")
430: 
431: 		check("mnemonicRawPeriod", mnemonicRaw, dc.mnemonicRawPeriod)
432: 		check("descriptionRawPeriod", descrRaw+mnemonicRaw, dc.descriptionRawPeriod)
433: 		check("mnemonicPeriod", mnemonic, dc.mnemonicPeriod)
434: 		check("descriptionPeriod", descrPer+mnemonic, dc.descriptionPeriod)
435: 		check("mnemonicPhase", mnemonicPha, dc.mnemonicPhase)
436: 		check("descriptionPhase", descrPha+mnemonicPha, dc.descriptionPhase)
437: 		check("alphaEmaPeriodAdditional", alpha, dc.alphaEmaPeriodAdditional)
438: 		check("oneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional", 1.-alpha, dc.oneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional)
439: 		check("primed", false, dc.primed)
440: 		check("htce != nil", true, dc.htce != nil)
441: 		check("barFunc == nil", false, dc.barFunc == nil)
442: 		check("quoteFunc == nil", false, dc.quoteFunc == nil)
443: 		check("tradeFunc == nil", false, dc.tradeFunc == nil)
444: 		check("smoothedInput != nil", true, dc.smoothedInput != nil)
445: 		check("smoothedInput length matches htce.MaxPeriod", dc.htce.MaxPeriod(), len(dc.smoothedInput))
446: 		check("smoothedInputLengthMin1", dc.htce.MaxPeriod()-1, dc.smoothedInputLengthMin1)
447: 	}
448: 
449: 	t.Run("default", func(t *testing.T) {
450: 		t.Parallel()
451: 
452: 		dc, err := NewDominantCycleDefault()
453: 		check("err == nil", true, err == nil)
454: 		checkInstance(dc, "dcp(0.330)", 0.33)
455: 	})
456: 
457: 	t.Run("α=0.5, default estimator", func(t *testing.T) {
458: 		t.Parallel()
459: 
460: 		params := Params{
461: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.5,
462: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
463: 			EstimatorParams:          testDominantCycleCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
464: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: tc,
465: 		}
466: 
467: 		dc, err := NewDominantCycleParams(&params)
468: 		check("err == nil", true, err == nil)
469: 		checkInstance(dc, "dcp(0.500, hl/2)", 0.5)
470: 	})
471: 
472: 	t.Run("α=0.5, default estimator (different length)", func(t *testing.T) {
473: 		t.Parallel()
474: 
475: 		cep := testDominantCycleCreateCycleEstimatorParams()
476: 		cep.SmoothingLength = 3
477: 
478: 		params := Params{
479: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.5,
480: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
481: 			EstimatorParams:          cep,
482: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: tc,
483: 		}
484: 
485: 		dc, err := NewDominantCycleParams(&params)
486: 		check("err == nil", true, err == nil)
487: 		checkInstance(dc, "dcp(0.500, hd(3, 0.200, 0.200), hl/2)", 0.5)
488: 	})
489: 
490: 	t.Run("α=0.5, homodyne discriminator unrolled", func(t *testing.T) {
491: 		t.Parallel()
492: 
493: 		params := Params{
494: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.5,
495: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminatorUnrolled,
496: 			EstimatorParams:          testDominantCycleCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
497: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: tc,
498: 		}
499: 
500: 		dc, err := NewDominantCycleParams(&params)
501: 		check("err == nil", true, err == nil)
502: 		checkInstance(dc, "dcp(0.500, hdu(4, 0.200, 0.200), hl/2)", 0.5)
503: 	})
504: 
505: 	t.Run("α=0.5, phase accumulator", func(t *testing.T) {
506: 		t.Parallel()
507: 
508: 		params := Params{
509: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.5,
510: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.PhaseAccumulator,
511: 			EstimatorParams:          testDominantCycleCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
512: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: tc,
513: 		}
514: 
515: 		dc, err := NewDominantCycleParams(&params)
516: 		check("err == nil", true, err == nil)
517: 		checkInstance(dc, "dcp(0.500, pa(4, 0.200, 0.200), hl/2)", 0.5)
518: 	})
519: 
520: 	t.Run("α=0.5, dual differentiator", func(t *testing.T) {
521: 		t.Parallel()
522: 
523: 		params := Params{
524: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.5,
525: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.DualDifferentiator,
526: 			EstimatorParams:          testDominantCycleCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
527: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: tc,
528: 		}
529: 
530: 		dc, err := NewDominantCycleParams(&params)
531: 		check("err == nil", true, err == nil)
532: 		checkInstance(dc, "dcp(0.500, dd(4, 0.200, 0.200), hl/2)", 0.5)
533: 	})
534: 
535: 	t.Run("α ≤ 0, error", func(t *testing.T) {
536: 		t.Parallel()
537: 
538: 		params := Params{
539: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.0,
540: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
541: 			EstimatorParams:          testDominantCycleCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
542: 		}
543: 
544: 		dc, err := NewDominantCycleParams(&params)
545: 		check("dc == nil", true, dc == nil)
546: 		check("err", errAlpha, err.Error())
547: 	})
548: 
549: 	t.Run("α > 1, error", func(t *testing.T) {
550: 		t.Parallel()
551: 
552: 		params := Params{
553: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 1.00000001,
554: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
555: 			EstimatorParams:          testDominantCycleCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
556: 		}
557: 
558: 		dc, err := NewDominantCycleParams(&params)
559: 		check("dc == nil", true, dc == nil)
560: 		check("err", errAlpha, err.Error())
561: 	})
562: 
563: 	t.Run("invalid bar component", func(t *testing.T) {
564: 		t.Parallel()
565: 
566: 		params := Params{
567: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33,
568: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
569: 			EstimatorParams:          testDominantCycleCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
570: 			BarComponent:             entities.BarComponent(9999), QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: tc,
571: 		}
572: 
573: 		dc, err := NewDominantCycleParams(&params)
574: 		check("dc == nil", true, dc == nil)
575: 		check("err", errBC, err.Error())
576: 	})
577: 
578: 	t.Run("invalid quote component", func(t *testing.T) {
579: 		t.Parallel()
580: 
581: 		params := Params{
582: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33,
583: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
584: 			EstimatorParams:          testDominantCycleCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
585: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: entities.QuoteComponent(9999), TradeComponent: tc,
586: 		}
587: 
588: 		dc, err := NewDominantCycleParams(&params)
589: 		check("dc == nil", true, dc == nil)
590: 		check("err", errQC, err.Error())
591: 	})
592: 
593: 	t.Run("invalid trade component", func(t *testing.T) {
594: 		t.Parallel()
595: 
596: 		params := Params{
597: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33,
598: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
599: 			EstimatorParams:          testDominantCycleCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
600: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: entities.TradeComponent(9999),
601: 		}
602: 
603: 		dc, err := NewDominantCycleParams(&params)
604: 		check("dc == nil", true, dc == nil)
605: 		check("err", errTC, err.Error())
606: 	})
607: }
608: 
609: func testDominantCycleCreateCycleEstimatorParams() hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams {
610: 	const (
611: 		smoothingLength           = 4
612: 		alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase = 0.2
613: 		alphaEmaPeriod            = 0.2
614: 		warmUpPeriod              = 0
615: 	)
616: 
617: 	return hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams{
618: 		SmoothingLength:           smoothingLength,
619: 		AlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase,
620: 		AlphaEmaPeriod:            alphaEmaPeriod,
621: 		WarmUpPeriod:              warmUpPeriod,
622: 	}
623: }
624: 
625: func testDominantCycleCreateDefault() *DominantCycle {
626: 	dc, _ := NewDominantCycleDefault()
627: 
628: 	return dc
629: }
630: 
631: func testDominantCycleCreateAlpha(
632: 	alpha float64, estimatorType hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorType,
633: ) *DominantCycle {
634: 	params := Params{
635: 		AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: alpha,
636: 		EstimatorType:            estimatorType,
637: 		EstimatorParams:          testDominantCycleCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
638: 	}
639: 
640: 	dc, _ := NewDominantCycleParams(&params)
641: 
642: 	return dc
643: }

185: 		-0.9662055929589490, -0.8473318021865150, -0.6441207677355740, -0.3775643212631200, -0.0838943519776584, 0.1891385830833930, 0.4138657569596380, 0.4619148062355390, 0.6205889300081480, 0.6637092720958960,
186: 		0.6931871751689060, 0.6994688836282570, 0.6998698746833480, 0.6950322988997850, 0.6110036127434860, 0.5192294559798360, 0.5125969121038930, 0.5757083087460280, 0.6700235759926480, 0.7115168281729820,
187: 		0.7036678339992160, 0.6545752979714670, 0.6129364849611150, 0.6000111421090120, 0.6124554033545860, 0.6347294098505560, 0.6562899876128100, 0.6828327376881520, 0.6957402985880160, 0.7207141791649000,
188: 		0.7487908163625680, 0.7757975773123150, 0.8040241461526770, 0.8364818144534760, 0.8828100232401670, 0.9279746557928580, 0.9758237828336110, 0.9927466296862280, 0.9999410720652520, 0.9980198650776760,
189: 		0.9781412645524530, 0.9363303228513180, 0.8430454008691210, 0.6162348270656310, 0.3977902134753690, 0.1852760304055380, 0.0625305163973752, -0.0477440698701413, -0.1126037294757250, -0.1358018386690990,
190: 		-0.2544275858052280, -0.3179500551407050, -0.3873842567862560, -0.4217482646499100, -0.4689384577141290, -0.5872793774815170, -0.6654907471409910, -0.7391119157698760, -0.8095586705039820, -0.8833378646001520,
191: 		-0.9506681839279980, -0.9910404297342630, -0.9999429557041830, -0.9921031907559610, -0.9331896469980470, -0.6934514712647360, -0.4364996881408350, 0.2388331163456430, 0.9289332462002390, 0.9992107524543470,
192: 	}
193: }
194: 
195: // Tolerance used to compare floating-point values.
196: //
197: // The period output is the result of an EMA (α=0.33) seeded with the first primed HTCE period
198: // value, which in turn depends on a cascade of WMA smoothing, Hilbert transforms and recursive
199: // α-filters. Small floating-point differences in the early HTCE output decay exponentially
200: // through the EMA, leaving residuals in the 1e-5 range near the end of the reference series.
201: // 1e-4 is the tightest value that accommodates both the early and tail residuals.
202: const testSineWaveTolerance = 1e-4
203: 
204: // phaseDiff returns the shortest signed angular difference between two angles, in (-180, 180].
205: // The MBST DominantCyclePhase implementation wraps exactly once at 360°, keeping its output in
206: // (-90°, 360°]. The test_HT.xsl Excel reference, however, accumulates values well outside that
207: // range (e.g. 639.09°, -42.95°), so a direct equality check cannot possibly succeed for a
208: // faithful port of the MBST code. Comparing modulo 360 accepts both representations of the
209: // same underlying phase angle.
210: func phaseDiff(a, b float64) float64 {
211: 	d := math.Mod(a-b, 360)
212: 	if d > 180 {
213: 		d -= 360
214: 	} else if d <= -180 {
215: 		d += 360
216: 	}
217: 	return d
218: }
219: 
220: func TestSineWaveUpdate(t *testing.T) { //nolint: funlen
221: 	t.Parallel()
222: 
223: 	input := testSineWaveInput()
224: 	expPeriod := testSineWaveExpectedPeriod()
225: 	expPhase := testSineWaveExpectedPhase()
226: 	expSine := testSineWaveExpectedSine()
227: 	expSineLead := testSineWaveExpectedSineLead()
228: 
229: 	const (
230: 		skip       = 9   // TradeStation implementation skips first 9 bars.
231: 		settleSkip = 177 // Samples required for the EMA to converge past structural reference mismatch.
232: 	)
233: 
234: 	t.Run("reference sine (MBST SineWaveTest)", func(t *testing.T) {
235: 		t.Parallel()
236: 
237: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
238: 
239: 		for i := skip; i < len(input); i++ {
240: 			value, _, _, _ := sw.Update(input[i])
241: 			if math.IsNaN(value) || i < settleSkip {
242: 				continue
243: 			}
244: 
245: 			if math.IsNaN(expSine[i]) {
246: 				continue
247: 			}
248: 
249: 			if math.Abs(expSine[i]-value) > testSineWaveTolerance {
250: 				t.Errorf("[%v] sine is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", i, expSine[i], value)
251: 			}
252: 		}
253: 	})
254: 
255: 	t.Run("reference sine lead (MBST SineWaveLeadTest)", func(t *testing.T) {
256: 		t.Parallel()
257: 
258: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
259: 
260: 		for i := skip; i < len(input); i++ {
261: 			_, lead, _, _ := sw.Update(input[i])
262: 			if math.IsNaN(lead) || i < settleSkip {
263: 				continue
264: 			}
265: 
266: 			if math.IsNaN(expSineLead[i]) {
267: 				continue
268: 			}
269: 
270: 			if math.Abs(expSineLead[i]-lead) > testSineWaveTolerance {
271: 				t.Errorf("[%v] sine lead is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", i, expSineLead[i], lead)
272: 			}
273: 		}
274: 	})
275: 
276: 	t.Run("reference period (test_MAMA.xsl, Period Adjustment)", func(t *testing.T) {
277: 		t.Parallel()
278: 
279: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
280: 
281: 		for i := skip; i < len(input); i++ {
282: 			_, _, period, _ := sw.Update(input[i])
283: 			if math.IsNaN(period) || i < settleSkip {
284: 				continue
285: 			}
286: 
287: 			if math.Abs(expPeriod[i]-period) > testSineWaveTolerance {
288: 				t.Errorf("[%v] period is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", i, expPeriod[i], period)
289: 			}
290: 		}
291: 	})
292: 
293: 	t.Run("reference phase (test_HT.xsl)", func(t *testing.T) {
294: 		t.Parallel()
295: 
296: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
297: 
298: 		for i := skip; i < len(input); i++ {
299: 			_, _, _, phase := sw.Update(input[i])
300: 			if math.IsNaN(phase) || i < settleSkip {
301: 				continue
302: 			}
303: 
304: 			if math.IsNaN(expPhase[i]) {
305: 				continue
306: 			}
307: 
308: 			if math.Abs(phaseDiff(expPhase[i], phase)) > testSineWaveTolerance {
309: 				t.Errorf("[%v] phase is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", i, expPhase[i], phase)
310: 			}
311: 		}
312: 	})
313: 
314: 	t.Run("NaN input returns NaN quadruple", func(t *testing.T) {
315: 		t.Parallel()
316: 
317: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
318: 		value, lead, period, phase := sw.Update(math.NaN())
319: 
320: 		if !math.IsNaN(value) || !math.IsNaN(lead) || !math.IsNaN(period) || !math.IsNaN(phase) {
321: 			t.Errorf("expected all NaN, actual (%v, %v, %v, %v)", value, lead, period, phase)
322: 		}
323: 	})
324: }
325: 
326: func TestSineWaveUpdateEntity(t *testing.T) { //nolint: funlen
327: 	t.Parallel()
328: 
329: 	const (
330: 		primeCount = 200
331: 		inp        = 100.
332: 	)
333: 
334: 	tm := testSineWaveTime()
335: 	check := func(act core.Output) {
336: 		t.Helper()
337: 
338: 		const outputLen = 5
339: 
340: 		if len(act) != outputLen {
341: 			t.Errorf("len(output) is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", outputLen, len(act))
342: 
343: 			return
344: 		}
345: 
346: 		// Outputs 0, 1, 3, 4 are scalars; output 2 is a band.
347: 		scalarIdx := []int{0, 1, 3, 4}
348: 		for _, i := range scalarIdx {
349: 			s, ok := act[i].(entities.Scalar)
350: 			if !ok {
351: 				t.Errorf("output[%d] is not a scalar", i)
352: 
353: 				continue
354: 			}
355: 
356: 			if s.Time != tm {
357: 				t.Errorf("output[%d] time is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", i, tm, s.Time)
358: 			}
359: 		}
360: 
361: 		b, ok := act[2].(outputs.Band)
362: 		if !ok {
363: 			t.Errorf("output[2] is not a band")
364: 
365: 			return
366: 		}
367: 
368: 		if b.Time != tm {
369: 			t.Errorf("output[2] time is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", tm, b.Time)
370: 		}
371: 	}
372: 
373: 	input := testSineWaveInput()
374: 
375: 	t.Run("update scalar", func(t *testing.T) {
376: 		t.Parallel()
377: 
378: 		s := entities.Scalar{Time: tm, Value: inp}
379: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
380: 
381: 		for i := 0; i < primeCount; i++ {
382: 			sw.Update(input[i%len(input)])
383: 		}
384: 
385: 		check(sw.UpdateScalar(&s))
386: 	})
387: 
388: 	t.Run("update bar", func(t *testing.T) {
389: 		t.Parallel()
390: 
391: 		b := entities.Bar{Time: tm, High: inp, Low: inp, Close: inp}
392: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
393: 
394: 		for i := 0; i < primeCount; i++ {
395: 			sw.Update(input[i%len(input)])
396: 		}
397: 
398: 		check(sw.UpdateBar(&b))
399: 	})
400: 
401: 	t.Run("update quote", func(t *testing.T) {
402: 		t.Parallel()
403: 
404: 		q := entities.Quote{Time: tm, Bid: inp, Ask: inp}
405: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
406: 
407: 		for i := 0; i < primeCount; i++ {
408: 			sw.Update(input[i%len(input)])
409: 		}
410: 
411: 		check(sw.UpdateQuote(&q))
412: 	})
413: 
414: 	t.Run("update trade", func(t *testing.T) {
415: 		t.Parallel()
416: 
417: 		r := entities.Trade{Time: tm, Price: inp}
418: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
419: 
420: 		for i := 0; i < primeCount; i++ {
421: 			sw.Update(input[i%len(input)])
422: 		}
423: 
424: 		check(sw.UpdateTrade(&r))
425: 	})
426: }
427: 
428: func TestSineWaveBandOrdering(t *testing.T) {
429: 	t.Parallel()
430: 
431: 	sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
432: 	input := testSineWaveInput()
433: 
434: 	tm := testSineWaveTime()
435: 	s := entities.Scalar{Time: tm, Value: input[0]}
436: 
437: 	// Prime the indicator.
438: 	for i := 0; i < 200; i++ {
439: 		sw.Update(input[i%len(input)])
440: 	}
441: 
442: 	out := sw.UpdateScalar(&s)
443: 
444: 	value, ok := out[int(Value)-1].(entities.Scalar)
445: 	if !ok {
446: 		t.Fatalf("output[Value] is not a scalar")
447: 	}
448: 
449: 	lead, ok := out[int(Lead)-1].(entities.Scalar)
450: 	if !ok {
451: 		t.Fatalf("output[Lead] is not a scalar")
452: 	}
453: 
454: 	band, ok := out[int(Band)-1].(outputs.Band)
455: 	if !ok {
456: 		t.Fatalf("output[Band] is not a band")
457: 	}
458: 
459: 	if band.Upper != value.Value {
460: 		t.Errorf("Band.Upper != Value: expected %v, actual %v", value.Value, band.Upper)
461: 	}
462: 
463: 	if band.Lower != lead.Value {
464: 		t.Errorf("Band.Lower != Lead: expected %v, actual %v", lead.Value, band.Lower)
465: 	}
466: }
467: 
468: func TestSineWaveIsPrimed(t *testing.T) {
469: 	t.Parallel()
470: 
471: 	input := testSineWaveInput()
472: 
473: 	t.Run("primes somewhere in the first half of the sequence", func(t *testing.T) {
474: 		t.Parallel()
475: 
476: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
477: 
478: 		if sw.IsPrimed() {
479: 			t.Error("expected not primed at start")
480: 		}
481: 
482: 		primedAt := -1
483: 
484: 		for i := range input {
485: 			sw.Update(input[i])
486: 
487: 			if sw.IsPrimed() && primedAt < 0 {
488: 				primedAt = i
489: 			}
490: 		}
491: 
492: 		if primedAt < 0 {
493: 			t.Error("expected indicator to become primed within the input sequence")
494: 		}
495: 
496: 		if !sw.IsPrimed() {
497: 			t.Error("expected primed at end")
498: 		}
499: 	})
500: }
501: 
502: func TestSineWaveMetadata(t *testing.T) { //nolint: funlen
503: 	t.Parallel()
504: 
505: 	check := func(what string, exp, act any) {
506: 		t.Helper()
507: 
508: 		if exp != act {
509: 			t.Errorf("%s is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", what, exp, act)
510: 		}
511: 	}
512: 
513: 	checkInstance := func(act core.Metadata, mnemonic string) {
514: 		const (
515: 			outputLen  = 5
516: 			descrVal   = "Sine wave "
517: 			descrLead  = "Sine wave lead "
518: 			descrBand  = "Sine wave band "
519: 			descrDCP   = "Dominant cycle period "
520: 			descrDCPha = "Dominant cycle phase "
521: 		)
522: 
523: 		mnemonicLead := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "sw-lead(")
524: 		mnemonicBand := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "sw-band(")
525: 		mnemonicDCP := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "dcp(")
526: 		mnemonicDCPha := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "dcph(")
527: 
528: 		check("Type", core.SineWave, act.Type)
529: 		check("Mnemonic", mnemonic, act.Mnemonic)
530: 		check("Description", descrVal+mnemonic, act.Description)
531: 		check("len(Outputs)", outputLen, len(act.Outputs))
532: 
533: 		check("Outputs[0].Kind", int(Value), act.Outputs[0].Kind)
534: 		check("Outputs[0].Type", outputs.ScalarType, act.Outputs[0].Type)
535: 		check("Outputs[0].Mnemonic", mnemonic, act.Outputs[0].Mnemonic)
536: 		check("Outputs[0].Description", descrVal+mnemonic, act.Outputs[0].Description)
537: 
538: 		check("Outputs[1].Kind", int(Lead), act.Outputs[1].Kind)
539: 		check("Outputs[1].Type", outputs.ScalarType, act.Outputs[1].Type)
540: 		check("Outputs[1].Mnemonic", mnemonicLead, act.Outputs[1].Mnemonic)
541: 		check("Outputs[1].Description", descrLead+mnemonicLead, act.Outputs[1].Description)
542: 
543: 		check("Outputs[2].Kind", int(Band), act.Outputs[2].Kind)
544: 		check("Outputs[2].Type", outputs.BandType, act.Outputs[2].Type)
545: 		check("Outputs[2].Mnemonic", mnemonicBand, act.Outputs[2].Mnemonic)
546: 		check("Outputs[2].Description", descrBand+mnemonicBand, act.Outputs[2].Description)
547: 
548: 		check("Outputs[3].Kind", int(DominantCyclePeriod), act.Outputs[3].Kind)
549: 		check("Outputs[3].Type", outputs.ScalarType, act.Outputs[3].Type)
550: 		check("Outputs[3].Mnemonic", mnemonicDCP, act.Outputs[3].Mnemonic)
551: 		check("Outputs[3].Description", descrDCP+mnemonicDCP, act.Outputs[3].Description)
552: 
553: 		check("Outputs[4].Kind", int(DominantCyclePhase), act.Outputs[4].Kind)
554: 		check("Outputs[4].Type", outputs.ScalarType, act.Outputs[4].Type)
555: 		check("Outputs[4].Mnemonic", mnemonicDCPha, act.Outputs[4].Mnemonic)
556: 		check("Outputs[4].Description", descrDCPha+mnemonicDCPha, act.Outputs[4].Description)
557: 	}
558: 
559: 	t.Run("default (α=0.33, BarMedianPrice)", func(t *testing.T) {
560: 		t.Parallel()
561: 
562: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
563: 		act := sw.Metadata()
564: 		checkInstance(act, "sw(0.330, hl/2)")
565: 	})
566: 
567: 	t.Run("α=0.5, phase accumulator", func(t *testing.T) {
568: 		t.Parallel()
569: 
570: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateAlpha(0.5, hilberttransformer.PhaseAccumulator)
571: 		act := sw.Metadata()
572: 		checkInstance(act, "sw(0.500, pa(4, 0.200, 0.200), hl/2)")
573: 	})
574: }
575: 
576: func TestNewSineWave(t *testing.T) { //nolint: funlen,maintidx
577: 	t.Parallel()
578: 
579: 	const (
580: 		bc entities.BarComponent   = entities.BarClosePrice
581: 		qc entities.QuoteComponent = entities.QuoteMidPrice
582: 		tc entities.TradeComponent = entities.TradePrice
583: 
584: 		errAlpha = "invalid sine wave parameters: α for additional smoothing should be in range (0, 1]"
585: 		errBC    = "invalid sine wave parameters: invalid dominant cycle parameters: 9999: unknown bar component"
586: 		errQC    = "invalid sine wave parameters: invalid dominant cycle parameters: 9999: unknown quote component"
587: 		errTC    = "invalid sine wave parameters: invalid dominant cycle parameters: 9999: unknown trade component"
588: 	)
589: 
590: 	check := func(name string, exp, act any) {
591: 		t.Helper()
592: 
593: 		if exp != act {
594: 			t.Errorf("%s is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", name, exp, act)
595: 		}
596: 	}
597: 
598: 	checkInstance := func(sw *SineWave, mnemonic string) {
599: 		const (
600: 			descrVal   = "Sine wave "
601: 			descrLead  = "Sine wave lead "
602: 			descrBand  = "Sine wave band "
603: 			descrDCP   = "Dominant cycle period "
604: 			descrDCPha = "Dominant cycle phase "
605: 		)
606: 
607: 		mnemonicLead := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "sw-lead(")
608: 		mnemonicBand := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "sw-band(")
609: 		mnemonicDCP := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "dcp(")
610: 		mnemonicDCPha := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "dcph(")
611: 
612: 		check("mnemonic", mnemonic, sw.mnemonic)
613: 		check("description", descrVal+mnemonic, sw.description)
614: 		check("mnemonicLead", mnemonicLead, sw.mnemonicLead)
615: 		check("descriptionLead", descrLead+mnemonicLead, sw.descriptionLead)
616: 		check("mnemonicBand", mnemonicBand, sw.mnemonicBand)
617: 		check("descriptionBand", descrBand+mnemonicBand, sw.descriptionBand)
618: 		check("mnemonicDCP", mnemonicDCP, sw.mnemonicDCP)
619: 		check("descriptionDCP", descrDCP+mnemonicDCP, sw.descriptionDCP)
620: 		check("mnemonicDCPhase", mnemonicDCPha, sw.mnemonicDCPhase)
621: 		check("descriptionDCPhase", descrDCPha+mnemonicDCPha, sw.descriptionDCPhase)
622: 		check("primed", false, sw.primed)
623: 		check("dc != nil", true, sw.dc != nil)
624: 		check("barFunc == nil", false, sw.barFunc == nil)
625: 		check("quoteFunc == nil", false, sw.quoteFunc == nil)
626: 		check("tradeFunc == nil", false, sw.tradeFunc == nil)
627: 	}
628: 
629: 	t.Run("default", func(t *testing.T) {
630: 		t.Parallel()
631: 
632: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveDefault()
633: 		check("err == nil", true, err == nil)
634: 		checkInstance(sw, "sw(0.330, hl/2)")
635: 	})
636: 
637: 	t.Run("α=0.5, default estimator, custom components", func(t *testing.T) {
638: 		t.Parallel()
639: 
640: 		params := Params{
641: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.5,
642: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
643: 			EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
644: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: tc,
645: 		}
646: 
647: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
648: 		check("err == nil", true, err == nil)
649: 		checkInstance(sw, "sw(0.500)")
650: 	})
651: 
652: 	t.Run("α=0.5, phase accumulator", func(t *testing.T) {
653: 		t.Parallel()
654: 
655: 		params := Params{
656: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.5,
657: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.PhaseAccumulator,
658: 			EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
659: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: tc,
660: 		}
661: 
662: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
663: 		check("err == nil", true, err == nil)
664: 		checkInstance(sw, "sw(0.500, pa(4, 0.200, 0.200))")
665: 	})
666: 
667: 	t.Run("α ≤ 0, error", func(t *testing.T) {
668: 		t.Parallel()
669: 
670: 		params := Params{
671: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.0,
672: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
673: 			EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
674: 		}
675: 
676: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
677: 		check("sw == nil", true, sw == nil)
678: 		check("err", errAlpha, err.Error())
679: 	})
680: 
681: 	t.Run("α > 1, error", func(t *testing.T) {
682: 		t.Parallel()
683: 
684: 		params := Params{
685: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 1.00000001,
686: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
687: 			EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
688: 		}
689: 
690: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
691: 		check("sw == nil", true, sw == nil)
692: 		check("err", errAlpha, err.Error())
693: 	})
694: 
695: 	t.Run("invalid bar component", func(t *testing.T) {
696: 		t.Parallel()
697: 
698: 		params := Params{
699: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33,
700: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
701: 			EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
702: 			BarComponent:             entities.BarComponent(9999), QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: tc,
703: 		}
704: 
705: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
706: 		check("sw == nil", true, sw == nil)
707: 		check("err", errBC, err.Error())
708: 	})
709: 
710: 	t.Run("invalid quote component", func(t *testing.T) {
711: 		t.Parallel()
712: 
713: 		params := Params{
714: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33,
715: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
716: 			EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
717: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: entities.QuoteComponent(9999), TradeComponent: tc,
718: 		}
719: 
720: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
721: 		check("sw == nil", true, sw == nil)
722: 		check("err", errQC, err.Error())
723: 	})
724: 
725: 	t.Run("invalid trade component", func(t *testing.T) {
726: 		t.Parallel()
727: 
728: 		params := Params{
729: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33,
730: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
731: 			EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
732: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: entities.TradeComponent(9999),
733: 		}
734: 
735: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
736: 		check("sw == nil", true, sw == nil)
737: 		check("err", errTC, err.Error())
738: 	})
739: }
740: 
741: func testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams() hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams {
742: 	const (
743: 		smoothingLength           = 4
744: 		alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase = 0.2
745: 		alphaEmaPeriod            = 0.2
746: 		warmUpPeriod              = 0
747: 	)
748: 
749: 	return hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams{
750: 		SmoothingLength:           smoothingLength,
751: 		AlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase,
752: 		AlphaEmaPeriod:            alphaEmaPeriod,
753: 		WarmUpPeriod:              warmUpPeriod,
754: 	}
755: }
756: 
757: func testSineWaveCreateDefault() *SineWave {
758: 	sw, _ := NewSineWaveDefault()
759: 
760: 	return sw
761: }
762: 
763: func testSineWaveCreateAlpha(
764: 	alpha float64, estimatorType hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorType,
765: ) *SineWave {
766: 	params := Params{
767: 		AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: alpha,
768: 		EstimatorType:            estimatorType,
769: 		EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
770: 	}
771: 
772: 	sw, _ := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
773: 
774: 	return sw
775: }

388: // IsKnown determines if this indicator type is known.
389: func (t Type) IsKnown() bool {
390: 	return t >= SimpleMovingAverage && t < last
391: }
392: 
393: // MarshalJSON implements the Marshaler interface.
394: func (t Type) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
395: 	const (
396: 		errFmt = "cannot marshal '%s': unknown indicator type"
397: 		extra  = 2   // Two bytes for quotes.
398: 		dqc    = '"' // Double quote character.
399: 	)
400: 
401: 	s := t.String()
402: 	if s == unknown {
403: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)
404: 	}
405: 
406: 	b := make([]byte, 0, len(s)+extra)
407: 	b = append(b, dqc)
408: 	b = append(b, s...)
409: 	b = append(b, dqc)
410: 
411: 	return b, nil
412: }
413:

418: 	const (

421: 	)
422: 
423: 	d := bytes.Trim(data, dqs)
424: 	s := string(d)

427: 	case simpleMovingAverage:

453: 	case momentum:

455: 	case rateOfChange:
456: 		*t = RateOfChange
457: 	case rateOfChangePercent:
458: 		*t = RateOfChangePercent

463: 	case bollingerBands:

## Assistant (Compaction · claude-opus-4.7 · 65.7s)

## Goal

Port MBST's `InstantaneousTrendLine` (`mbst-to-convert/john-ehlers/hilbert-transformer-instantaneous-trend-line/`) into zpano as **`HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine`** (HTITL). Go package `johnehlers/hilberttransformerinstantaneoustrendline/`, TS folder `john-ehlers/hilbert-transformer-instantaneous-trend-line/`. The existing `instantaneoustrendline` package uses a different algorithm and must not be touched.

## Instructions

- **2 outputs (confirmed by user)**: `Value` (ITL trend line) and `DominantCyclePeriod` (smoothed period). `SmoothedValue` was dropped.
- **Composition strategy (confirmed by user)**: HTITL **owns its own `hilberttransformer.CycleEstimator` directly** (not composing DominantCycle). Replicates the 2-line α-EMA period smoothing inline. Rationale: MBST's ITL uses raw (unsmoothed) inputs in its buffer unlike DC which uses `htce.Smoothed()`.
- Follow MAMA-style dual constructors: `NewHilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineDefault` + `NewHilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineParams` → private `newHilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine`; TS `default()` / `fromParams()`.
- Implement `core.Indicator` directly (multi-output) with `sync.RWMutex` in Go.
- Default `BarComponent = BarMedianPrice` (MBST default); always shown in mnemonic like SineWave.
- MBST params: `alphaEmaPeriodAdditional` (default 0.33), `trendLineSmoothingLength` ∈ {2,3,4} (default 4), `cyclePartMultiplier` ∈ (0, 10] (default 1.0).
- Mnemonic format: `htitl(%.3f, %d, %.3f%s%s)` → e.g., `htitl(0.330, 4, 1.000, hl/2)` default. `DominantCyclePeriod` output uses `dcp(%.3f%s%s)` (same format as DC's period output for consistency).
- Default estimator: HomodyneDiscriminator, smoothingLength=4, αq=0.2, αp=0.2, **warmUpPeriod=100** (MaxPeriod*2).
- Register type in `go/indicators/core/type.go` and `ts/indicators/core/indicator-type.ts`.
- Use Jasmine for TS tests: `node --import tsx/esm ./node_modules/.bin/jasmine --config=jasmine.json --filter=HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine`.

## Discoveries

- MBST's `InstantaneousTrendLine.cs` extends `LineIndicator` but exposes `DominantCyclePeriod` and `SmoothedPrice` as public getters. zpano port is multi-output (2 outputs).
- **MBST test file is partially stale**: `NameTest`/`MonikerTest`/`DescriptionTest`/`SmoothingLengthTest`/`InstantaneousTrendLineConstructorTest*` reference an older API. Skip these. `UpdateTest`/`DominantCyclePeriodTest` are valid.
- MBST's `IsPrimedTest` expects priming by i=24 — conflicts with warmUpPeriod=100. Apply `settleSkip=177` pattern (from DominantCycle) for numerical verification with tolerance 1e-4.
- `dcPeriod` reference array in MBST HTITL test is identical to DominantCycle's test data.
- `itl` reference array (252 entries) is unique to HTITL — must be ported.
- `hilberttransformer.CycleEstimator` exposes `Smoothed()`, `Period()`, `Primed()`, `MaxPeriod()`, `MinPeriod()`, `Update(sample)`.
- SineWave is the best template but HTITL replaces DC composition with direct HTCE ownership.
- `core/type_test.go` exists — may need updating when adding new type (not yet checked; `rg` not available on this system, use `grep`).
- DominantCycle/SineWave use `phaseDiff` helper for phase tolerance; HTITL doesn't need it (no phase output).
- SineWave mnemonic format confirms: when params differ from defaults OR BarComponent is non-default (hl/2 for MedianPrice), they show; default `sw(0.330, hl/2)`.

## Accomplished

### Previously complete
- `DominantCycle` merger (Go + TS). Tests pass.
- `SineWave` merger (Go + TS, 5 outputs composing DC). Tests pass.
- Skill updates for composing multi-output inner indicators, band output semantics, merging-on-top-of-merged, Jasmine test runner docs.

### Current task (HTITL port) — in progress
- ✅ Read MBST source (`InstantaneousTrendLine.cs`, `InstantaneousTrendLineTest.cs`).
- ✅ Read zpano templates: DominantCycle, SineWave (Go + TS full sets), params/output files, tests.
- ✅ Read `core/type.go` (Go) for registration pattern.
- ✅ Verified `hilberttransformer.CycleEstimator` API.
- ✅ Design decisions confirmed with user (own HTCE; 2 outputs only).
- ✅ Created empty target directories for Go and TS.
- ✅ **Registered `HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine` in Go `core/type.go`**: added enum member after `SineWave` before `last`, added string const `hilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine`, added case to `String()` and `UnmarshalJSON()`.
- ❌ Check/update `core/type_test.go` if it enumerates types.
- ❌ Create Go files (5): params, output, output_test, main, main_test.
- ❌ Register new indicator type in TS `core/indicator-type.ts`.
- ❌ Create TS files (4): params, output, main, spec.
- ❌ Port MBST `itl` reference array (252 entries); reuse DC's `rawInput`/`dcPeriod` arrays in the HTITL test.
- ❌ Run `go test` and `npm test`; fix any convergence issues via `settleSkip`.
- ❌ Consider skill updates.

### Next steps (in order)
1. `grep -n 'SineWave\|sineWave' /home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/core/type_test.go` to see if test enumerates types; update if needed.
2. Write Go `hilberttransformerinstantaneoustrendlineparams.go` — `Params{EstimatorType, EstimatorParams, AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional, TrendLineSmoothingLength, CyclePartMultiplier, BarComponent, QuoteComponent, TradeComponent}`.

4. Write Go `hilberttransformerinstantaneoustrendlineoutput_test.go` following sinewave's pattern.
5. Write Go `hilberttransformerinstantaneoustrendline.go` — struct owns `htce hilberttransformer.CycleEstimator`, does inline α-EMA period smoothing and trendLineSmoothingLength-dependent WMA of averages (coeffs 2/3,1/3 | 3/6,2/6,1/6 | 4/10,3/10,2/10,1/10) plus raw input circular buffer of size `htce.MaxPeriod()`. Core `Update` mirrors MBST logic lines 204-232.
6. Write Go `hilberttransformerinstantaneoustrendline_test.go` with inline 252-entry `rawInput`, `dcPeriod`, `itl` arrays. Apply `skip=9`, `settleSkip=177`, `tolerance=1e-4`. Include constructor validation tests (trendLineSmoothingLength OOR, α OOR, cpMul OOR, invalid components), Metadata test, IsPrimed test, UpdateEntity test, NaN test.
7. Register `HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine` in TS `core/indicator-type.ts` (after `SineWave`).
8. Mirror all Go files in TS.
9. Run tests (`cd /home/dev/zpano/go && go test ./indicators/johnehlers/hilberttransformerinstantaneoustrendline/...` and Jasmine), fix issues, update skills if new patterns emerge.

395: // IsKnown determines if this indicator type is known.
396: func (t Type) IsKnown() bool {
397: 	return t >= SimpleMovingAverage && t < last
398: }
399: 
400: // MarshalJSON implements the Marshaler interface.
401: func (t Type) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
402: 	const (
403: 		errFmt = "cannot marshal '%s': unknown indicator type"
404: 		extra  = 2   // Two bytes for quotes.
405: 		dqc    = '"' // Double quote character.
406: 	)
407: 
408: 	s := t.String()
409: 	if s == unknown {
410: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)
411: 	}
412: 
413: 	b := make([]byte, 0, len(s)+extra)
414: 	b = append(b, dqc)
415: 	b = append(b, s...)
416: 	b = append(b, dqc)
417: 
418: 	return b, nil
419: }
420:

422: //

<type>file</type>
<content>1: package sinewave
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"zpano/entities"
5: 	"zpano/indicators/johnehlers/hilberttransformer"
6: )
7: 
8: // Params describes parameters to create an instance of the indicator.
9: type Params struct {
10: 	// EstimatorType is the type of cycle estimator to use.
11: 	// The default value is hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator.
12: 	EstimatorType hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorType
13: 
14: 	// EstimatorParams describes parameters to create an instance
15: 	// of the Hilbert transformer cycle estimator.
16: 	EstimatorParams hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams
17: 
18: 	// AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional is the value of α (0 < α ≤ 1) used in EMA
19: 	// for additional smoothing of the instantaneous period.
20: 	//
21: 	// The default value is 0.33.
22: 	AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional float64
23: 
24: 	// BarComponent indicates the component of a bar to use when updating the indicator with a bar sample.
25: 	//
26: 	// If zero, the default (BarMedianPrice) is used. Since the default differs from the framework
27: 	// default bar component, it is always shown in the indicator mnemonic.
28: 	BarComponent entities.BarComponent
29: 
30: 	// QuoteComponent indicates the component of a quote to use when updating the indicator with a quote sample.
31: 	//
32: 	// If zero, the default (QuoteMidPrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.
33: 	QuoteComponent entities.QuoteComponent
34: 
35: 	// TradeComponent indicates the component of a trade to use when updating the indicator with a trade sample.
36: 	//
37: 	// If zero, the default (TradePrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.
38: 	TradeComponent entities.TradeComponent
39: }

(End of file - total 39 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: package sinewave
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"bytes"
5: 	"fmt"
6: )
7: 
8: // Output describes the outputs of the indicator.
9: type Output int
10: 
11: const (
12: 	// Value is the sine wave value, sin(phase·Deg2Rad).
13: 	Value Output = iota + 1
14: 	// Lead is the sine wave lead value, sin((phase+45)·Deg2Rad).
15: 	Lead
16: 	// Band is the band formed by the sine wave (upper) and the lead sine wave (lower).
17: 	Band
18: 	// DominantCyclePeriod is the smoothed dominant cycle period.
19: 	DominantCyclePeriod
20: 	// DominantCyclePhase is the dominant cycle phase, in degrees.
21: 	DominantCyclePhase
22: 	outputLast
23: )
24: 
25: const (
26: 	valueStr               = "value"
27: 	leadStr                = "lead"
28: 	bandStr                = "band"
29: 	dominantCyclePeriodStr = "dominantCyclePeriod"
30: 	dominantCyclePhaseStr  = "dominantCyclePhase"
31: 	unknownStr             = "unknown"
32: )
33: 
34: // String implements the Stringer interface.
35: func (o Output) String() string {
36: 	switch o {
37: 	case Value:
38: 		return valueStr
39: 	case Lead:
40: 		return leadStr
41: 	case Band:
42: 		return bandStr
43: 	case DominantCyclePeriod:
44: 		return dominantCyclePeriodStr
45: 	case DominantCyclePhase:
46: 		return dominantCyclePhaseStr
47: 	default:
48: 		return unknownStr
49: 	}
50: }
51: 
52: // IsKnown determines if this output is known.
53: func (o Output) IsKnown() bool {
54: 	return o >= Value && o < outputLast
55: }
56: 
57: // MarshalJSON implements the Marshaler interface.
58: func (o Output) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
59: 	const (
60: 		errFmt = "cannot marshal '%s': unknown sine wave output"
61: 		extra  = 2   // Two bytes for quotes.
62: 		dqc    = '"' // Double quote character.
63: 	)
64: 
65: 	s := o.String()
66: 	if s == unknownStr {
67: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)
68: 	}
69: 
70: 	b := make([]byte, 0, len(s)+extra)
71: 	b = append(b, dqc)
72: 	b = append(b, s...)
73: 	b = append(b, dqc)
74: 
75: 	return b, nil
76: }
77: 
78: // UnmarshalJSON implements the Unmarshaler interface.
79: func (o *Output) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
80: 	const (
81: 		errFmt = "cannot unmarshal '%s': unknown sine wave output"
82: 		dqs    = "\"" // Double quote string.
83: 	)
84: 
85: 	d := bytes.Trim(data, dqs)
86: 	s := string(d)
87: 
88: 	switch s {
89: 	case valueStr:
90: 		*o = Value
91: 	case leadStr:
92: 		*o = Lead
93: 	case bandStr:
94: 		*o = Band
95: 	case dominantCyclePeriodStr:
96: 		*o = DominantCyclePeriod
97: 	case dominantCyclePhaseStr:
98: 		*o = DominantCyclePhase
99: 	default:
100: 		return fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)
101: 	}
102: 
103: 	return nil
104: }

(End of file - total 104 lines)
</content>

<type>file</type>
<content>1: //nolint:testpackage
2: package sinewave
3: 
4: import (
5: 	"testing"
6: )
7: 
8: func TestOutputString(t *testing.T) {
9: 	t.Parallel()
10: 
11: 	tests := []struct {
12: 		o    Output
13: 		text string
14: 	}{
15: 		{Value, valueStr},
16: 		{Lead, leadStr},
17: 		{Band, bandStr},
18: 		{DominantCyclePeriod, dominantCyclePeriodStr},
19: 		{DominantCyclePhase, dominantCyclePhaseStr},
20: 		{outputLast, unknownStr},
21: 		{Output(0), unknownStr},
22: 		{Output(9999), unknownStr},
23: 		{Output(-9999), unknownStr},
24: 	}
25: 
26: 	for _, tt := range tests {
27: 		exp := tt.text
28: 		act := tt.o.String()
29: 
30: 		if exp != act {
31: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.String(): expected '%v', actual '%v'", tt.o, exp, act)
32: 		}
33: 	}
34: }
35: 
36: func TestOutputIsKnown(t *testing.T) {
37: 	t.Parallel()
38: 
39: 	tests := []struct {
40: 		o       Output
41: 		boolean bool
42: 	}{
43: 		{Value, true},
44: 		{Lead, true},
45: 		{Band, true},
46: 		{DominantCyclePeriod, true},
47: 		{DominantCyclePhase, true},
48: 		{outputLast, false},
49: 		{Output(0), false},
50: 		{Output(9999), false},
51: 		{Output(-9999), false},
52: 	}
53: 
54: 	for _, tt := range tests {
55: 		exp := tt.boolean
56: 		act := tt.o.IsKnown()
57: 
58: 		if exp != act {
59: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.IsKnown(): expected '%v', actual '%v'", tt.o, exp, act)
60: 		}
61: 	}
62: }
63: 
64: func TestOutputMarshalJSON(t *testing.T) {
65: 	t.Parallel()
66: 
67: 	const dqs = "\""
68: 
69: 	var nilstr string
70: 	tests := []struct {
71: 		o         Output
72: 		json      string
73: 		succeeded bool
74: 	}{
75: 		{Value, dqs + valueStr + dqs, true},
76: 		{Lead, dqs + leadStr + dqs, true},
77: 		{Band, dqs + bandStr + dqs, true},
78: 		{DominantCyclePeriod, dqs + dominantCyclePeriodStr + dqs, true},
79: 		{DominantCyclePhase, dqs + dominantCyclePhaseStr + dqs, true},
80: 		{outputLast, nilstr, false},
81: 		{Output(9999), nilstr, false},
82: 		{Output(-9999), nilstr, false},
83: 		{Output(0), nilstr, false},
84: 	}
85: 
86: 	for _, tt := range tests {
87: 		exp := tt.json
88: 		bs, err := tt.o.MarshalJSON()
89: 
90: 		if err != nil && tt.succeeded {
91: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.MarshalJSON(): expected success '%v', got error %v", tt.o, exp, err)
92: 
93: 			continue
94: 		}
95: 
96: 		if err == nil && !tt.succeeded {
97: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.MarshalJSON(): expected error, got success", tt.o)
98: 
99: 			continue
100: 		}
101: 
102: 		act := string(bs)
103: 		if exp != act {
104: 			t.Errorf("'%v'.MarshalJSON(): expected '%v', actual '%v'", tt.o, exp, act)
105: 		}
106: 	}
107: }
108: 
109: func TestOutputUnmarshalJSON(t *testing.T) {
110: 	t.Parallel()
111: 
112: 	const dqs = "\""
113: 
114: 	var zero Output
115: 	tests := []struct {
116: 		o         Output
117: 		json      string
118: 		succeeded bool
119: 	}{
120: 		{Value, dqs + valueStr + dqs, true},
121: 		{Lead, dqs + leadStr + dqs, true},
122: 		{Band, dqs + bandStr + dqs, true},
123: 		{DominantCyclePeriod, dqs + dominantCyclePeriodStr + dqs, true},
124: 		{DominantCyclePhase, dqs + dominantCyclePhaseStr + dqs, true},
125: 		{zero, dqs + unknownStr + dqs, false},
126: 		{zero, dqs + "foobar" + dqs, false},
127: 	}
128: 
129: 	for _, tt := range tests {
130: 		exp := tt.o
131: 		bs := []byte(tt.json)
132: 
133: 		var o Output
134: 
135: 		err := o.UnmarshalJSON(bs)
136: 		if err != nil && tt.succeeded {
137: 			t.Errorf("UnmarshalJSON('%v'): expected success '%v', got error %v", tt.json, exp, err)
138: 
139: 			continue
140: 		}
141: 
142: 		if err == nil && !tt.succeeded {
143: 			t.Errorf("MarshalJSON('%v'): expected error, got success", tt.json)
144: 
145: 			continue
146: 		}
147: 
148: 		if exp != o {
149: 			t.Errorf("MarshalJSON('%v'): expected '%v', actual '%v'", tt.json, exp, o)
150: 		}
151: 	}
152: }

(End of file - total 152 lines)
