51: 
52:         /// <summary>
53:         /// The default value of the multiplier to the dominant cycle period used to determine the window length to calculate the trend line.
54:         /// </summary>
55:         public const double DefaultCyclePartMultiplier = 1;
56:

69:         [DataMember]
70:         private readonly double coeff1;
71:         [DataMember]

73:         [DataMember]

75:         [DataMember]

77:         [DataMember]

83: 
84:         #region Variables to track the last htce.MaxCycle input values in a circular buffer.

95: 
96:         private const string itl = "ehlersItl";
97:         private const string itlFull = "John Ehlers Instantaneous Trend Line";
98:         #endregion
99: 
100:         #region Construction
101:         /// <summary>
102:         /// Constructs a new instance of the class.
103:         /// </summary>
104:         /// <param name="estimator">The type of an estimator. The default is homodyne discriminator.</param>
105:         /// <param name="alphaEmaPeriodAdditional">The value of α (0 &lt; α ≤ 1) used in EMA for additional smoothing of the instantaneous period. The default value is <c>DominantCyclePhase.DefaultAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional</c>.</param>
106:         /// <param name="trendLineSmoothingLength">The additional WMA smoothing length used to smooth the trend line. The valid values are 2, 3, 4. The default value is <c>DefaultTrendLineSmoothingLength</c>.</param>
107:         /// <param name="cyclePartMultiplier">The multiplier to the dominant cycle period used to determine the window length to calculate the trend line. The typical values are in [0.5, 1.5]. The default value is <c>DefaultCyclePartMultiplier</c>.</param>
108:         /// <param name="ohlcvComponent">The Ohlcv component. The default value is median price.</param>
109:         public InstantaneousTrendLine(HilbertTransformerCycleEstimator estimator = HilbertTransformerCycleEstimator.HomodyneDiscriminator,
110:             double alphaEmaPeriodAdditional = DominantCyclePhase.DefaultAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
111:             int trendLineSmoothingLength = DefaultTrendLineSmoothingLength, double cyclePartMultiplier = DefaultCyclePartMultiplier,
112:             OhlcvComponent ohlcvComponent = OhlcvComponent.MedianPrice)
113:             : this(estimator.Create(), estimator, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, trendLineSmoothingLength, cyclePartMultiplier, ohlcvComponent) {}
114: 
115:         /// <summary>
116:         /// Constructs a new instance of the class.
117:         /// </summary>
118:         /// <param name="estimator">The type of an estimator. The default is homodyne discriminator.</param>
119:         /// <param name="smoothingLength">The WMA smoothing length. The valid values are 2, 3, 4. The default value is <c>estimator.DefaultSmoothingLength</c>.</param>
120:         /// <param name="warmUpPeriod">The number of updates before the indicator is primed. If less than the real primed period, then will be reset to the real primed period. The default value is <c>estimator.DefaultWarmUpPeriod</c>.</param>
121:         /// <param name="alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase">The value of α (0 &lt; α ≤ 1) used in EMA to smooth the in-phase and quadrature components. The default value is <c>estimator.DefaultAlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase</c>.</param>
122:         /// <param name="alphaEmaPeriod">The value of α (0 &lt; α ≤ 1) used in EMA to smooth the instantaneous period. The default value is <c>estimator.DefaultAlphaEmaPeriod</c>.</param>
123:         /// <param name="alphaEmaPeriodAdditional">The value of α (0 &lt; α ≤ 1) used in EMA for additional smoothing of the instantaneous period. The default value is <c>DominantCyclePhase.DefaultAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional</c>.</param>
124:         /// <param name="trendLineSmoothingLength">The additional WMA smoothing length used to smooth the trend line. The valid values are 2, 3, 4. The default value is <c>DefaultTrendLineSmoothingLength</c>..</param>
125:         /// <param name="cyclePartMultiplier">The multiplier to the dominant cycle period used to determine the window length to calculate the trend line. The typical values are in [0.5, 1.5]. The default value is <c>DefaultCyclePartMultiplier</c>.</param>
126:         /// <param name="ohlcvComponent">The Ohlcv component. The default value is median price.</param>
127:         public InstantaneousTrendLine(HilbertTransformerCycleEstimator estimator, int smoothingLength, int warmUpPeriod,
128:             double alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase, double alphaEmaPeriod, double alphaEmaPeriodAdditional = DominantCyclePhase.DefaultAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
129:             int trendLineSmoothingLength = DefaultTrendLineSmoothingLength, double cyclePartMultiplier = DefaultCyclePartMultiplier,
130:             OhlcvComponent ohlcvComponent = OhlcvComponent.MedianPrice)
131:             : this(estimator.Create(smoothingLength, warmUpPeriod, alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase, alphaEmaPeriod),
132:                   estimator, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, trendLineSmoothingLength, cyclePartMultiplier, ohlcvComponent) {}
133: 
134:         private InstantaneousTrendLine(IHilbertTransformerCycleEstimator htce, HilbertTransformerCycleEstimator estimator,
135:             double alphaEmaPeriodAdditional = DominantCyclePhase.DefaultAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
136:             int trendLineSmoothingLength = DefaultTrendLineSmoothingLength, double cyclePartMultiplier = DefaultCyclePartMultiplier,
137:             OhlcvComponent ohlcvComponent = OhlcvComponent.MedianPrice)
138:             : base(itl + estimator.ShortName(), itlFull + estimator.FullNameInBrackets(), ohlcvComponent)
139:         {
140:             if (2 > trendLineSmoothingLength || 4 < trendLineSmoothingLength)
141:                 throw new ArgumentOutOfRangeException(nameof(trendLineSmoothingLength));
142:             if (0d > alphaEmaPeriodAdditional || 1d < alphaEmaPeriodAdditional)
143:                 throw new ArgumentOutOfRangeException(nameof(alphaEmaPeriodAdditional));
144:             if (0d >= cyclePartMultiplier || 10d < cyclePartMultiplier)
145:                 throw new ArgumentOutOfRangeException(nameof(cyclePartMultiplier));
146:             this.htce = htce;
147:             moniker = string.Format(CultureInfo.InvariantCulture, "{0}({1},αPeAdd={2:0.####},tlsl={3},cpMul={4:0.####})",
148:                 name, htce.Parameters(), alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, trendLineSmoothingLength, cyclePartMultiplier);
149:             this.cyclePartMultiplier = cyclePartMultiplier;
150:             this.alphaEmaPeriodAdditional = alphaEmaPeriodAdditional;
151:             oneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional = 1d - alphaEmaPeriodAdditional;
152:             inputLength = htce.MaxPeriod;
153:             inputLengthMin1 = inputLength - 1;
154:             input = new double[inputLength];
155:             input.Initialize();
156:             this.trendLineSmoothingLength = trendLineSmoothingLength;
157:             if (2 == trendLineSmoothingLength)
158:             {
159:                 coeff0 = 2d / 3d;
160:                 coeff1 = 1d / 3d;
161:             }
162:             else if (3 == trendLineSmoothingLength)
163:             {
164:                 coeff0 = 3d / 6d;
165:                 coeff1 = 2d / 6d;
166:                 coeff2 = 1d / 6d;
167:             }
168:             else //if (4 == trendLineSmoothingLength)
169:             {
170:                 coeff0 = 4d / 10d;
171:                 coeff1 = 3d / 10d;
172:                 coeff2 = 2d / 10d;
173:                 coeff3 = 1d / 10d;
174:             }
175:         }
176:         #endregion
177: 
178:         #region Reset
179:         /// <summary>
180:         /// Resets the indicator.
181:         /// </summary>
182:         public override void Reset()
183:         {
184:             lock (updateLock)
185:             {
186:                 primed = false;
187:                 htce.Reset();
188:                 value = 0d;
189:                 average1 = 0d;
190:                 average2 = 0d;
191:                 average3 = 0d;
192:                 smoothedPeriod = 0d;
193:                 input.Initialize();
194:             }
195:         }
196:         #endregion
197: 
198:         #region Update
199:         /// <summary>
200:         /// Updates the value of the indicator.
201:         /// </summary>
202:         /// <param name="sample">A new sample.</param>
203:         /// <returns>The new value of the indicator.</returns>
204:         public override double Update(double sample)
205:         {
206:             if (double.IsNaN(sample))
207:                 return sample;
208:             lock (updateLock)
209:             {
210:                 htce.Update(sample);
211:                 PushInput(sample);
212:                 if (primed)
213:                 {
214:                     smoothedPeriod = alphaEmaPeriodAdditional * htce.Period + oneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional * smoothedPeriod;
215:                     double average = CalculateAverage();
216:                     value = Wma(average);
217:                     PushAverage(average);
218:                     return value;
219:                 }
220:                 if (htce.IsPrimed)
221:                 {
222:                     primed = true;
223:                     smoothedPeriod = htce.Period;
224:                     value = CalculateAverage();
225:                     average3 = value;
226:                     average2 = value;
227:                     average1 = value;
228:                     return value;
229:                 }
230:                 return double.NaN;
231:             }
232:         }
233:         #endregion
234: 
235:         #region Implementation
236:         private void PushInput(double val)
237:         {
238:             Array.Copy(input, 0, input, 1, inputLengthMin1);
239:             input[0] = val;
240:         }
241:         private void PushAverage(double val)
242:         {
243:             average3 = average2;
244:             average2 = average1;
245:             average1 = val;
246:         }
247: 
248:         private double Wma(double val)
249:         {
250:             return coeff0 * val + coeff1 * average1 + coeff2 * average2 + coeff3 * average3;
251:         }
252: 
253:         private double CalculateAverage()
254:         {
255:             // Compute the trend line as a simple average over the measured dominant cycle period.
256:             int length = (int)Math.Floor(smoothedPeriod * cyclePartMultiplier + 0.5);
257:             if (length > inputLength)
258:                 length = inputLength;
259:             else if (length < 1)
260:                 length = 1;
261:             double temp = 0d;
262:             for (int i = 0; i < length; ++i)
263:                 temp += input[i];
264:             return temp / length;
265:         }
266:         #endregion
267:     }
268: }

(End of file - total 268 lines)

<type>file</type>
<content>1: package sinewave
2: 
3: //nolint: gofumpt
4: import (
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"math"
7: 	"sync"
8: 	"time"
9: 
10: 	"zpano/entities"
11: 	"zpano/indicators/core"
12: 	"zpano/indicators/core/outputs"
13: 	"zpano/indicators/johnehlers/dominantcycle"
14: 	"zpano/indicators/johnehlers/hilberttransformer"
15: )
16: 
17: // Deg2Rad converts degrees to radians.
18: const deg2Rad = math.Pi / 180.0
19: 
20: // SineWave is the Ehlers' Sine Wave indicator.
21: //
22: // It exposes five outputs:
23: //
24: //   - Value: the sine wave value, sin(phase·Deg2Rad).
25: //   - Lead: the sine wave lead value, sin((phase+45)·Deg2Rad).
26: //   - Band: a band with Upper=Value and Lower=Lead.
27: //   - DominantCyclePeriod: the smoothed dominant cycle period.
28: //   - DominantCyclePhase: the dominant cycle phase, in degrees.
29: //
30: // Reference:
31: // John Ehlers, Rocket Science for Traders, Wiley, 2001, 0471405671, pp 95-105.
32: type SineWave struct {
33: 	mu                 sync.RWMutex
34: 	mnemonic           string
35: 	description        string
36: 	mnemonicLead       string
37: 	descriptionLead    string
38: 	mnemonicBand       string
39: 	descriptionBand    string
40: 	mnemonicDCP        string
41: 	descriptionDCP     string
42: 	mnemonicDCPhase    string
43: 	descriptionDCPhase string
44: 	dc                 *dominantcycle.DominantCycle
45: 	primed             bool
46: 	value              float64
47: 	lead               float64
48: 	barFunc            entities.BarFunc
49: 	quoteFunc          entities.QuoteFunc
50: 	tradeFunc          entities.TradeFunc
51: }
52: 
53: // NewSineWaveDefault returns an instance of the indicator
54: // created using default values of the parameters.
55: func NewSineWaveDefault() (*SineWave, error) {
56: 	const (
57: 		smoothingLength           = 4
58: 		alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase = 0.2
59: 		alphaEmaPeriod            = 0.2
60: 		alphaEmaPeriodAdditional  = 0.33
61: 		warmUpPeriod              = 100
62: 	)
63: 
64: 	return newSineWave(
65: 		hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
66: 		&hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams{
67: 			SmoothingLength:           smoothingLength,
68: 			AlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase,
69: 			AlphaEmaPeriod:            alphaEmaPeriod,
70: 			WarmUpPeriod:              warmUpPeriod,
71: 		},
72: 		alphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
73: 		0, 0, 0)
74: }
75: 
76: // NewSineWaveParams returns an instance of the indicator created using supplied parameters.
77: func NewSineWaveParams(p *Params) (*SineWave, error) {
78: 	return newSineWave(
79: 		p.EstimatorType, &p.EstimatorParams,
80: 		p.AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
81: 		p.BarComponent, p.QuoteComponent, p.TradeComponent)
82: }
83: 
84: //nolint:funlen
85: func newSineWave(
86: 	estimatorType hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorType,
87: 	estimatorParams *hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams,
88: 	alphaEmaPeriodAdditional float64,
89: 	bc entities.BarComponent, qc entities.QuoteComponent, tc entities.TradeComponent,
90: ) (*SineWave, error) {
91: 	const (
92: 		invalid    = "invalid sine wave parameters"
93: 		fmta       = "%s: α for additional smoothing should be in range (0, 1]"
94: 		fmtw       = "%s: %w"
95: 		fmtnVal    = "sw(%.3f%s%s)"
96: 		fmtnLead   = "sw-lead(%.3f%s%s)"
97: 		fmtnBand   = "sw-band(%.3f%s%s)"
98: 		fmtnDCP    = "dcp(%.3f%s%s)"
99: 		fmtnDCPha  = "dcph(%.3f%s%s)"
100: 		four       = 4
101: 		alpha      = 0.2
102: 		descrVal   = "Sine wave "
103: 		descrLead  = "Sine wave lead "
104: 		descrBand  = "Sine wave band "
105: 		descrDCP   = "Dominant cycle period "
106: 		descrDCPha = "Dominant cycle phase "
107: 	)
108: 
109: 	if alphaEmaPeriodAdditional <= 0. || alphaEmaPeriodAdditional > 1. {
110: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmta, invalid)
111: 	}
112: 
113: 	// Resolve defaults for component functions.
114: 	// SineWave defaults to BarMedianPrice (not the framework default), so it always shows in the mnemonic.
115: 	if bc == 0 {
116: 		bc = entities.BarMedianPrice
117: 	}
118: 
119: 	if qc == 0 {
120: 		qc = entities.DefaultQuoteComponent
121: 	}
122: 
123: 	if tc == 0 {
124: 		tc = entities.DefaultTradeComponent
125: 	}
126: 
127: 	// Build the inner DominantCycle with explicit components.
128: 	dcParams := &dominantcycle.Params{
129: 		EstimatorType:            estimatorType,
130: 		EstimatorParams:          *estimatorParams,
131: 		AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: alphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
132: 		BarComponent:             bc,
133: 		QuoteComponent:           qc,
134: 		TradeComponent:           tc,
135: 	}
136: 
137: 	dc, err := dominantcycle.NewDominantCycleParams(dcParams)
138: 	if err != nil {
139: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
140: 	}
141: 
142: 	// Compose the estimator moniker (same logic as DominantCycle).
143: 	estimator, err := hilberttransformer.NewCycleEstimator(estimatorType, estimatorParams)
144: 	if err != nil {
145: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
146: 	}
147: 
148: 	estimatorMoniker := ""
149: 	if estimatorType != hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator ||
150: 		estimatorParams.SmoothingLength != four ||
151: 		estimatorParams.AlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase != alpha ||
152: 		estimatorParams.AlphaEmaPeriod != alpha {
153: 		estimatorMoniker = hilberttransformer.EstimatorMoniker(estimatorType, estimator)
154: 		if len(estimatorMoniker) > 0 {
155: 			estimatorMoniker = ", " + estimatorMoniker
156: 		}
157: 	}
158: 
159: 	componentMnemonic := core.ComponentTripleMnemonic(bc, qc, tc)
160: 
161: 	mnemonicVal := fmt.Sprintf(fmtnVal, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, estimatorMoniker, componentMnemonic)
162: 	mnemonicLead := fmt.Sprintf(fmtnLead, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, estimatorMoniker, componentMnemonic)
163: 	mnemonicBand := fmt.Sprintf(fmtnBand, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, estimatorMoniker, componentMnemonic)
164: 	mnemonicDCP := fmt.Sprintf(fmtnDCP, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, estimatorMoniker, componentMnemonic)
165: 	mnemonicDCPha := fmt.Sprintf(fmtnDCPha, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, estimatorMoniker, componentMnemonic)
166: 
167: 	barFunc, err := entities.BarComponentFunc(bc)
168: 	if err != nil {
169: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
170: 	}
171: 
172: 	quoteFunc, err := entities.QuoteComponentFunc(qc)
173: 	if err != nil {
174: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
175: 	}
176: 
177: 	tradeFunc, err := entities.TradeComponentFunc(tc)
178: 	if err != nil {
179: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
180: 	}
181: 
182: 	nan := math.NaN()
183: 
184: 	return &SineWave{
185: 		mnemonic:           mnemonicVal,
186: 		description:        descrVal + mnemonicVal,
187: 		mnemonicLead:       mnemonicLead,
188: 		descriptionLead:    descrLead + mnemonicLead,
189: 		mnemonicBand:       mnemonicBand,
190: 		descriptionBand:    descrBand + mnemonicBand,
191: 		mnemonicDCP:        mnemonicDCP,
192: 		descriptionDCP:     descrDCP + mnemonicDCP,
193: 		mnemonicDCPhase:    mnemonicDCPha,
194: 		descriptionDCPhase: descrDCPha + mnemonicDCPha,
195: 		dc:                 dc,
196: 		value:              nan,
197: 		lead:               nan,
198: 		barFunc:            barFunc,
199: 		quoteFunc:          quoteFunc,
200: 		tradeFunc:          tradeFunc,
201: 	}, nil
202: }
203: 
204: // IsPrimed indicates whether an indicator is primed.
205: func (s *SineWave) IsPrimed() bool {
206: 	s.mu.RLock()
207: 	defer s.mu.RUnlock()
208: 
209: 	return s.primed
210: }
211: 
212: // Metadata describes an output data of the indicator.
213: func (s *SineWave) Metadata() core.Metadata {
214: 	return core.Metadata{
215: 		Type:        core.SineWave,
216: 		Mnemonic:    s.mnemonic,
217: 		Description: s.description,
218: 		Outputs: []outputs.Metadata{
219: 			{
220: 				Kind:        int(Value),
221: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
222: 				Mnemonic:    s.mnemonic,
223: 				Description: s.description,
224: 			},
225: 			{
226: 				Kind:        int(Lead),
227: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
228: 				Mnemonic:    s.mnemonicLead,
229: 				Description: s.descriptionLead,
230: 			},
231: 			{
232: 				Kind:        int(Band),
233: 				Type:        outputs.BandType,
234: 				Mnemonic:    s.mnemonicBand,
235: 				Description: s.descriptionBand,
236: 			},
237: 			{
238: 				Kind:        int(DominantCyclePeriod),
239: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
240: 				Mnemonic:    s.mnemonicDCP,
241: 				Description: s.descriptionDCP,
242: 			},
243: 			{
244: 				Kind:        int(DominantCyclePhase),
245: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
246: 				Mnemonic:    s.mnemonicDCPhase,
247: 				Description: s.descriptionDCPhase,
248: 			},
249: 		},
250: 	}
251: }
252: 
253: // Update updates the value of the indicator given the next sample, returning
254: // the (value, lead, period, phase) tuple. Returns NaN values if the indicator is not yet primed.
255: func (s *SineWave) Update(sample float64) (float64, float64, float64, float64) {
256: 	if math.IsNaN(sample) {
257: 		return sample, sample, sample, sample
258: 	}
259: 
260: 	// Delegate to the inner DominantCycle. Its own mutex is independent.
261: 	_, period, phase := s.dc.Update(sample)
262: 
263: 	s.mu.Lock()
264: 	defer s.mu.Unlock()
265: 
266: 	if math.IsNaN(phase) {
267: 		nan := math.NaN()
268: 
269: 		return nan, nan, nan, nan
270: 	}
271: 
272: 	const leadOffset = 45.0
273: 
274: 	s.primed = true
275: 	s.value = math.Sin(phase * deg2Rad)
276: 	s.lead = math.Sin((phase + leadOffset) * deg2Rad)
277: 
278: 	return s.value, s.lead, period, phase
279: }
280: 
281: // UpdateScalar updates the indicator given the next scalar sample.
282: func (s *SineWave) UpdateScalar(sample *entities.Scalar) core.Output {
283: 	return s.updateEntity(sample.Time, sample.Value)
284: }
285: 
286: // UpdateBar updates the indicator given the next bar sample.
287: func (s *SineWave) UpdateBar(sample *entities.Bar) core.Output {
288: 	return s.updateEntity(sample.Time, s.barFunc(sample))
289: }
290: 
291: // UpdateQuote updates the indicator given the next quote sample.
292: func (s *SineWave) UpdateQuote(sample *entities.Quote) core.Output {
293: 	return s.updateEntity(sample.Time, s.quoteFunc(sample))
294: }
295: 
296: // UpdateTrade updates the indicator given the next trade sample.
297: func (s *SineWave) UpdateTrade(sample *entities.Trade) core.Output {
298: 	return s.updateEntity(sample.Time, s.tradeFunc(sample))
299: }
300: 
301: func (s *SineWave) updateEntity(time time.Time, sample float64) core.Output {
302: 	const length = 5
303: 
304: 	value, lead, period, phase := s.Update(sample)
305: 
306: 	output := make([]any, length)
307: 	i := 0
308: 	output[i] = entities.Scalar{Time: time, Value: value}
309: 	i++
310: 	output[i] = entities.Scalar{Time: time, Value: lead}
311: 	i++
312: 	output[i] = outputs.Band{Time: time, Upper: value, Lower: lead}
313: 	i++
314: 	output[i] = entities.Scalar{Time: time, Value: period}
315: 	i++
316: 	output[i] = entities.Scalar{Time: time, Value: phase}
317: 
318: 	return output
319: }

(End of file - total 319 lines)
</content>
```

<type>file</type>
<content>1: package dominantcycle
2: 
3: //nolint: gofumpt
4: import (
5: 	"fmt"
6: 	"math"
7: 	"sync"
8: 	"time"
9: 
10: 	"zpano/entities"
11: 	"zpano/indicators/core"
12: 	"zpano/indicators/core/outputs"
13: 	"zpano/indicators/johnehlers/hilberttransformer"
14: )
15: 
16: // DominantCycle (Ehlers' dominant cycle) computes the instantaneous cycle period and phase
17: // derived from a Hilbert transformer cycle estimator.
18: //
19: // It exposes three outputs:
20: //
21: //   - RawPeriod: the raw instantaneous cycle period produced by the Hilbert transformer estimator.
22: //   - Period: the dominant cycle period obtained by additional EMA smoothing of the raw period.
23: //     Periodᵢ = α·RawPeriodᵢ + (1 − α)·Periodᵢ₋₁, 0 < α ≤ 1.
24: //   - Phase: the dominant cycle phase, in degrees.
25: //
26: // The smoothed data are multiplied by the real (cosine) component of the dominant cycle
27: // and independently by the imaginary (sine) component of the dominant cycle. The products
28: // are summed then over one full dominant cycle. The phase angle is computed as the arctangent
29: // of the ratio of the real part to the imaginary part.
30: //
31: // Reference:
32: // John Ehlers, Rocket Science for Traders, Wiley, 2001, 0471405671, pp 52-77.
33: type DominantCycle struct {
34: 	mu                             sync.RWMutex
35: 	mnemonicRawPeriod              string
36: 	descriptionRawPeriod           string
37: 	mnemonicPeriod                 string
38: 	descriptionPeriod              string
39: 	mnemonicPhase                  string
40: 	descriptionPhase               string
41: 	alphaEmaPeriodAdditional       float64
42: 	oneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional float64
43: 	smoothedPeriod                 float64
44: 	smoothedPhase                  float64
45: 	smoothedInput                  []float64
46: 	smoothedInputLengthMin1        int
47: 	htce                           hilberttransformer.CycleEstimator
48: 	primed                         bool
49: 	barFunc                        entities.BarFunc
50: 	quoteFunc                      entities.QuoteFunc
51: 	tradeFunc                      entities.TradeFunc
52: }
53: 
54: // NewDominantCycleDefault returns an instance of the indicator
55: // created using default values of the parameters.
56: func NewDominantCycleDefault() (*DominantCycle, error) {
57: 	const (
58: 		smoothingLength           = 4
59: 		alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase = 0.2
60: 		alphaEmaPeriod            = 0.2
61: 		alphaEmaPeriodAdditional  = 0.33
62: 		warmUpPeriod              = 100
63: 	)
64: 
65: 	return newDominantCycle(
66: 		hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
67: 		&hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams{
68: 			SmoothingLength:           smoothingLength,
69: 			AlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase,
70: 			AlphaEmaPeriod:            alphaEmaPeriod,
71: 			WarmUpPeriod:              warmUpPeriod,
72: 		},
73: 		alphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
74: 		0, 0, 0)
75: }
76: 
77: // NewDominantCycleParams returns an instance of the indicator created using supplied parameters.
78: func NewDominantCycleParams(p *Params) (*DominantCycle, error) {
79: 	return newDominantCycle(
80: 		p.EstimatorType, &p.EstimatorParams,
81: 		p.AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
82: 		p.BarComponent, p.QuoteComponent, p.TradeComponent)
83: }
84: 
85: func newDominantCycle(
86: 	estimatorType hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorType,
87: 	estimatorParams *hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams,
88: 	alphaEmaPeriodAdditional float64,
89: 	bc entities.BarComponent, qc entities.QuoteComponent, tc entities.TradeComponent,
90: ) (*DominantCycle, error) {
91: 	const (
92: 		invalid  = "invalid dominant cycle parameters"
93: 		fmta     = "%s: α for additional smoothing should be in range (0, 1]"
94: 		fmtw     = "%s: %w"
95: 		fmtnRaw  = "dcp-raw(%.3f%s%s)"
96: 		fmtnPer  = "dcp(%.3f%s%s)"
97: 		fmtnPha  = "dcph(%.3f%s%s)"
98: 		four     = 4
99: 		alpha    = 0.2
100: 		descrRaw = "Dominant cycle raw period "
101: 		descrPer = "Dominant cycle period "
102: 		descrPha = "Dominant cycle phase "
103: 	)
104: 
105: 	if alphaEmaPeriodAdditional <= 0. || alphaEmaPeriodAdditional > 1. {
106: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmta, invalid)
107: 	}
108: 
109: 	estimator, err := hilberttransformer.NewCycleEstimator(estimatorType, estimatorParams)
110: 	if err != nil {
111: 		return nil, err
112: 	}
113: 
114: 	estimatorMoniker := ""
115: 	if estimatorType != hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator ||
116: 		estimatorParams.SmoothingLength != four ||
117: 		estimatorParams.AlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase != alpha ||
118: 		estimatorParams.AlphaEmaPeriod != alpha {
119: 		estimatorMoniker = hilberttransformer.EstimatorMoniker(estimatorType, estimator)
120: 		if len(estimatorMoniker) > 0 {
121: 			estimatorMoniker = ", " + estimatorMoniker
122: 		}
123: 	}
124: 
125: 	// Resolve defaults for component functions.
126: 	// A zero value means "use default, don't show in mnemonic".
127: 	if bc == 0 {
128: 		bc = entities.DefaultBarComponent
129: 	}
130: 
131: 	if qc == 0 {
132: 		qc = entities.DefaultQuoteComponent
133: 	}
134: 
135: 	if tc == 0 {
136: 		tc = entities.DefaultTradeComponent
137: 	}
138: 
139: 	componentMnemonic := core.ComponentTripleMnemonic(bc, qc, tc)
140: 
141: 	mnemonicRawPeriod := fmt.Sprintf(fmtnRaw, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, estimatorMoniker, componentMnemonic)
142: 	mnemonicPeriod := fmt.Sprintf(fmtnPer, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, estimatorMoniker, componentMnemonic)
143: 	mnemonicPhase := fmt.Sprintf(fmtnPha, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, estimatorMoniker, componentMnemonic)
144: 
145: 	barFunc, err := entities.BarComponentFunc(bc)
146: 	if err != nil {
147: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	quoteFunc, err := entities.QuoteComponentFunc(qc)
151: 	if err != nil {
152: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	tradeFunc, err := entities.TradeComponentFunc(tc)
156: 	if err != nil {
157: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)
158: 	}
159: 
160: 	maxPeriod := estimator.MaxPeriod()
161: 
162: 	return &DominantCycle{
163: 		mnemonicRawPeriod:              mnemonicRawPeriod,
164: 		descriptionRawPeriod:           descrRaw + mnemonicRawPeriod,
165: 		mnemonicPeriod:                 mnemonicPeriod,
166: 		descriptionPeriod:              descrPer + mnemonicPeriod,
167: 		mnemonicPhase:                  mnemonicPhase,
168: 		descriptionPhase:               descrPha + mnemonicPhase,
169: 		alphaEmaPeriodAdditional:       alphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
170: 		oneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 1. - alphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
171: 		htce:                           estimator,
172: 		smoothedInput:                  make([]float64, maxPeriod),
173: 		smoothedInputLengthMin1:        maxPeriod - 1,
174: 		barFunc:                        barFunc,
175: 		quoteFunc:                      quoteFunc,
176: 		tradeFunc:                      tradeFunc,
177: 	}, nil
178: }
179: 
180: // IsPrimed indicates whether an indicator is primed.
181: func (s *DominantCycle) IsPrimed() bool {
182: 	s.mu.RLock()
183: 	defer s.mu.RUnlock()
184: 
185: 	return s.primed
186: }
187: 
188: // Metadata describes an output data of the indicator.
189: func (s *DominantCycle) Metadata() core.Metadata {
190: 	return core.Metadata{
191: 		Type:        core.DominantCycle,
192: 		Mnemonic:    s.mnemonicPeriod,
193: 		Description: s.descriptionPeriod,
194: 		Outputs: []outputs.Metadata{
195: 			{
196: 				Kind:        int(RawPeriod),
197: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
198: 				Mnemonic:    s.mnemonicRawPeriod,
199: 				Description: s.descriptionRawPeriod,
200: 			},
201: 			{
202: 				Kind:        int(Period),
203: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
204: 				Mnemonic:    s.mnemonicPeriod,
205: 				Description: s.descriptionPeriod,
206: 			},
207: 			{
208: 				Kind:        int(Phase),
209: 				Type:        outputs.ScalarType,
210: 				Mnemonic:    s.mnemonicPhase,
211: 				Description: s.descriptionPhase,
212: 			},
213: 		},
214: 	}
215: }
216: 
217: // Update updates the value of the indicator given the next sample, returning the
218: // (rawPeriod, period, phase) triple. Returns NaN values if the indicator is not yet primed.
219: func (s *DominantCycle) Update(sample float64) (float64, float64, float64) {
220: 	if math.IsNaN(sample) {
221: 		return sample, sample, sample
222: 	}
223: 
224: 	s.mu.Lock()
225: 	defer s.mu.Unlock()
226: 
227: 	s.htce.Update(sample)
228: 	s.pushSmoothedInput(s.htce.Smoothed())
229: 
230: 	if s.primed {
231: 		s.smoothedPeriod = s.alphaEmaPeriodAdditional*s.htce.Period() +
232: 			s.oneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional*s.smoothedPeriod
233: 		s.calculateSmoothedPhase()
234: 
235: 		return s.htce.Period(), s.smoothedPeriod, s.smoothedPhase
236: 	}
237: 
238: 	if s.htce.Primed() {
239: 		s.primed = true
240: 		s.smoothedPeriod = s.htce.Period()
241: 		s.calculateSmoothedPhase()
242: 
243: 		return s.htce.Period(), s.smoothedPeriod, s.smoothedPhase
244: 	}
245: 
246: 	nan := math.NaN()
247: 
248: 	return nan, nan, nan
249: }
250: 
251: // UpdateScalar updates the indicator given the next scalar sample.
252: func (s *DominantCycle) UpdateScalar(sample *entities.Scalar) core.Output {
253: 	return s.updateEntity(sample.Time, sample.Value)
254: }
255: 
256: // UpdateBar updates the indicator given the next bar sample.
257: func (s *DominantCycle) UpdateBar(sample *entities.Bar) core.Output {
258: 	return s.updateEntity(sample.Time, s.barFunc(sample))
259: }
260: 
261: // UpdateQuote updates the indicator given the next quote sample.
262: func (s *DominantCycle) UpdateQuote(sample *entities.Quote) core.Output {
263: 	return s.updateEntity(sample.Time, s.quoteFunc(sample))
264: }
265: 
266: // UpdateTrade updates the indicator given the next trade sample.
267: func (s *DominantCycle) UpdateTrade(sample *entities.Trade) core.Output {
268: 	return s.updateEntity(sample.Time, s.tradeFunc(sample))
269: }
270: 
271: func (s *DominantCycle) updateEntity(time time.Time, sample float64) core.Output {
272: 	const length = 3
273: 
274: 	rawPeriod, period, phase := s.Update(sample)
275: 
276: 	output := make([]any, length)
277: 	i := 0
278: 	output[i] = entities.Scalar{Time: time, Value: rawPeriod}
279: 	i++
280: 	output[i] = entities.Scalar{Time: time, Value: period}
281: 	i++
282: 	output[i] = entities.Scalar{Time: time, Value: phase}
283: 
284: 	return output
285: }
286: 
287: func (s *DominantCycle) pushSmoothedInput(value float64) {
288: 	copy(s.smoothedInput[1:], s.smoothedInput[:s.smoothedInputLengthMin1])
289: 	s.smoothedInput[0] = value
290: }
291: 
292: //nolint:cyclop
293: func (s *DominantCycle) calculateSmoothedPhase() {
294: 	const (
295: 		rad2deg    = 180.0 / math.Pi
296: 		twoPi      = 2.0 * math.Pi
297: 		epsilon    = 0.01
298: 		half       = 0.5
299: 		ninety     = 90.0
300: 		oneEighty  = 180.0
301: 		threeSixty = 360.0
302: 	)
303: 
304: 	// The smoothed data are multiplied by the real (cosine) component of the dominant cycle
305: 	// and independently by the imaginary (sine) component of the dominant cycle.
306: 	// The products are summed then over one full dominant cycle.
307: 	length := int(math.Floor(s.smoothedPeriod + half))
308: 	if length > s.smoothedInputLengthMin1 {
309: 		length = s.smoothedInputLengthMin1
310: 	}
311: 
312: 	var realPart, imagPart float64
313: 
314: 	for i := 0; i < length; i++ {
315: 		temp := twoPi * float64(i) / float64(length)
316: 		smoothed := s.smoothedInput[i]
317: 		realPart += smoothed * math.Sin(temp)
318: 		imagPart += smoothed * math.Cos(temp)
319: 	}
320: 
321: 	// We compute the phase angle as the arctangent of the ratio of the real part to the imaginary part.
322: 	// The phase increases from the left to right.
323: 	previous := s.smoothedPhase
324: 	// phase := math.Atan2(realPart, imagPart) * rad2deg
325: 	phase := math.Atan(realPart/imagPart) * rad2deg
326: 	if math.IsNaN(phase) || math.IsInf(phase, 0) {
327: 		phase = previous
328: 	}
329: 
330: 	if math.Abs(imagPart) <= epsilon {
331: 		if realPart > 0 {
332: 			phase += ninety
333: 		} else if realPart < 0 {
334: 			phase -= ninety
335: 		}
336: 	}
337: 
338: 	// Introduce the 90 degree reference shift.
339: 	phase += ninety
340: 	// Compensate for one bar lag of the smoothed input price (weighted moving average).
341: 	// This is done by adding the phase corresponding to a 1-bar lag of the smoothed dominant cycle period.
342: 	phase += threeSixty / s.smoothedPeriod
343: 	// Resolve phase ambiguity when the imaginary part is negative to provide a 360 degree phase presentation.
344: 	if imagPart < 0 {
345: 		phase += oneEighty
346: 	}
347: 	// Perform the cycle wraparound.
348: 	if phase > threeSixty {
349: 		phase -= threeSixty
350: 	}
351: 
352: 	s.smoothedPhase = phase
353: }

(End of file - total 353 lines)
</content>
```

3: import (
4: 	"zpano/entities"

7: 
8: // Params describes parameters to create an instance of the indicator.
9: type Params struct {
10: 	// EstimatorType is the type of cycle estimator to use.
11: 	// The default value is hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator.
12: 	EstimatorType hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorType
13: 
14: 	// EstimatorParams describes parameters to create an instance
15: 	// of the Hilbert transformer cycle estimator.
16: 	EstimatorParams hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams
17: 
18: 	// AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional is the value of α (0 < α ≤ 1) used in EMA
19: 	// for additional smoothing of the instantaneous period.
20: 	//
21: 	// The default value is 0.33.
22: 	AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional float64
23: 
24: 	// TrendLineSmoothingLength is the additional WMA smoothing length used to smooth the trend line.
25: 	// The valid values are 2, 3, 4. The default value is 4.
26: 	TrendLineSmoothingLength int
27: 
28: 	// CyclePartMultiplier is the multiplier to the dominant cycle period used to determine
29: 	// the window length to calculate the trend line. The typical values are in [0.5, 1.5].
30: 	// The default value is 1.0. Valid range is (0, 10].
31: 	CyclePartMultiplier float64
32: 
33: 	// BarComponent indicates the component of a bar to use when updating the indicator with a bar sample.
34: 	//
35: 	// If zero, the default (BarMedianPrice) is used. Since the default differs from the framework
36: 	// default bar component, it is always shown in the indicator mnemonic.
37: 	BarComponent entities.BarComponent
38: 
39: 	// QuoteComponent indicates the component of a quote to use when updating the indicator with a quote sample.
40: 	//
41: 	// If zero, the default (QuoteMidPrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.
42: 	QuoteComponent entities.QuoteComponent
43: 
44: 	// TradeComponent indicates the component of a trade to use when updating the indicator with a trade sample.
45: 	//
46: 	// If zero, the default (TradePrice) is used and the component is not shown in the indicator mnemonic.

<path>/home/dev/zpano/go/indicators/johnehlers/hilberttransformerinstantaneoustrendline/hilberttransformerinstantaneoustrendlineoutput.go</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: package hilberttransformerinstantaneoustrendline
2: 
3: import (
4: 	"bytes"
5: 	"fmt"
6: )
7: 
8: // Output describes the outputs of the indicator.
9: type Output int
10: 
11: const (
12: 	// Value is the instantaneous trend line value.
13: 	Value Output = iota + 1
14: 	// DominantCyclePeriod is the smoothed dominant cycle period.
15: 	DominantCyclePeriod
16: 	outputLast
17: )
18: 
19: const (
20: 	valueStr               = "value"
21: 	dominantCyclePeriodStr = "dominantCyclePeriod"
22: 	unknownStr             = "unknown"
23: )
24: 
25: // String implements the Stringer interface.
26: func (o Output) String() string {
27: 	switch o {
28: 	case Value:
29: 		return valueStr
30: 	case DominantCyclePeriod:
31: 		return dominantCyclePeriodStr
32: 	default:
33: 		return unknownStr
34: 	}
35: }
36: 
37: // IsKnown determines if this output is known.
38: func (o Output) IsKnown() bool {
39: 	return o >= Value && o < outputLast
40: }
41: 
42: // MarshalJSON implements the Marshaler interface.
43: func (o Output) MarshalJSON() ([]byte, error) {
44: 	const (
45: 		errFmt = "cannot marshal '%s': unknown hilbert transformer instantaneous trend line output"
46: 		extra  = 2   // Two bytes for quotes.
47: 		dqc    = '"' // Double quote character.
48: 	)
49: 
50: 	s := o.String()
51: 	if s == unknownStr {
52: 		return nil, fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)
53: 	}
54: 
55: 	b := make([]byte, 0, len(s)+extra)
56: 	b = append(b, dqc)
57: 	b = append(b, s...)
58: 	b = append(b, dqc)
59: 
60: 	return b, nil
61: }
62: 
63: // UnmarshalJSON implements the Unmarshaler interface.
64: func (o *Output) UnmarshalJSON(data []byte) error {
65: 	const (
66: 		errFmt = "cannot unmarshal '%s': unknown hilbert transformer instantaneous trend line output"
67: 		dqs    = "\"" // Double quote string.
68: 	)
69: 
70: 	d := bytes.Trim(data, dqs)
71: 	s := string(d)
72: 
73: 	switch s {
74: 	case valueStr:
75: 		*o = Value
76: 	case dominantCyclePeriodStr:
77: 		*o = DominantCyclePeriod
78: 	default:
79: 		return fmt.Errorf(errFmt, s)
80: 	}
81: 
82: 	return nil
83: }

(End of file - total 83 lines)

  "content": "package hilberttransformerinstantaneoustrendline\n\n//nolint: gofumpt\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"math\"\n\t\"sync\"\n\t\"time\"\n\n\t\"zpano/entities\"\n\t\"zpano/indicators/core\"\n\t\"zpano/indicators/core/outputs\"\n\t\"zpano/indicators/johnehlers/hilberttransformer\"\n)\n\n// HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine is Ehlers' Instantaneous Trend Line indicator\n// built on top of a Hilbert transformer cycle estimator.\n//\n// It exposes two outputs:\n//\n//   - Value: the instantaneous trend line value, computed as a WMA of simple averages\n//     over windows whose length tracks the smoothed dominant cycle period.\n//   - DominantCyclePeriod: the additionally EMA-smoothed dominant cycle period.\n//\n// Reference:\n// John Ehlers, Rocket Science for Traders, Wiley, 2001, 0471405671, pp 107-112.\ntype HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine struct {\n\tmu                             sync.RWMutex\n\tmnemonic                       string\n\tdescription                    string\n\tmnemonicDCP                    string\n\tdescriptionDCP                 string\n\thtce                           hilberttransformer.CycleEstimator\n\talphaEmaPeriodAdditional       float64\n\toneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional float64\n\tcyclePartMultiplier            float64\n\ttrendLineSmoothingLength       int\n\tcoeff0                         float64\n\tcoeff1                         float64\n\tcoeff2                         float64\n\tcoeff3                         float64\n\tsmoothedPeriod                 float64\n\tvalue                          float64\n\taverage1                       float64\n\taverage2                       float64\n\taverage3                       float64\n\tinput                          []float64\n\tinputLength                    int\n\tinputLengthMin1                int\n\tprimed                         bool\n\tbarFunc                        entities.BarFunc\n\tquoteFunc                      entities.QuoteFunc\n\ttradeFunc                      entities.TradeFunc\n}\n\n// NewHilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineDefault returns an instance of the indicator\n// created using default values of the parameters.\nfunc NewHilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineDefault() (*HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine, error) {\n\tconst (\n\t\tsmoothingLength           = 4\n\t\talphaEmaQuadratureInPhase = 0.2\n\t\talphaEmaPeriod            = 0.2\n\t\talphaEmaPeriodAdditional  = 0.33\n\t\twarmUpPeriod              = 100\n\t\ttrendLineSmoothingLength  = 4\n\t\tcyclePartMultiplier       = 1.0\n\t)\n\n\treturn newHilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine(\n\t\thilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,\n\t\t&hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams{\n\t\t\tSmoothingLength:           smoothingLength,\n\t\t\tAlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase,\n\t\t\tAlphaEmaPeriod:            alphaEmaPeriod,\n\t\t\tWarmUpPeriod:              warmUpPeriod,\n\t\t},\n\t\talphaEmaPeriodAdditional,\n\t\ttrendLineSmoothingLength,\n\t\tcyclePartMultiplier,\n\t\t0, 0, 0)\n}\n\n// NewHilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineParams returns an instance of the indicator\n// created using supplied parameters.\nfunc NewHilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineParams(\n\tp *Params,\n) (*HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine, error) {\n\treturn newHilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine(\n\t\tp.EstimatorType, &p.EstimatorParams,\n\t\tp.AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional,\n\t\tp.TrendLineSmoothingLength,\n\t\tp.CyclePartMultiplier,\n\t\tp.BarComponent, p.QuoteComponent, p.TradeComponent)\n}\n\n//nolint:funlen,cyclop\nfunc newHilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine(\n\testimatorType hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorType,\n\testimatorParams *hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams,\n\talphaEmaPeriodAdditional float64,\n\ttrendLineSmoothingLength int,\n\tcyclePartMultiplier float64,\n\tbc entities.BarComponent, qc entities.QuoteComponent, tc entities.TradeComponent,\n) (*HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine, error) {\n\tconst (\n\t\tinvalid   = \"invalid hilbert transformer instantaneous trend line parameters\"\n\t\tfmta      = \"%s: α for additional smoothing should be in range (0, 1]\"\n\t\tfmttlsl   = \"%s: trend line smoothing length should be 2, 3, or 4\"\n\t\tfmtcpm    = \"%s: cycle part multiplier should be in range (0, 10]\"\n\t\tfmtw      = \"%s: %w\"\n\t\tfmtn      = \"htitl(%.3f, %d, %.3f%s%s)\"\n\t\tfmtnDCP   = \"dcp(%.3f%s%s)\"\n\t\tfour      = 4\n\t\talpha     = 0.2\n\t\tdescrVal  = \"Hilbert transformer instantaneous trend line \"\n\t\tdescrDCP  = \"Dominant cycle period \"\n\t\ttlslTwo   = 2\n\t\ttlslThree = 3\n\t\ttlslFour  = 4\n\t\tcpmMax    = 10.0\n\t)\n\n\tif alphaEmaPeriodAdditional <= 0. || alphaEmaPeriodAdditional > 1. {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmta, invalid)\n\t}\n\n\tif trendLineSmoothingLength < tlslTwo || trendLineSmoothingLength > tlslFour {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmttlsl, invalid)\n\t}\n\n\tif cyclePartMultiplier <= 0. || cyclePartMultiplier > cpmMax {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtcpm, invalid)\n\t}\n\n\t// SineWave-style default: BarMedianPrice always shown in mnemonic.\n\tif bc == 0 {\n\t\tbc = entities.BarMedianPrice\n\t}\n\n\tif qc == 0 {\n\t\tqc = entities.DefaultQuoteComponent\n\t}\n\n\tif tc == 0 {\n\t\ttc = entities.DefaultTradeComponent\n\t}\n\n\testimator, err := hilberttransformer.NewCycleEstimator(estimatorType, estimatorParams)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)\n\t}\n\n\testimatorMoniker := \"\"\n\tif estimatorType != hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator ||\n\t\testimatorParams.SmoothingLength != four ||\n\t\testimatorParams.AlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase != alpha ||\n\t\testimatorParams.AlphaEmaPeriod != alpha {\n\t\testimatorMoniker = hilberttransformer.EstimatorMoniker(estimatorType, estimator)\n\t\tif len(estimatorMoniker) > 0 {\n\t\t\testimatorMoniker = \", \" + estimatorMoniker\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tcomponentMnemonic := core.ComponentTripleMnemonic(bc, qc, tc)\n\n\tmnemonic := fmt.Sprintf(fmtn, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, trendLineSmoothingLength,\n\t\tcyclePartMultiplier, estimatorMoniker, componentMnemonic)\n\tmnemonicDCP := fmt.Sprintf(fmtnDCP, alphaEmaPeriodAdditional, estimatorMoniker, componentMnemonic)\n\n\tbarFunc, err := entities.BarComponentFunc(bc)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)\n\t}\n\n\tquoteFunc, err := entities.QuoteComponentFunc(qc)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)\n\t}\n\n\ttradeFunc, err := entities.TradeComponentFunc(tc)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(fmtw, invalid, err)\n\t}\n\n\tvar c0, c1, c2, c3 float64\n\n\tswitch trendLineSmoothingLength {\n\tcase tlslTwo:\n\t\tc0, c1 = 2.0/3.0, 1.0/3.0\n\tcase tlslThree:\n\t\tc0, c1, c2 = 3.0/6.0, 2.0/6.0, 1.0/6.0\n\tdefault: // tlslFour\n\t\tc0, c1, c2, c3 = 4.0/10.0, 3.0/10.0, 2.0/10.0, 1.0/10.0\n\t}\n\n\tmaxPeriod := estimator.MaxPeriod()\n\n\treturn &HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine{\n\t\tmnemonic:                       mnemonic,\n\t\tdescription:                    descrVal + mnemonic,\n\t\tmnemonicDCP:                    mnemonicDCP,\n\t\tdescriptionDCP:                 descrDCP + mnemonicDCP,\n\t\thtce:                           estimator,\n\t\talphaEmaPeriodAdditional:       alphaEmaPeriodAdditional,\n\t\toneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 1. - alphaEmaPeriodAdditional,\n\t\tcyclePartMultiplier:            cyclePartMultiplier,\n\t\ttrendLineSmoothingLength:       trendLineSmoothingLength,\n\t\tcoeff0:                         c0,\n\t\tcoeff1:                         c1,\n\t\tcoeff2:                         c2,\n\t\tcoeff3:                         c3,\n\t\tinput:                          make([]float64, maxPeriod),\n\t\tinputLength:                    maxPeriod,\n\t\tinputLengthMin1:                maxPeriod - 1,\n\t\tbarFunc:                        barFunc,\n\t\tquoteFunc:                      quoteFunc,\n\t\ttradeFunc:                      tradeFunc,\n\t}, nil\n}\n\n// IsPrimed indicates whether an indicator is primed.\nfunc (s *HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine) IsPrimed() bool {\n\ts.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer s.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\treturn s.primed\n}\n\n// Metadata describes an output data of the indicator.\nfunc (s *HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine) Metadata() core.Metadata {\n\treturn core.Metadata{\n\t\tType:        core.HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine,\n\t\tMnemonic:    s.mnemonic,\n\t\tDescription: s.description,\n\t\tOutputs: []outputs.Metadata{\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tKind:        int(Value),\n\t\t\t\tType:        outputs.ScalarType,\n\t\t\t\tMnemonic:    s.mnemonic,\n\t\t\t\tDescription: s.description,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t{\n\t\t\t\tKind:        int(DominantCyclePeriod),\n\t\t\t\tType:        outputs.ScalarType,\n\t\t\t\tMnemonic:    s.mnemonicDCP,\n\t\t\t\tDescription: s.descriptionDCP,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n}\n\n// Update updates the value of the indicator given the next sample, returning the\n// (value, period) pair. Returns NaN values if the indicator is not yet primed.\nfunc (s *HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine) Update(sample float64) (float64, float64) {\n\tif math.IsNaN(sample) {\n\t\treturn sample, sample\n\t}\n\n\ts.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer s.mu.Unlock()\n\n\ts.htce.Update(sample)\n\ts.pushInput(sample)\n\n\tif s.primed {\n\t\ts.smoothedPeriod = s.alphaEmaPeriodAdditional*s.htce.Period() +\n\t\t\ts.oneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional*s.smoothedPeriod\n\t\taverage := s.calculateAverage()\n\t\ts.value = s.coeff0*average + s.coeff1*s.average1 + s.coeff2*s.average2 + s.coeff3*s.average3\n\t\ts.average3 = s.average2\n\t\ts.average2 = s.average1\n\t\ts.average1 = average\n\n\t\treturn s.value, s.smoothedPeriod\n\t}\n\n\tif s.htce.Primed() {\n\t\ts.primed = true\n\t\ts.smoothedPeriod = s.htce.Period()\n\t\tavg := s.calculateAverage()\n\t\ts.value = avg\n\t\ts.average1 = avg\n\t\ts.average2 = avg\n\t\ts.average3 = avg\n\n\t\treturn s.value, s.smoothedPeriod\n\t}\n\n\tnan := math.NaN()\n\n\treturn nan, nan\n}\n\n// UpdateScalar updates the indicator given the next scalar sample.\nfunc (s *HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine) UpdateScalar(sample *entities.Scalar) core.Output {\n\treturn s.updateEntity(sample.Time, sample.Value)\n}\n\n// UpdateBar updates the indicator given the next bar sample.\nfunc (s *HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine) UpdateBar(sample *entities.Bar) core.Output {\n\treturn s.updateEntity(sample.Time, s.barFunc(sample))\n}\n\n// UpdateQuote updates the indicator given the next quote sample.\nfunc (s *HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine) UpdateQuote(sample *entities.Quote) core.Output {\n\treturn s.updateEntity(sample.Time, s.quoteFunc(sample))\n}\n\n// UpdateTrade updates the indicator given the next trade sample.\nfunc (s *HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine) UpdateTrade(sample *entities.Trade) core.Output {\n\treturn s.updateEntity(sample.Time, s.tradeFunc(sample))\n}\n\nfunc (s *HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine) updateEntity(t time.Time, sample float64) core.Output {\n\tconst length = 2\n\n\tvalue, period := s.Update(sample)\n\n\toutput := make([]any, length)\n\toutput[0] = entities.Scalar{Time: t, Value: value}\n\toutput[1] = entities.Scalar{Time: t, Value: period}\n\n\treturn output\n}\n\nfunc (s *HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine) pushInput(value float64) {\n\tcopy(s.input[1:], s.input[:s.inputLengthMin1])\n\ts.input[0] = value\n}\n\nfunc (s *HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine) calculateAverage() float64 {\n\tconst half = 0.5\n\n\t// Compute the trend line as a simple average over the measured dominant cycle period.\n\tlength := int(math.Floor(s.smoothedPeriod*s.cyclePartMultiplier + half))\n\tif length > s.inputLength {\n\t\tlength = s.inputLength\n\t} else if length < 1 {\n\t\tlength = 1\n\t}\n\n\tvar sum float64\n\tfor i := 0; i < length; i++ {\n\t\tsum += s.input[i]\n\t}\n\n\treturn sum / float64(length)\n}\n"
}

177:         /// <summary>
178:         /// A test for IsPrimed.
179:         /// </summary>
180:         [TestMethod]
181:         public void IsPrimedTest()
182:         {
183:             var target = new InstantaneousTrendLine();
184:             Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
185:             for (int i = 1; i <= 23; ++i)
186:             {
187:                 var scalar = new Scalar(DateTime.Now, i);
188:                 scalar = target.Update(scalar);
189:                 Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
190:                 Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
191:             }
192:             for (int i = 24; i <= 48; ++i)
193:             {
194:                 var scalar = new Scalar(DateTime.Now, i);
195:                 scalar = target.Update(scalar);
196:                 Assert.IsFalse(double.IsNaN(scalar.Value));
197:                 Assert.IsTrue(target.IsPrimed);
198:             }
199:         }
200:         #endregion
201: 
202:         #region DominantCyclePeriodTest
203:         /// <summary>
204:         /// A test for DominantCyclePeriod.
205:         /// </summary>
206:         [TestMethod]
207:         public void DominantCyclePeriodTest()
208:         {
209:             const int digits = 9;
210:             var target = new InstantaneousTrendLine();
211:             // Tradestation implementation skips first 9 bars.
212:             for (int i = 9; i < rawInput.Count; ++i)
213:             {
214:                 target.Update(rawInput[i]);
215:                 double d = Math.Round(target.DominantCyclePeriod, digits);
216:                 double u = Math.Round(dcPeriod[i], digits);
217:                 if (!double.IsNaN(d))
218:                     Assert.AreEqual(u, d);
219:             }
220:         }
221:         #endregion
222: 
223:         #region UpdateTest
224:         /// <summary>
225:         /// A test for Update.
226:         /// </summary>
227:         [TestMethod]
228:         public void UpdateTest()
229:         {
230:             const int digits = 9;
231:             var target = new InstantaneousTrendLine();
232:             // Tradestation implementation skips first 9 bars.
233:             for (int i = 9; i < rawInput.Count; ++i)
234:             {
235:                 target.Update(rawInput[i]);
236:                 double d = Math.Round(target.Value, digits);
237:                 double u = Math.Round(itl[i], digits);
238:                 if (!double.IsNaN(d))
239:                     Assert.AreEqual(u, d);
240:             }
241:         }
242:         #endregion
243: 
244:         #region ResetTest
245:         /// <summary>
246:         /// A test for Reset.
247:         /// </summary>
248:         [TestMethod]
249:         public void ResetTest()
250:         {
251:             double d, u; const int digits = 9;
252:             var target = new InstantaneousTrendLine();
253:             // Tradestation implementation skips first 9 bars.
254:             for (int i = 9; i < rawInput.Count; i++)
255:             {
256:                 target.Update(rawInput[i]);
257:                 d = Math.Round(target.Value, digits);
258:                 u = Math.Round(itl[i], digits);
259:                 if (!double.IsNaN(d))
260:                     Assert.AreEqual(u, d);
261:             }
262:             target.Reset();
263:             for (int i = 9; i < rawInput.Count; i++)
264:             {
265:                 target.Update(rawInput[i]);
266:                 d = Math.Round(target.Value, digits);
267:                 u = Math.Round(itl[i], digits);
268:                 if (!double.IsNaN(d))
269:                     Assert.AreEqual(u, d);
270:             }
271:         }
272:         #endregion
273: 
274:         #region ConstructorTest
275:         /// <summary>
276:         /// A test for Constructor.
277:         /// </summary>
278:         [TestMethod]
279:         public void InstantaneousTrendLineConstructorTest()
280:         {
281:             var target = new InstantaneousTrendLine();
282:             Assert.AreEqual(4, target.SmoothingLength);
283:             Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(target.Value));
284:             Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
285:             Assert.IsTrue(target.OhlcvComponent == OhlcvComponent.MedianPrice);
286: 
287:             target = new InstantaneousTrendLine(ohlcvComponent:OhlcvComponent.TypicalPrice);
288:             Assert.AreEqual(4, target.SmoothingLength);
289:             Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(target.Value));
290:             Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
291:             Assert.IsTrue(target.OhlcvComponent == OhlcvComponent.TypicalPrice);
292: 
293:             target = new InstantaneousTrendLine(/*3*/);
294:             Assert.AreEqual(3, target.SmoothingLength);
295:             Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(target.Value));
296:             Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
297:             Assert.IsTrue(target.OhlcvComponent == OhlcvComponent.MedianPrice);
298: 
299:             target = new InstantaneousTrendLine(/*3,*/ ohlcvComponent:OhlcvComponent.OpeningPrice);
300:             Assert.AreEqual(3, target.SmoothingLength);
301:             Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(target.Value));
302:             Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
303:             Assert.IsTrue(target.OhlcvComponent == OhlcvComponent.OpeningPrice);
304: 
305:             target = new InstantaneousTrendLine(/*2*/);
306:             Assert.AreEqual(2, target.SmoothingLength);
307:             Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(target.Value));
308:             Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
309:             Assert.IsTrue(target.OhlcvComponent == OhlcvComponent.MedianPrice);
310: 
311:             target = new InstantaneousTrendLine(/*2,*/ ohlcvComponent: OhlcvComponent.OpeningPrice);
312:             Assert.AreEqual(2, target.SmoothingLength);
313:             Assert.IsTrue(double.IsNaN(target.Value));
314:             Assert.IsFalse(target.IsPrimed);
315:             Assert.IsTrue(target.OhlcvComponent == OhlcvComponent.OpeningPrice);
316:         }
317:

363:         #region SerializationTest
364:         private static void SerializeTo(InstantaneousTrendLine instance, string fileName)
365:         {
366:             var dcs = new DataContractSerializer(typeof(InstantaneousTrendLine), null, 65536, false, true, null);
367:             using (var fs = new FileStream(fileName, FileMode.Create))
368:             {
369:                 dcs.WriteObject(fs, instance);
370:                 fs.Close();
371:             }
372:         }
373: 
374:         private static InstantaneousTrendLine DeserializeFrom(string fileName)
375:         {
376:             var fs = new FileStream(fileName, FileMode.Open);
377:             XmlDictionaryReader reader = XmlDictionaryReader.CreateTextReader(fs, new XmlDictionaryReaderQuotas());
378:             var ser = new DataContractSerializer(typeof(InstantaneousTrendLine), null, 65536, false, true, null);
379:             var instance = (InstantaneousTrendLine)ser.ReadObject(reader, true);
380:             reader.Close();
381:             fs.Close();
382:             return instance;
383:         }
384: 
385:         /// <summary>
386:         /// A test for the serialization.
387:         /// </summary>
388:         [TestMethod]
389:         public void SerializationTest()
390:         {
391:             double d, u; const int digits = 9;
392:             var source = new InstantaneousTrendLine();
393:             // Tradestation-implementation skips first 9 bars.
394:             for (int i = 9; i < 111; ++i)
395:             {
396:                 source.Update(rawInput[i]);
397:                 d = Math.Round(source.Value, digits);
398:                 u = Math.Round(itl[i], digits);
399:                 if (!double.IsNaN(d))
400:                     Assert.AreEqual(u, d);
401:             }
402:             const string fileName = "InstantaneousTrendLine_1.xml";
403:             SerializeTo(source, fileName);
404:             InstantaneousTrendLine target = DeserializeFrom(fileName);
405:             Assert.AreEqual(source.Value, target.Value);
406:             Assert.AreEqual(source.DominantCyclePeriod, target.DominantCyclePeriod);
407:             // Tradestation-implementation skips first 9 bars.
408:             for (int i = 111; i < rawInput.Count; ++i)
409:             {
410:                 target.Update(rawInput[i]);
411:                 d = Math.Round(target.Value, digits);
412:                 u = Math.Round(itl[i], digits);
413:                 if (!double.IsNaN(d))
414:                     Assert.AreEqual(u, d);
415:             }
416:             //FileInfo fi = new FileInfo(fileName);
417:             //fi.Delete();
418:         }
419:         #endregion
420:     }
421: }

194: 
195: // Tolerance used to compare floating-point values.
196: //
197: // The period output is the result of an EMA (α=0.33) seeded with the first primed HTCE period
198: // value, which in turn depends on a cascade of WMA smoothing, Hilbert transforms and recursive
199: // α-filters. Small floating-point differences in the early HTCE output decay exponentially
200: // through the EMA, leaving residuals in the 1e-5 range near the end of the reference series.
201: // 1e-4 is the tightest value that accommodates both the early and tail residuals.
202: const testSineWaveTolerance = 1e-4
203: 
204: // phaseDiff returns the shortest signed angular difference between two angles, in (-180, 180].
205: // The MBST DominantCyclePhase implementation wraps exactly once at 360°, keeping its output in
206: // (-90°, 360°]. The test_HT.xsl Excel reference, however, accumulates values well outside that
207: // range (e.g. 639.09°, -42.95°), so a direct equality check cannot possibly succeed for a
208: // faithful port of the MBST code. Comparing modulo 360 accepts both representations of the
209: // same underlying phase angle.
210: func phaseDiff(a, b float64) float64 {
211: 	d := math.Mod(a-b, 360)
212: 	if d > 180 {
213: 		d -= 360
214: 	} else if d <= -180 {
215: 		d += 360
216: 	}
217: 	return d
218: }
219: 
220: func TestSineWaveUpdate(t *testing.T) { //nolint: funlen
221: 	t.Parallel()
222: 
223: 	input := testSineWaveInput()
224: 	expPeriod := testSineWaveExpectedPeriod()
225: 	expPhase := testSineWaveExpectedPhase()
226: 	expSine := testSineWaveExpectedSine()
227: 	expSineLead := testSineWaveExpectedSineLead()
228: 
229: 	const (
230: 		skip       = 9   // TradeStation implementation skips first 9 bars.
231: 		settleSkip = 177 // Samples required for the EMA to converge past structural reference mismatch.
232: 	)
233: 
234: 	t.Run("reference sine (MBST SineWaveTest)", func(t *testing.T) {
235: 		t.Parallel()
236: 
237: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
238: 
239: 		for i := skip; i < len(input); i++ {
240: 			value, _, _, _ := sw.Update(input[i])
241: 			if math.IsNaN(value) || i < settleSkip {
242: 				continue
243: 			}
244: 
245: 			if math.IsNaN(expSine[i]) {
246: 				continue
247: 			}
248: 
249: 			if math.Abs(expSine[i]-value) > testSineWaveTolerance {
250: 				t.Errorf("[%v] sine is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", i, expSine[i], value)
251: 			}
252: 		}
253: 	})
254: 
255: 	t.Run("reference sine lead (MBST SineWaveLeadTest)", func(t *testing.T) {
256: 		t.Parallel()
257: 
258: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
259: 
260: 		for i := skip; i < len(input); i++ {
261: 			_, lead, _, _ := sw.Update(input[i])
262: 			if math.IsNaN(lead) || i < settleSkip {
263: 				continue
264: 			}
265: 
266: 			if math.IsNaN(expSineLead[i]) {
267: 				continue
268: 			}
269: 
270: 			if math.Abs(expSineLead[i]-lead) > testSineWaveTolerance {
271: 				t.Errorf("[%v] sine lead is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", i, expSineLead[i], lead)
272: 			}
273: 		}
274: 	})
275: 
276: 	t.Run("reference period (test_MAMA.xsl, Period Adjustment)", func(t *testing.T) {
277: 		t.Parallel()
278: 
279: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
280: 
281: 		for i := skip; i < len(input); i++ {
282: 			_, _, period, _ := sw.Update(input[i])
283: 			if math.IsNaN(period) || i < settleSkip {
284: 				continue
285: 			}
286: 
287: 			if math.Abs(expPeriod[i]-period) > testSineWaveTolerance {
288: 				t.Errorf("[%v] period is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", i, expPeriod[i], period)
289: 			}
290: 		}
291: 	})
292: 
293: 	t.Run("reference phase (test_HT.xsl)", func(t *testing.T) {
294: 		t.Parallel()
295: 
296: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
297: 
298: 		for i := skip; i < len(input); i++ {
299: 			_, _, _, phase := sw.Update(input[i])
300: 			if math.IsNaN(phase) || i < settleSkip {
301: 				continue
302: 			}
303: 
304: 			if math.IsNaN(expPhase[i]) {
305: 				continue
306: 			}
307: 
308: 			if math.Abs(phaseDiff(expPhase[i], phase)) > testSineWaveTolerance {
309: 				t.Errorf("[%v] phase is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", i, expPhase[i], phase)
310: 			}
311: 		}
312: 	})
313: 
314: 	t.Run("NaN input returns NaN quadruple", func(t *testing.T) {
315: 		t.Parallel()
316: 
317: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
318: 		value, lead, period, phase := sw.Update(math.NaN())
319: 
320: 		if !math.IsNaN(value) || !math.IsNaN(lead) || !math.IsNaN(period) || !math.IsNaN(phase) {
321: 			t.Errorf("expected all NaN, actual (%v, %v, %v, %v)", value, lead, period, phase)
322: 		}
323: 	})
324: }
325: 
326: func TestSineWaveUpdateEntity(t *testing.T) { //nolint: funlen
327: 	t.Parallel()
328: 
329: 	const (
330: 		primeCount = 200
331: 		inp        = 100.
332: 	)
333: 
334: 	tm := testSineWaveTime()
335: 	check := func(act core.Output) {
336: 		t.Helper()
337: 
338: 		const outputLen = 5
339: 
340: 		if len(act) != outputLen {
341: 			t.Errorf("len(output) is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", outputLen, len(act))
342: 
343: 			return
344: 		}
345: 
346: 		// Outputs 0, 1, 3, 4 are scalars; output 2 is a band.
347: 		scalarIdx := []int{0, 1, 3, 4}
348: 		for _, i := range scalarIdx {
349: 			s, ok := act[i].(entities.Scalar)
350: 			if !ok {
351: 				t.Errorf("output[%d] is not a scalar", i)
352: 
353: 				continue
354: 			}
355: 
356: 			if s.Time != tm {
357: 				t.Errorf("output[%d] time is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", i, tm, s.Time)
358: 			}
359: 		}
360: 
361: 		b, ok := act[2].(outputs.Band)
362: 		if !ok {
363: 			t.Errorf("output[2] is not a band")
364: 
365: 			return
366: 		}
367: 
368: 		if b.Time != tm {
369: 			t.Errorf("output[2] time is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", tm, b.Time)
370: 		}
371: 	}
372: 
373: 	input := testSineWaveInput()
374: 
375: 	t.Run("update scalar", func(t *testing.T) {
376: 		t.Parallel()
377: 
378: 		s := entities.Scalar{Time: tm, Value: inp}
379: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
380: 
381: 		for i := 0; i < primeCount; i++ {
382: 			sw.Update(input[i%len(input)])
383: 		}
384: 
385: 		check(sw.UpdateScalar(&s))
386: 	})
387: 
388: 	t.Run("update bar", func(t *testing.T) {
389: 		t.Parallel()
390: 
391: 		b := entities.Bar{Time: tm, High: inp, Low: inp, Close: inp}
392: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
393: 
394: 		for i := 0; i < primeCount; i++ {
395: 			sw.Update(input[i%len(input)])
396: 		}
397: 
398: 		check(sw.UpdateBar(&b))
399: 	})
400: 
401: 	t.Run("update quote", func(t *testing.T) {
402: 		t.Parallel()
403: 
404: 		q := entities.Quote{Time: tm, Bid: inp, Ask: inp}
405: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
406: 
407: 		for i := 0; i < primeCount; i++ {
408: 			sw.Update(input[i%len(input)])
409: 		}
410: 
411: 		check(sw.UpdateQuote(&q))
412: 	})
413: 
414: 	t.Run("update trade", func(t *testing.T) {
415: 		t.Parallel()
416: 
417: 		r := entities.Trade{Time: tm, Price: inp}
418: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
419: 
420: 		for i := 0; i < primeCount; i++ {
421: 			sw.Update(input[i%len(input)])
422: 		}
423: 
424: 		check(sw.UpdateTrade(&r))
425: 	})
426: }
427: 
428: func TestSineWaveBandOrdering(t *testing.T) {
429: 	t.Parallel()
430: 
431: 	sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
432: 	input := testSineWaveInput()
433: 
434: 	tm := testSineWaveTime()
435: 	s := entities.Scalar{Time: tm, Value: input[0]}
436: 
437: 	// Prime the indicator.
438: 	for i := 0; i < 200; i++ {
439: 		sw.Update(input[i%len(input)])
440: 	}
441: 
442: 	out := sw.UpdateScalar(&s)
443: 
444: 	value, ok := out[int(Value)-1].(entities.Scalar)
445: 	if !ok {
446: 		t.Fatalf("output[Value] is not a scalar")
447: 	}
448: 
449: 	lead, ok := out[int(Lead)-1].(entities.Scalar)
450: 	if !ok {
451: 		t.Fatalf("output[Lead] is not a scalar")
452: 	}
453: 
454: 	band, ok := out[int(Band)-1].(outputs.Band)
455: 	if !ok {
456: 		t.Fatalf("output[Band] is not a band")
457: 	}
458: 
459: 	if band.Upper != value.Value {
460: 		t.Errorf("Band.Upper != Value: expected %v, actual %v", value.Value, band.Upper)
461: 	}
462: 
463: 	if band.Lower != lead.Value {
464: 		t.Errorf("Band.Lower != Lead: expected %v, actual %v", lead.Value, band.Lower)
465: 	}
466: }
467: 
468: func TestSineWaveIsPrimed(t *testing.T) {
469: 	t.Parallel()
470: 
471: 	input := testSineWaveInput()
472: 
473: 	t.Run("primes somewhere in the first half of the sequence", func(t *testing.T) {
474: 		t.Parallel()
475: 
476: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
477: 
478: 		if sw.IsPrimed() {
479: 			t.Error("expected not primed at start")
480: 		}
481: 
482: 		primedAt := -1
483: 
484: 		for i := range input {
485: 			sw.Update(input[i])
486: 
487: 			if sw.IsPrimed() && primedAt < 0 {
488: 				primedAt = i
489: 			}
490: 		}
491: 
492: 		if primedAt < 0 {
493: 			t.Error("expected indicator to become primed within the input sequence")
494: 		}
495: 
496: 		if !sw.IsPrimed() {
497: 			t.Error("expected primed at end")
498: 		}
499: 	})
500: }

502: func TestSineWaveMetadata(t *testing.T) { //nolint: funlen
503: 	t.Parallel()
504: 
505: 	check := func(what string, exp, act any) {
506: 		t.Helper()
507: 
508: 		if exp != act {
509: 			t.Errorf("%s is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", what, exp, act)
510: 		}
511: 	}
512: 
513: 	checkInstance := func(act core.Metadata, mnemonic string) {
514: 		const (
515: 			outputLen  = 5
516: 			descrVal   = "Sine wave "
517: 			descrLead  = "Sine wave lead "
518: 			descrBand  = "Sine wave band "
519: 			descrDCP   = "Dominant cycle period "
520: 			descrDCPha = "Dominant cycle phase "
521: 		)
522: 
523: 		mnemonicLead := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "sw-lead(")
524: 		mnemonicBand := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "sw-band(")
525: 		mnemonicDCP := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "dcp(")
526: 		mnemonicDCPha := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "dcph(")
527: 
528: 		check("Type", core.SineWave, act.Type)
529: 		check("Mnemonic", mnemonic, act.Mnemonic)
530: 		check("Description", descrVal+mnemonic, act.Description)
531: 		check("len(Outputs)", outputLen, len(act.Outputs))
532: 
533: 		check("Outputs[0].Kind", int(Value), act.Outputs[0].Kind)
534: 		check("Outputs[0].Type", outputs.ScalarType, act.Outputs[0].Type)
535: 		check("Outputs[0].Mnemonic", mnemonic, act.Outputs[0].Mnemonic)
536: 		check("Outputs[0].Description", descrVal+mnemonic, act.Outputs[0].Description)
537: 
538: 		check("Outputs[1].Kind", int(Lead), act.Outputs[1].Kind)
539: 		check("Outputs[1].Type", outputs.ScalarType, act.Outputs[1].Type)
540: 		check("Outputs[1].Mnemonic", mnemonicLead, act.Outputs[1].Mnemonic)
541: 		check("Outputs[1].Description", descrLead+mnemonicLead, act.Outputs[1].Description)
542: 
543: 		check("Outputs[2].Kind", int(Band), act.Outputs[2].Kind)
544: 		check("Outputs[2].Type", outputs.BandType, act.Outputs[2].Type)
545: 		check("Outputs[2].Mnemonic", mnemonicBand, act.Outputs[2].Mnemonic)
546: 		check("Outputs[2].Description", descrBand+mnemonicBand, act.Outputs[2].Description)
547: 
548: 		check("Outputs[3].Kind", int(DominantCyclePeriod), act.Outputs[3].Kind)
549: 		check("Outputs[3].Type", outputs.ScalarType, act.Outputs[3].Type)
550: 		check("Outputs[3].Mnemonic", mnemonicDCP, act.Outputs[3].Mnemonic)
551: 		check("Outputs[3].Description", descrDCP+mnemonicDCP, act.Outputs[3].Description)
552: 
553: 		check("Outputs[4].Kind", int(DominantCyclePhase), act.Outputs[4].Kind)
554: 		check("Outputs[4].Type", outputs.ScalarType, act.Outputs[4].Type)
555: 		check("Outputs[4].Mnemonic", mnemonicDCPha, act.Outputs[4].Mnemonic)
556: 		check("Outputs[4].Description", descrDCPha+mnemonicDCPha, act.Outputs[4].Description)
557: 	}
558: 
559: 	t.Run("default (α=0.33, BarMedianPrice)", func(t *testing.T) {
560: 		t.Parallel()
561: 
562: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateDefault()
563: 		act := sw.Metadata()
564: 		checkInstance(act, "sw(0.330, hl/2)")
565: 	})
566: 
567: 	t.Run("α=0.5, phase accumulator", func(t *testing.T) {
568: 		t.Parallel()
569: 
570: 		sw := testSineWaveCreateAlpha(0.5, hilberttransformer.PhaseAccumulator)
571: 		act := sw.Metadata()
572: 		checkInstance(act, "sw(0.500, pa(4, 0.200, 0.200), hl/2)")
573: 	})
574: }
575: 
576: func TestNewSineWave(t *testing.T) { //nolint: funlen,maintidx
577: 	t.Parallel()
578: 
579: 	const (
580: 		bc entities.BarComponent   = entities.BarClosePrice
581: 		qc entities.QuoteComponent = entities.QuoteMidPrice
582: 		tc entities.TradeComponent = entities.TradePrice
583: 
584: 		errAlpha = "invalid sine wave parameters: α for additional smoothing should be in range (0, 1]"
585: 		errBC    = "invalid sine wave parameters: invalid dominant cycle parameters: 9999: unknown bar component"
586: 		errQC    = "invalid sine wave parameters: invalid dominant cycle parameters: 9999: unknown quote component"
587: 		errTC    = "invalid sine wave parameters: invalid dominant cycle parameters: 9999: unknown trade component"
588: 	)
589: 
590: 	check := func(name string, exp, act any) {
591: 		t.Helper()
592: 
593: 		if exp != act {
594: 			t.Errorf("%s is incorrect: expected %v, actual %v", name, exp, act)
595: 		}
596: 	}
597: 
598: 	checkInstance := func(sw *SineWave, mnemonic string) {
599: 		const (
600: 			descrVal   = "Sine wave "
601: 			descrLead  = "Sine wave lead "
602: 			descrBand  = "Sine wave band "
603: 			descrDCP   = "Dominant cycle period "
604: 			descrDCPha = "Dominant cycle phase "
605: 		)
606: 
607: 		mnemonicLead := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "sw-lead(")
608: 		mnemonicBand := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "sw-band(")
609: 		mnemonicDCP := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "dcp(")
610: 		mnemonicDCPha := strings.ReplaceAll(mnemonic, "sw(", "dcph(")
611: 
612: 		check("mnemonic", mnemonic, sw.mnemonic)
613: 		check("description", descrVal+mnemonic, sw.description)
614: 		check("mnemonicLead", mnemonicLead, sw.mnemonicLead)
615: 		check("descriptionLead", descrLead+mnemonicLead, sw.descriptionLead)
616: 		check("mnemonicBand", mnemonicBand, sw.mnemonicBand)
617: 		check("descriptionBand", descrBand+mnemonicBand, sw.descriptionBand)
618: 		check("mnemonicDCP", mnemonicDCP, sw.mnemonicDCP)
619: 		check("descriptionDCP", descrDCP+mnemonicDCP, sw.descriptionDCP)
620: 		check("mnemonicDCPhase", mnemonicDCPha, sw.mnemonicDCPhase)
621: 		check("descriptionDCPhase", descrDCPha+mnemonicDCPha, sw.descriptionDCPhase)
622: 		check("primed", false, sw.primed)
623: 		check("dc != nil", true, sw.dc != nil)
624: 		check("barFunc == nil", false, sw.barFunc == nil)
625: 		check("quoteFunc == nil", false, sw.quoteFunc == nil)
626: 		check("tradeFunc == nil", false, sw.tradeFunc == nil)
627: 	}
628: 
629: 	t.Run("default", func(t *testing.T) {
630: 		t.Parallel()
631: 
632: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveDefault()
633: 		check("err == nil", true, err == nil)
634: 		checkInstance(sw, "sw(0.330, hl/2)")
635: 	})
636: 
637: 	t.Run("α=0.5, default estimator, custom components", func(t *testing.T) {
638: 		t.Parallel()
639: 
640: 		params := Params{
641: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.5,
642: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
643: 			EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
644: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: tc,
645: 		}
646: 
647: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
648: 		check("err == nil", true, err == nil)
649: 		checkInstance(sw, "sw(0.500)")
650: 	})
651: 
652: 	t.Run("α=0.5, phase accumulator", func(t *testing.T) {
653: 		t.Parallel()
654: 
655: 		params := Params{
656: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.5,
657: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.PhaseAccumulator,
658: 			EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
659: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: tc,
660: 		}
661: 
662: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
663: 		check("err == nil", true, err == nil)
664: 		checkInstance(sw, "sw(0.500, pa(4, 0.200, 0.200))")
665: 	})
666: 
667: 	t.Run("α ≤ 0, error", func(t *testing.T) {
668: 		t.Parallel()
669: 
670: 		params := Params{
671: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.0,
672: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
673: 			EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
674: 		}
675: 
676: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
677: 		check("sw == nil", true, sw == nil)
678: 		check("err", errAlpha, err.Error())
679: 	})
680: 
681: 	t.Run("α > 1, error", func(t *testing.T) {
682: 		t.Parallel()
683: 
684: 		params := Params{
685: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 1.00000001,
686: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
687: 			EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
688: 		}
689: 
690: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
691: 		check("sw == nil", true, sw == nil)
692: 		check("err", errAlpha, err.Error())
693: 	})
694: 
695: 	t.Run("invalid bar component", func(t *testing.T) {
696: 		t.Parallel()
697: 
698: 		params := Params{
699: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33,
700: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
701: 			EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
702: 			BarComponent:             entities.BarComponent(9999), QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: tc,
703: 		}
704: 
705: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
706: 		check("sw == nil", true, sw == nil)
707: 		check("err", errBC, err.Error())
708: 	})
709: 
710: 	t.Run("invalid quote component", func(t *testing.T) {
711: 		t.Parallel()
712: 
713: 		params := Params{
714: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33,
715: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
716: 			EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
717: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: entities.QuoteComponent(9999), TradeComponent: tc,
718: 		}
719: 
720: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
721: 		check("sw == nil", true, sw == nil)
722: 		check("err", errQC, err.Error())
723: 	})
724: 
725: 	t.Run("invalid trade component", func(t *testing.T) {
726: 		t.Parallel()
727: 
728: 		params := Params{
729: 			AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33,
730: 			EstimatorType:            hilberttransformer.HomodyneDiscriminator,
731: 			EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
732: 			BarComponent:             bc, QuoteComponent: qc, TradeComponent: entities.TradeComponent(9999),
733: 		}
734: 
735: 		sw, err := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
736: 		check("sw == nil", true, sw == nil)
737: 		check("err", errTC, err.Error())
738: 	})
739: }
740: 
741: func testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams() hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams {
742: 	const (
743: 		smoothingLength           = 4
744: 		alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase = 0.2
745: 		alphaEmaPeriod            = 0.2
746: 		warmUpPeriod              = 0
747: 	)
748: 
749: 	return hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams{
750: 		SmoothingLength:           smoothingLength,
751: 		AlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase,
752: 		AlphaEmaPeriod:            alphaEmaPeriod,
753: 		WarmUpPeriod:              warmUpPeriod,
754: 	}
755: }
756: 
757: func testSineWaveCreateDefault() *SineWave {
758: 	sw, _ := NewSineWaveDefault()
759: 
760: 	return sw
761: }
762: 
763: func testSineWaveCreateAlpha(
764: 	alpha float64, estimatorType hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorType,
765: ) *SineWave {
766: 	params := Params{
767: 		AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: alpha,
768: 		EstimatorType:            estimatorType,
769: 		EstimatorParams:          testSineWaveCreateCycleEstimatorParams(),
770: 	}
771: 
772: 	sw, _ := NewSineWaveParams(&params)
773: 
774: 	return sw
775: }
