105: 
106: const tolerance = 1e-4;
107: 
108: describe('SineWave', () => {
109:   const time = new Date(2021, 3, 1);
110:   const skip = 9;
111:   const settleSkip = 177;
112: 
113:   it('should have correct output enum values', () => {
114:     expect(SineWaveOutput.Value).toBe(0);
115:     expect(SineWaveOutput.Lead).toBe(1);
116:     expect(SineWaveOutput.Band).toBe(2);
117:     expect(SineWaveOutput.DominantCyclePeriod).toBe(3);
118:     expect(SineWaveOutput.DominantCyclePhase).toBe(4);
119:   });
120: 
121:   it('should return expected mnemonic for default params (BarComponent.Median)', () => {
122:     const sw = SineWave.default();
123:     expect(sw.metadata().mnemonic).toBe('sw(0.330, hl/2)');
124:   });
125: 
126:   it('should return expected mnemonic for explicit components matching Close default', () => {
127:     const sw = SineWave.fromParams({ alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.5, barComponent: BarComponent.Close });
128:     expect(sw.metadata().mnemonic).toBe('sw(0.500)');
129:   });
130: 
131:   it('should return expected mnemonic for phase accumulator', () => {
132:     const sw = SineWave.fromParams({
133:       alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.5,
134:       estimatorType: HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType.PhaseAccumulator,
135:       estimatorParams: { smoothingLength: 4, alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: 0.2, alphaEmaPeriod: 0.2 },
136:       barComponent: BarComponent.Close,
137:     });
138:     expect(sw.metadata().mnemonic).toBe('sw(0.500, pa(4, 0.200, 0.200))');
139:   });
140: 
141:   it('should return expected metadata', () => {
142:     const sw = SineWave.default();
143:     const meta = sw.metadata();
144:     const mn = 'sw(0.330, hl/2)';
145:     const mnLead = 'sw-lead(0.330, hl/2)';
146:     const mnBand = 'sw-band(0.330, hl/2)';
147:     const mnDCP = 'dcp(0.330, hl/2)';
148:     const mnDCPha = 'dcph(0.330, hl/2)';
149: 
150:     expect(meta.type).toBe(IndicatorType.SineWave);
151:     expect(meta.mnemonic).toBe(mn);
152:     expect(meta.description).toBe('Sine wave ' + mn);
153:     expect(meta.outputs.length).toBe(5);
154: 
155:     expect(meta.outputs[0].kind).toBe(SineWaveOutput.Value);
156:     expect(meta.outputs[0].type).toBe(OutputType.Scalar);
157:     expect(meta.outputs[0].mnemonic).toBe(mn);
158:     expect(meta.outputs[0].description).toBe('Sine wave ' + mn);
159: 
160:     expect(meta.outputs[1].kind).toBe(SineWaveOutput.Lead);
161:     expect(meta.outputs[1].type).toBe(OutputType.Scalar);
162:     expect(meta.outputs[1].mnemonic).toBe(mnLead);
163:     expect(meta.outputs[1].description).toBe('Sine wave lead ' + mnLead);
164: 
165:     expect(meta.outputs[2].kind).toBe(SineWaveOutput.Band);
166:     expect(meta.outputs[2].type).toBe(OutputType.Band);
167:     expect(meta.outputs[2].mnemonic).toBe(mnBand);
168:     expect(meta.outputs[2].description).toBe('Sine wave band ' + mnBand);
169: 
170:     expect(meta.outputs[3].kind).toBe(SineWaveOutput.DominantCyclePeriod);
171:     expect(meta.outputs[3].type).toBe(OutputType.Scalar);
172:     expect(meta.outputs[3].mnemonic).toBe(mnDCP);
173:     expect(meta.outputs[3].description).toBe('Dominant cycle period ' + mnDCP);
174: 
175:     expect(meta.outputs[4].kind).toBe(SineWaveOutput.DominantCyclePhase);
176:     expect(meta.outputs[4].type).toBe(OutputType.Scalar);
177:     expect(meta.outputs[4].mnemonic).toBe(mnDCPha);
178:     expect(meta.outputs[4].description).toBe('Dominant cycle phase ' + mnDCPha);
179:   });
180: 
181:   it('should throw if α is out of range (0, 1]', () => {
182:     expect(() => SineWave.fromParams({ alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0 })).toThrow();
183:     expect(() => SineWave.fromParams({ alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: -0.1 })).toThrow();
184:     expect(() => SineWave.fromParams({ alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 1.0001 })).toThrow();
185:     expect(() => SineWave.fromParams({ alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.5 })).not.toThrow();
186:     expect(() => SineWave.fromParams({ alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 1.0 })).not.toThrow();
187:   });
188: 
189:   it('should return NaN quadruple for NaN input', () => {
190:     const sw = SineWave.default();
191:     const [v, l, p, h] = sw.update(Number.NaN);
192:     expect(Number.isNaN(v)).toBe(true);
193:     expect(Number.isNaN(l)).toBe(true);
194:     expect(Number.isNaN(p)).toBe(true);
195:     expect(Number.isNaN(h)).toBe(true);
196:   });
197: 
198:   it('should match reference sine (MBST SineWaveTest) past settle window', () => {
199:     const sw = SineWave.default();
200:     for (let i = skip; i < input.length; i++) {
201:       const [value] = sw.update(input[i]);
202:       if (Number.isNaN(value) || i < settleSkip) continue;
203:       if (Number.isNaN(expectedSine[i])) continue;
204:       expect(Math.abs(expectedSine[i] - value))
205:         .withContext(`sine[${i}]: expected ${expectedSine[i]}, actual ${value}`)
206:         .toBeLessThan(tolerance);
207:     }
208:   });
209: 
210:   it('should match reference sine lead (MBST SineWaveLeadTest) past settle window', () => {
211:     const sw = SineWave.default();
212:     for (let i = skip; i < input.length; i++) {
213:       const [, lead] = sw.update(input[i]);
214:       if (Number.isNaN(lead) || i < settleSkip) continue;
215:       if (Number.isNaN(expectedSineLead[i])) continue;
216:       expect(Math.abs(expectedSineLead[i] - lead))
217:         .withContext(`sineLead[${i}]: expected ${expectedSineLead[i]}, actual ${lead}`)
218:         .toBeLessThan(tolerance);
219:     }
220:   });
221: 
222:   it('should become primed within the input sequence', () => {
223:     const sw = SineWave.default();
224:     expect(sw.isPrimed()).toBe(false);
225: 
226:     let primedAt = -1;
227:     for (let i = 0; i < input.length; i++) {
228:       sw.update(input[i]);
229:       if (sw.isPrimed() && primedAt < 0) {
230:         primedAt = i;
231:       }
232:     }
233:     expect(primedAt).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);
234:     expect(sw.isPrimed()).toBe(true);
235:   });
236: 
237:   const primeCount = 200;
238:   const value = 100.0;
239: 
240:   function checkOutput(out: any[]): void {
241:     expect(out.length).toBe(5);
242:     // indices 0, 1, 3, 4 are scalars; 2 is a band.
243:     for (const i of [0, 1, 3, 4]) {
244:       const s = out[i] as Scalar;
245:       expect(s.time).toEqual(time);
246:     }
247:     const b = out[2] as Band;
248:     expect(b.time).toEqual(time);
249:   }
250: 
251:   it('should produce 5-element output via updateScalar', () => {
252:     const sw = SineWave.default();
253:     for (let i = 0; i < primeCount; i++) sw.update(input[i % input.length]);
254:     const s = new Scalar();
255:     s.time = time;
256:     s.value = value;
257:     checkOutput(sw.updateScalar(s) as any[]);
258:   });
259: 
260:   it('should produce 5-element output via updateBar', () => {
261:     const sw = SineWave.default();
262:     for (let i = 0; i < primeCount; i++) sw.update(input[i % input.length]);
263:     const bar = new Bar({ time, open: value, high: value, low: value, close: value, volume: 0 });
264:     checkOutput(sw.updateBar(bar) as any[]);
265:   });
266: 
267:   it('should produce 5-element output via updateQuote', () => {
268:     const sw = SineWave.default();
269:     for (let i = 0; i < primeCount; i++) sw.update(input[i % input.length]);
270:     const q = new Quote({ time, bid: value, ask: value, bidSize: 0, askSize: 0 });
271:     checkOutput(sw.updateQuote(q) as any[]);
272:   });
273: 
274:   it('should produce 5-element output via updateTrade', () => {
275:     const sw = SineWave.default();
276:     for (let i = 0; i < primeCount; i++) sw.update(input[i % input.length]);
277:     const t = new Trade({ time, price: value, volume: 0 });
278:     checkOutput(sw.updateTrade(t) as any[]);
279:   });
280: 
281:   it('should order band as {upper: value, lower: lead}', () => {
282:     const sw = SineWave.default();
283:     for (let i = 0; i < primeCount; i++) sw.update(input[i % input.length]);
284:     const s = new Scalar();
285:     s.time = time;
286:     s.value = input[0];
287:     const out = sw.updateScalar(s) as any[];
288:     const sv = out[SineWaveOutput.Value] as Scalar;
289:     const sl = out[SineWaveOutput.Lead] as Scalar;
290:     const band = out[SineWaveOutput.Band] as Band;
291:     expect(band.upper).toBe(sv.value);
292:     expect(band.lower).toBe(sl.value);
293:   });
294: });

(End of file - total 294 lines)
</content>

<content>1: /** Describes a common Hilbert transformer cycle estimator functionality. */
2: export interface HilbertTransformerCycleEstimator {
3:   /** The underlying linear-Weighted Moving Average (WMA) smoothing length. */
4:   readonly smoothingLength: number; // integer
5: 
6:   /** The current WMA-smoothed value used by underlying Hilbert transformer.
7:    * 
8:    * The linear-Weighted Moving Average has a window size of __smoothingLength__.
9:    */
10:   readonly smoothed: number;
11: 
12:   /** The current de-trended value. */
13:   readonly detrended: number;
14: 
15:   /** The current Quadrature component value. */

17: 
18:   /** The current InPhase component value. */
19:   readonly inPhase: number;
20: 
21:   /** The current period value. */
22:   readonly period: number;
23: 
24:   /** The current count value. */
25:   readonly count: number; // integer
26: 
27:   /** Indicates whether an estimator is primed. */
28:   readonly primed: boolean;
29: 
30:   /** The minimal cycle period supported by this Hilbert transformer. */
31:   readonly minPeriod: number; // integer
32: 
33:   /** The maximual cycle period supported by this Hilbert transformer. */
34:   readonly maxPeriod: number; // integer
35: 
36:   /** The value of α (0 < α ≤ 1) used in EMA to smooth the in-phase and quadrature components. */
37:   readonly alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: number;
38: 
39:   /** The value of α (0 < α ≤ 1) used in EMA to smooth the instantaneous period. */
40:   readonly alphaEmaPeriod: number;
41: 
42:   /** The number of updates before the estimator is primed (MaxPeriod * 2 = 100). */
43:   readonly warmUpPeriod: number; // integer
44: 
45:   /** Updates the estimator given the next sample value. */
46:   update(sample: number): void;
47: }

<path>/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/john-ehlers/hilbert-transformer/hilbert-transformer-common.ts</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>1: import { HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorParams } from './hilbert-transformer-cycle-estimator-params';
2: import { HilbertTransformerCycleEstimator } from './hilbert-transformer-cycle-estimator';
3: import { HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType } from './hilbert-transformer-cycle-estimator-type';
4: import { HilbertTransformerHomodyneDiscriminator } from './hilbert-transformer-homodyne-discriminator';
5: import { HilbertTransformerHomodyneDiscriminatorUnrolled } from './hilbert-transformer-homodyne-discriminator-unrolled';
6: import { HilbertTransformerPhaseAccumulator } from './hilbert-transformer-phase-accumulator';
7: import { HilbertTransformerDualDifferentiator } from './hilbert-transformer-dual-differentiator';
8: 
9: export const defaultMinPeriod = 6;
10: export const defaultMaxPeriod = 50;
11: export const htLength = 7;
12: export const quadratureIndex = Math.floor(htLength / 2);
13: 
14: /** Shift all elements to the right and place the new value at index zero. */
15: export function push(array: number[], value: number): void {
16:   for (let i = array.length - 1; i > 0; i--) {
17:     array[i] = array[i - 1];
18:   }
19:   array[0] = value;
20: }
21: 
22: export function correctAmplitude(previousPeriod: number): number {
23:   const a = 0.54;
24:   const b = 0.075;
25:   return a + b * previousPeriod;
26: }
27: 
28: export function ht(array: number[]): number {
29:   const a = 0.0962;
30:   const b = 0.5769;
31:   let value = 0;
32:   value += a * array[0];
33:   value += b * array[2];
34:   value -= b * array[4];
35:   value -= a * array[6];
36:   return value;
37: }
38: 
39: export function adjustPeriod(period: number, periodPrevious: number): number {
40:   const minPreviousPeriodFactor = 0.67;
41:   const maxPreviousPeriodFactor = 1.5;
42: 
43:   let temp = maxPreviousPeriodFactor * periodPrevious;
44:   if (period > temp) {
45:     period = temp;
46:   } else {
47:     temp = minPreviousPeriodFactor * periodPrevious;
48:     if (period < temp)
49:       period = temp;
50:   }
51: 
52:   if (period < defaultMinPeriod)
53:     period = defaultMinPeriod;
54:   else if (period > defaultMaxPeriod)
55:     period = defaultMaxPeriod;
56:   return period;
57: }
58: 
59: export function fillWmaFactors(length: number, factors: number[]): void {
60:   if (length === 4) {
61:     factors[0] = 0.4;
62:     factors[1] = 0.3;
63:     factors[2] = 0.2;
64:     factors[3] = 0.1;
65:   } else if (length === 3) {
66:     factors[0] = 3. / 6.;
67:     factors[1] = 2. / 6.;
68:     factors[2] = 1. / 6.;
69:   } else { // if length === 2
70:     factors[0] = 2. / 3.;
71:     factors[1] = 1. / 3.;
72:   }
73: }
74: 
75: export function verifyParameters(params: HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorParams): string | undefined {
76:   const invalid = "invalid cycle estimator parameters: ";
77:   const minLen = 2;
78:   const maxLen = 4;
79: 
80:   const length = Math.floor(params.smoothingLength);
81:   if (length < minLen || length > maxLen) {
82:     return invalid + "smoothingLength should be in range [2, 4]";
83:   }
84: 
85:   const alphaQuad = params.alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase;
86:   if (alphaQuad <= 0 || alphaQuad >= 1) {
87:     return invalid + "alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase should be in range (0, 1)";
88:   }
89: 
90:   const alphaPeriod = params.alphaEmaPeriod;
91:   if (alphaPeriod <= 0 || alphaPeriod >= 1) {
92:     return invalid + "alphaEmaPeriod should be in range (0, 1)";
93:   }
94: 
95:   return undefined;
96: }
97: 
98: /** Returns the moniker of the cycle estimator in the form `hd(4, 0.200, 0.200)`. */
99: export function estimatorMoniker(
100:   estimatorType: HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType,
101:   estimator: HilbertTransformerCycleEstimator): string {
102:   const namer = (s: string, e: HilbertTransformerCycleEstimator): string =>
103:     `${s}(${e.smoothingLength}, ${e.alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase.toFixed(3)}, ${e.alphaEmaPeriod.toFixed(3)})`;
104: 
105:   switch (estimatorType) {
106:     case HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType.HomodyneDiscriminator:
107:       return namer('hd', estimator);
108:     case HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType.HomodyneDiscriminatorUnrolled:
109:       return namer('hdu', estimator);
110:     case HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType.PhaseAccumulator:
111:       return namer('pa', estimator);
112:     case HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType.DualDifferentiator:
113:       return namer('dd', estimator);
114:     default:
115:       return '';
116:   }
117: }
118: 
119: export function createEstimator(
120:   estimatorType?: HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType,
121:   estimatorParams?: HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorParams): HilbertTransformerCycleEstimator {
122: 
123:   if (estimatorType === undefined) {
124:     estimatorType = HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType.HomodyneDiscriminator;
125:   }
126: 
127:   switch (estimatorType) {
128:     case HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType.HomodyneDiscriminator:
129:       if (estimatorParams === undefined) {
130:         estimatorParams = {
131:           smoothingLength: 4,
132:           alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: 0.2,
133:           alphaEmaPeriod: 0.2
134:         };
135:       }
136:       return new HilbertTransformerHomodyneDiscriminator(estimatorParams);
137:     case HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType.HomodyneDiscriminatorUnrolled:
138:       if (estimatorParams === undefined) {
139:         estimatorParams = {
140:           smoothingLength: 4,
141:           alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: 0.2,
142:           alphaEmaPeriod: 0.2
143:         };
144:       }
145:       return new HilbertTransformerHomodyneDiscriminatorUnrolled(estimatorParams);
146:     case HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType.PhaseAccumulator:
147:       if (estimatorParams === undefined) {
148:         estimatorParams = {
149:           smoothingLength: 4,
150:           alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: 0.15,
151:           alphaEmaPeriod: 0.25
152:         };
153:       }
154:       return new HilbertTransformerPhaseAccumulator(estimatorParams);
155:     case HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType.DualDifferentiator:
156:       if (estimatorParams === undefined) {
157:         estimatorParams = {
158:           smoothingLength: 4,
159:           alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: 0.15,
160:           alphaEmaPeriod: 0.15
161:         };
162:       }
163:       return new HilbertTransformerDualDifferentiator(estimatorParams);
164:     default:
165:       throw new Error("Invalid cycle estimator type: " + estimatorType);
166:   }
167: }

(End of file - total 167 lines)
</content>

{ DefaultQuoteComponent, quoteComponentValue } from '../../../entities/quote-component';\nimport { Scalar } from '../../../entities/scalar';\nimport { Trade } from '../../../entities/trade';\nimport { DefaultTradeComponent, tradeComponentValue } from '../../../entities/trade-component';\nimport { componentTripleMnemonic } from '../../core/component-triple-mnemonic';\nimport { Indicator } from '../../core/indicator';\nimport { IndicatorMetadata } from '../../core/indicator-metadata';\nimport { IndicatorOutput } from '../../core/indicator-output';\nimport { IndicatorType } from '../../core/indicator-type';\nimport { OutputType } from '../../core/outputs/output-type';\nimport { HilbertTransformerCycleEstimator } from '../hilbert-transformer/hilbert-transformer-cycle-estimator';\nimport { HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType } from '../hilbert-transformer/hilbert-transformer-cycle-estimator-type';\nimport { createEstimator, estimatorMoniker } from '../hilbert-transformer/hilbert-transformer-common';\nimport { HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineOutput } from './hilbert-transformer-instantaneous-trend-line-output';\nimport { HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineParams } from './hilbert-transformer-instantaneous-trend-line-params';\n\nconst DEFAULT_ALPHA_EMA_PERIOD_ADDITIONAL = 0.33;\nconst DEFAULT_SMOOTHING_LENGTH = 4;\nconst DEFAULT_ALPHA_EMA_QI = 0.2;\nconst DEFAULT_ALPHA_EMA_PERIOD = 0.2;\n// MBST's DominantCyclePeriod default warm-up is MaxPeriod * 2 = 100.\nconst DEFAULT_WARM_UP_PERIOD = 100;\nconst DEFAULT_TREND_LINE_SMOOTHING_LENGTH = 4;\nconst DEFAULT_CYCLE_PART_MULTIPLIER = 1.0;\nconst MAX_CYCLE_PART_MULTIPLIER = 10.0;\n\n/** __Hilbert Transformer Instantaneous Trend Line__ (Ehlers) computes a smoothed trend line\n * derived from averaging over a window whose length tracks the smoothed dominant cycle period\n * produced by a Hilbert transformer cycle estimator.\n *\n * It exposes two outputs:\n *\n *\t- Value: the instantaneous trend line value, a length-`trendLineSmoothingLength` WMA of\n *\t  simple averages over the last `round(smoothedPeriod·cyclePartMultiplier)` raw input samples.\n *\t- DominantCyclePeriod: the additionally EMA-smoothed dominant cycle period.\n *\n * Reference:\n *\n *\tJohn Ehlers, Rocket Science for Traders, Wiley, 2001, 0471405671, pp 107-112.\n */\nexport class HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine implements Indicator {\n  private readonly htce: HilbertTransformerCycleEstimator;\n  private readonly alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: number;\n  private readonly oneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: number;\n  private readonly cyclePartMultiplier: number;\n  private readonly trendLineSmoothingLength: number;\n  private readonly coeff0: number;\n  private readonly coeff1: number;\n  private readonly coeff2: number;\n  private readonly coeff3: number;\n  private readonly input: number[];\n  private readonly inputLength: number;\n  private readonly inputLengthMin1: number;\n  private smoothedPeriod = 0;\n  private value = Number.NaN;\n  private average1 = 0;\n  private average2 = 0;\n  private average3 = 0;\n  private primed = false;\n\n  private readonly mnemonicValue: string;\n  private readonly descriptionValue: string;\n  private readonly mnemonicDCP: string;\n  private readonly descriptionDCP: string;\n\n  private readonly barComponentFunc: (bar: Bar) => number;\n  private readonly quoteComponentFunc: (quote: Quote) => number;\n  private readonly tradeComponentFunc: (trade: Trade) => number;\n\n  /** Creates an instance using default parameters (α=0.33, trendLineSmoothingLength=4,\n   * cyclePartMultiplier=1.0, HomodyneDiscriminator cycle estimator with smoothingLength=4,\n   * αq=0.2, αp=0.2, warmUpPeriod=100, BarComponent.Median). */\n  public static default(): HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine {\n    return new HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine({\n      alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: DEFAULT_ALPHA_EMA_PERIOD_ADDITIONAL,\n      trendLineSmoothingLength: DEFAULT_TREND_LINE_SMOOTHING_LENGTH,\n      cyclePartMultiplier: DEFAULT_CYCLE_PART_MULTIPLIER,\n      estimatorType: HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType.HomodyneDiscriminator,\n      estimatorParams: {\n        smoothingLength: DEFAULT_SMOOTHING_LENGTH,\n        alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: DEFAULT_ALPHA_EMA_QI,\n        alphaEmaPeriod: DEFAULT_ALPHA_EMA_PERIOD,\n        warmUpPeriod: DEFAULT_WARM_UP_PERIOD,\n      },\n    });\n  }\n\n  /** Creates an instance based on the given parameters. */\n  public static fromParams(\n    params: HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineParams,\n  ): HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine {\n    return new HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine(params);\n  }\n\n  private constructor(params: HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineParams) {\n    const alpha = params.alphaEmaPeriodAdditional;\n    if (alpha <= 0 || alpha > 1) {\n      throw new Error(\n        'invalid hilbert transformer instantaneous trend line parameters: '\n          + 'α for additional smoothing should be in range (0, 1]',\n      );\n    }\n\n    const tlsl = params.trendLineSmoothingLength ?? DEFAULT_TREND_LINE_SMOOTHING_LENGTH;\n    if (tlsl < 2 || tlsl > 4 || !Number.isInteger(tlsl)) {\n      throw new Error(\n        'invalid hilbert transformer instantaneous trend line parameters: '\n          + 'trend line smoothing length should be 2, 3, or 4',\n      );\n    }\n\n    const cpm = params.cyclePartMultiplier ?? DEFAULT_CYCLE_PART_MULTIPLIER;\n    if (cpm <= 0 || cpm > MAX_CYCLE_PART_MULTIPLIER) {\n      throw new Error(\n        'invalid hilbert transformer instantaneous trend line parameters: '\n          + 'cycle part multiplier should be in range (0, 10]',\n      );\n    }\n\n    this.alphaEmaPeriodAdditional = alpha;\n    this.oneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional = 1 - alpha;\n    this.cyclePartMultiplier = cpm;\n    this.trendLineSmoothingLength = tlsl;\n\n    // Default to BarComponent.Median (MBST default; always shown in mnemonic).\n    const bc = params.barComponent ?? BarComponent.Median;\n    const qc = params.quoteComponent ?? DefaultQuoteComponent;\n    const tc = params.tradeComponent ?? DefaultTradeComponent;\n\n    this.barComponentFunc = barComponentValue(bc);\n    this.quoteComponentFunc = quoteComponentValue(qc);\n    this.tradeComponentFunc = tradeComponentValue(tc);\n\n    this.htce = createEstimator(params.estimatorType, params.estimatorParams);\n\n    const effectiveType = params.estimatorType ?? HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType.HomodyneDiscriminator;\n    let em = '';\n    const isDefaultHd = effectiveType === HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType.HomodyneDiscriminator\n      && this.htce.smoothingLength === DEFAULT_SMOOTHING_LENGTH\n      && this.htce.alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase === DEFAULT_ALPHA_EMA_QI\n      && this.htce.alphaEmaPeriod === DEFAULT_ALPHA_EMA_PERIOD;\n    if (!isDefaultHd) {\n      const moniker = estimatorMoniker(effectiveType, this.htce);\n      if (moniker.length > 0) {\n        em = ', ' + moniker;\n      }\n    }\n\n    const cm = componentTripleMnemonic(bc, qc, tc);\n    const a = alpha.toFixed(3);\n    const c = cpm.toFixed(3);\n\n    this.mnemonicValue = `htitl(${a}, ${tlsl}, ${c}${em}${cm})`;\n    this.mnemonicDCP = `dcp(${a}${em}${cm})`;\n\n    this.descriptionValue = 'Hilbert transformer instantaneous trend line ' + this.mnemonicValue;\n    this.descriptionDCP = 'Dominant cycle period ' + this.mnemonicDCP;\n\n    // WMA coefficients.\n    let c0 = 0, c1 = 0, c2 = 0, c3 = 0;\n    if (tlsl === 2) {\n      c0 = 2 / 3;\n      c1 = 1 / 3;\n    } else if (tlsl === 3) {\n      c0 = 3 / 6;\n      c1 = 2 / 6;\n      c2 = 1 / 6;\n    } else { // tlsl === 4\n      c0 = 4 / 10;\n      c1 = 3 / 10;\n      c2 = 2 / 10;\n      c3 = 1 / 10;\n    }\n    this.coeff0 = c0;\n    this.coeff1 = c1;\n    this.coeff2 = c2;\n    this.coeff3 = c3;\n\n    const maxPeriod = this.htce.maxPeriod;\n    this.input = new Array<number>(maxPeriod).fill(0);\n    this.inputLength = maxPeriod;\n    this.inputLengthMin1 = maxPeriod - 1;\n  }\n\n  /** Indicates whether the indicator is primed. */\n  public isPrimed(): boolean { return this.primed; }\n\n  /** Describes the output data of the indicator. */\n  public metadata(): IndicatorMetadata {\n    return {\n      type: IndicatorType.HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine,\n      mnemonic: this.mnemonicValue,\n      description: this.descriptionValue,\n      outputs: [\n        {\n          kind: HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineOutput.Value,\n          type: OutputType.Scalar,\n          mnemonic: this.mnemonicValue,\n          description: this.descriptionValue,\n        },\n        {\n          kind: HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineOutput.DominantCyclePeriod,\n          type: OutputType.Scalar,\n          mnemonic: this.mnemonicDCP,\n          description: this.descriptionDCP,\n        },\n      ],\n    };\n  }\n\n  /** Updates the indicator given the next sample value. Returns the pair\n   * (value, period). Returns (NaN, NaN) if not yet primed. */\n  public update(sample: number): [number, number] {\n    if (Number.isNaN(sample)) {\n      return [sample, sample];\n    }\n\n    this.htce.update(sample);\n    this.pushInput(sample);\n\n    if (this.primed) {\n      this.smoothedPeriod = this.alphaEmaPeriodAdditional * this.htce.period\n        + this.oneMinAlphaEmaPeriodAdditional * this.smoothedPeriod;\n      const average = this.calculateAverage();\n      this.value = this.coeff0 * average\n        + this.coeff1 * this.average1\n        + this.coeff2 * this.average2\n        + this.coeff3 * this.average3;\n      this.average3 = this.average2;\n      this.average2 = this.average1;\n      this.average1 = average;\n      return [this.value, this.smoothedPeriod];\n    }\n\n    if (this.htce.primed) {\n      this.primed = true;\n      this.smoothedPeriod = this.htce.period;\n      const avg = this.calculateAverage();\n      this.value = avg;\n      this.average1 = avg;\n      this.average2 = avg;\n      this.average3 = avg;\n      return [this.value, this.smoothedPeriod];\n    }\n\n    return [Number.NaN, Number.NaN];\n  }\n\n  /** Updates an indicator given the next scalar sample. */\n  public updateScalar(sample: Scalar): IndicatorOutput {\n    return this.updateEntity(sample.time, sample.value);\n  }\n\n  /** Updates an indicator given the next bar sample. */\n  public updateBar(sample: Bar): IndicatorOutput {\n    return this.updateEntity(sample.time, this.barComponentFunc(sample));\n  }\n\n  /** Updates an indicator given the next quote sample. */\n  public updateQuote(sample: Quote): IndicatorOutput {\n    return this.updateEntity(sample.time, this.quoteComponentFunc(sample));\n  }\n\n  /** Updates an indicator given the next trade sample. */\n  public updateTrade(sample: Trade): IndicatorOutput {\n    return this.updateEntity(sample.time, this.tradeComponentFunc(sample));\n  }\n\n  private updateEntity(time: Date, sample: number): IndicatorOutput {\n    const [value, period] = this.update(sample);\n\n    const sv = new Scalar();\n    sv.time = time;\n    sv.value = value;\n\n    const sp = new Scalar();\n    sp.time = time;\n    sp.value = period;\n\n    return [sv, sp];\n  }\n\n  private pushInput(value: number): void {\n    for (let i = this.inputLengthMin1; i > 0; i--) {\n      this.input[i] = this.input[i - 1];\n    }\n    this.input[0] = value;\n  }\n\n  private calculateAverage(): number {\n    // Compute simple average over a window tracking the smoothed dominant cycle period.\n    let length = Math.floor(this.smoothedPeriod * this.cyclePartMultiplier + 0.5);\n    if (length > this.inputLength) {\n      length = this.inputLength;\n    } else if (length < 1) {\n      length = 1;\n    }\n\n    let sum = 0;\n    for (let i = 0; i < length; i++) {\n      sum += this.input[i];\n    }\n    return sum / length;\n  }\n}\n",

109.6873409090910, 110.1830037593980, 110.4551917293230,\n];\n\nconst tolerance = 1e-4;\n\ndescribe('HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine', () => {\n  const time = new Date(2021, 3, 1);\n  const skip = 9;\n  const settleSkip = 177;\n\n  it('should have correct output enum values', () => {\n    expect(HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineOutput.Value).toBe(0);\n    expect(HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineOutput.DominantCyclePeriod).toBe(1);\n  });\n\n  it('should return expected mnemonic for default params (BarComponent.Median)', () => {\n    const x = HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.default();\n    expect(x.metadata().mnemonic).toBe('htitl(0.330, 4, 1.000, hl/2)');\n  });\n\n  it('should return expected mnemonic for tlsl=2 with Close component', () => {\n    const x = HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({\n      alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33,\n      trendLineSmoothingLength: 2,\n      cyclePartMultiplier: 1.0,\n      barComponent: BarComponent.Close,\n    });\n    expect(x.metadata().mnemonic).toBe('htitl(0.330, 2, 1.000)');\n  });\n\n  it('should return expected mnemonic for phase accumulator', () => {\n    const x = HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({\n      alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.5,\n      trendLineSmoothingLength: 3,\n      cyclePartMultiplier: 0.5,\n      estimatorType: HilbertTransformerCycleEstimatorType.PhaseAccumulator,\n      estimatorParams: { smoothingLength: 4, alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: 0.2, alphaEmaPeriod: 0.2 },\n      barComponent: BarComponent.Close,\n    });\n    expect(x.metadata().mnemonic).toBe('htitl(0.500, 3, 0.500, pa(4, 0.200, 0.200))');\n  });\n\n  it('should return expected metadata', () => {\n    const x = HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.default();\n    const meta = x.metadata();\n    const mn = 'htitl(0.330, 4, 1.000, hl/2)';\n    const mnDCP = 'dcp(0.330, hl/2)';\n\n    expect(meta.type).toBe(IndicatorType.HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine);\n    expect(meta.mnemonic).toBe(mn);\n    expect(meta.description).toBe('Hilbert transformer instantaneous trend line ' + mn);\n    expect(meta.outputs.length).toBe(2);\n\n    expect(meta.outputs[0].kind).toBe(HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineOutput.Value);\n    expect(meta.outputs[0].type).toBe(OutputType.Scalar);\n    expect(meta.outputs[0].mnemonic).toBe(mn);\n    expect(meta.outputs[0].description).toBe('Hilbert transformer instantaneous trend line ' + mn);\n\n    expect(meta.outputs[1].kind).toBe(HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLineOutput.DominantCyclePeriod);\n    expect(meta.outputs[1].type).toBe(OutputType.Scalar);\n    expect(meta.outputs[1].mnemonic).toBe(mnDCP);\n    expect(meta.outputs[1].description).toBe('Dominant cycle period ' + mnDCP);\n  });\n\n  it('should throw if α is out of range (0, 1]', () => {\n    expect(() => HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({ alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0 })).toThrow();\n    expect(() => HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({ alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: -0.1 })).toThrow();\n    expect(() => HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({ alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 1.0001 })).toThrow();\n    expect(() => HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({ alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.5 })).not.toThrow();\n    expect(() => HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({ alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 1.0 })).not.toThrow();\n  });\n\n  it('should throw if trendLineSmoothingLength is out of range [2, 4]', () => {\n    expect(() => HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({\n      alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33, trendLineSmoothingLength: 1,\n    })).toThrow();\n    expect(() => HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({\n      alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33, trendLineSmoothingLength: 5,\n    })).toThrow();\n    expect(() => HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({\n      alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33, trendLineSmoothingLength: 2,\n    })).not.toThrow();\n    expect(() => HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({\n      alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33, trendLineSmoothingLength: 3,\n    })).not.toThrow();\n    expect(() => HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({\n      alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33, trendLineSmoothingLength: 4,\n    })).not.toThrow();\n  });\n\n  it('should throw if cyclePartMultiplier is out of range (0, 10]', () => {\n    expect(() => HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({\n      alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33, cyclePartMultiplier: 0,\n    })).toThrow();\n    expect(() => HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({\n      alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33, cyclePartMultiplier: -1,\n    })).toThrow();\n    expect(() => HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({\n      alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33, cyclePartMultiplier: 10.0001,\n    })).toThrow();\n    expect(() => HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.fromParams({\n      alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: 0.33, cyclePartMultiplier: 10.0,\n    })).not.toThrow();\n  });\n\n  it('should return NaN pair for NaN input', () => {\n    const x = HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.default();\n    const [v, p] = x.update(Number.NaN);\n    expect(Number.isNaN(v)).toBe(true);\n    expect(Number.isNaN(p)).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  it('should match reference value (MBST InstantaneousTrendLineTest) past settle window', () => {\n    const x = HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.default();\n    for (let i = skip; i < input.length; i++) {\n      const [value] = x.update(input[i]);\n      if (Number.isNaN(value) || i < settleSkip) continue;\n      if (Number.isNaN(expectedValue[i])) continue;\n      expect(Math.abs(expectedValue[i] - value))\n        .withContext(`value[${i}]: expected ${expectedValue[i]}, actual ${value}`)\n        .toBeLessThan(tolerance);\n    }\n  });\n\n  it('should match reference period (test_MAMA.xsl) past settle window', () => {\n    const x = HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.default();\n    for (let i = skip; i < input.length; i++) {\n      const [, period] = x.update(input[i]);\n      if (Number.isNaN(period) || i < settleSkip) continue;\n      expect(Math.abs(expectedPeriod[i] - period))\n        .withContext(`period[${i}]: expected ${expectedPeriod[i]}, actual ${period}`)\n        .toBeLessThan(tolerance);\n    }\n  });\n\n  it('should become primed within the input sequence', () => {\n    const x = HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.default();\n    expect(x.isPrimed()).toBe(false);\n\n    let primedAt = -1;\n    for (let i = 0; i < input.length; i++) {\n      x.update(input[i]);\n      if (x.isPrimed() && primedAt < 0) {\n        primedAt = i;\n      }\n    }\n    expect(primedAt).toBeGreaterThanOrEqual(0);\n    expect(x.isPrimed()).toBe(true);\n  });\n\n  const primeCount = 200;\n  const value = 100.0;\n\n  function checkOutput(out: any[]): void {\n    expect(out.length).toBe(2);\n    for (let i = 0; i < 2; i++) {\n      const s = out[i] as Scalar;\n      expect(s.time).toEqual(time);\n    }\n  }\n\n  it('should produce 2-element output via updateScalar', () => {\n    const x = HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.default();\n    for (let i = 0; i < primeCount; i++) x.update(input[i % input.length]);\n    const s = new Scalar();\n    s.time = time;\n    s.value = value;\n    checkOutput(x.updateScalar(s) as any[]);\n  });\n\n  it('should produce 2-element output via updateBar', () => {\n    const x = HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.default();\n    for (let i = 0; i < primeCount; i++) x.update(input[i % input.length]);\n    const bar = new Bar({ time, open: value, high: value, low: value, close: value, volume: 0 });\n    checkOutput(x.updateBar(bar) as any[]);\n  });\n\n  it('should produce 2-element output via updateQuote', () => {\n    const x = HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.default();\n    for (let i = 0; i < primeCount; i++) x.update(input[i % input.length]);\n    const q = new Quote({ time, bid: value, ask: value, bidSize: 0, askSize: 0 });\n    checkOutput(x.updateQuote(q) as any[]);\n  });\n\n  it('should produce 2-element output via updateTrade', () => {\n    const x = HilbertTransformerInstantaneousTrendLine.default();\n    for (let i = 0; i < primeCount; i++) x.update(input[i % input.length]);\n    const t = new Trade({ time, price: value, volume: 0 });\n    checkOutput(x.updateTrade(t) as any[]);\n  });\n});\n",
  "filePath": "/home/dev/zpano/ts/indicators/john-ehlers/hilbert-transformer-instantaneous-trend-line/hilbert-transformer-instantaneous-trend-line.spec.ts"
}

- Created `hilbert-transformer-instantaneous-trend-line.ts` — uses raw HTCE directly via `createEstimator()` (not DC), α-EMA period smoothing inline, 4-tap WMA over circular buffer of raw inputs, `BarComponent.Median` default
- Created `hilbert-transformer-instantaneous-trend-line.spec.ts` — 16 specs covering output enum, mnemonic (default/tlsl=2/PA), metadata, param validation (α/tlsl/cpm), NaN, reference value+period with `skip=9, settleSkip=177, tolerance=1e-4`, priming, 4-entity update paths

**Results:** 16/16 Jasmine specs pass. Go tests already passing from previous step. HTITL port is complete in both Go and TS.

### Optional follow-up
- Update skill (`indicator-architecture` or `mbst-indicator-conversion`) to document the new pattern: "indicator composing raw HTCE directly rather than DC, when MBST pushes raw (not WMA-smoothed) inputs into its averaging buffer" — would benefit future MBST ports if similar indicators come up. Want me to update the skill, or stop here?

<content>1: ---
2: name: mbst-indicator-conversion
3: description: Step-by-step guide for converting MBST C# indicators to zpano Go and TypeScript. Load when converting an MBST indicator or understanding the mapping between MBST and zpano patterns.
4: ---
5: 
6: # Converting MBST C# Indicators to Zpano
7: 
8: This guide provides recipes and tips for converting indicators from the MBST C# codebase
9: (`Mbst.Trading.Indicators`) to the zpano multi-language library (Go and TypeScript).
10: 
11: Load the `indicator-architecture` and `indicator-conversion` skills alongside this one
12: for the full zpano architecture reference and internal conversion patterns.
13: 
14: Load the `mbst-indicator-architecture` skill for the full MBST type hierarchy reference.
15: 
16: ---
17: 
18: ## Table of Contents
19: 
20: 1. [Overview](#overview)
21: 2. [Determine the Indicator Pattern](#determine-the-indicator-pattern)
22: 3. [Component Mapping](#component-mapping)
23: 4. [Constructor Conversion](#constructor-conversion)
24: 5. [Algorithm Conversion](#algorithm-conversion)
25: 6. [Multi-Output Indicators](#multi-output-indicators)
26: 7. [Metadata Conversion](#metadata-conversion)
27: 8. [What to Drop](#what-to-drop)
28: 9. [Test Conversion](#test-conversion)
29: 10. [Worked Example: CenterOfGravityOscillator](#worked-example-centerofgravityoscillator)
30: 
31: ---
32: 
33: ## Overview
34: 
35: MBST indicators live in `mbst-to-convert/<author>/<indicator>/` as C# classes.
36: Each indicator has a `.cs` implementation and a `Test.cs` test file.
37: 
38: The conversion produces:
39: - **Go:** `go/indicators/<author>/<indicator>/` (5 files: params, output, output_test, impl, impl_test)
40: - **TS:** `ts/indicators/<author>/<indicator>/` (4 files: params, output, impl, impl.spec)
41: 
42: Always convert Go first, then TypeScript.
43: 
44: ---
45: 
46: ## Determine the Indicator Pattern
47: 
48: Read the MBST source and identify which base class/interface the indicator uses:
49: 
50: | MBST Pattern | Zpano Pattern |
51: |---|---|
52: | `class X : LineIndicator` | Single output. Use `LineIndicator` embedding (Go) / `extends LineIndicator` (TS). Only implement `Update(float64)` / `update(number)`. |
53: | `class X : Indicator, ILineIndicator` | Multi-output (e.g., value + trigger). Implement `Indicator` interface directly (Go) / `implements Indicator` (TS). Must write `UpdateScalar/Bar/Quote/Trade` manually. |
54: | `class X : BandIndicator` | Band output. Map to zpano's band indicator pattern. |
55: | `class X : Indicator, IBandIndicator` | Custom band output. Implement `Indicator` interface directly. |
56: | `class X : ... IHeatmapIndicator` | Heatmap output. Handle case-by-case. |
57: 
58: ### How to tell if it's multi-output
59: 
60: Look for:
61: 1. **Facade properties** (`ValueFacade`, `TriggerFacade`, etc.) — the indicator exposes
62:    multiple outputs via facades.
63: 2. **Multiple named value fields** (e.g., `value` + `valuePrevious` both exposed publicly).
64: 3. **The class extends `Indicator` directly** (not `LineIndicator`) but implements
65:    `ILineIndicator` — this is a strong signal of multi-output.
66: 
67: **Exception:** Some MBST indicators use `: Indicator, ILineIndicator` but only have a single
68: output (`Value`). Example: `SuperSmoother` has this signature but no facades or additional
69: outputs. In this case, use `LineIndicator` embedding/inheritance in zpano (not the direct
70: `Indicator` interface). Always check the actual outputs — the class hierarchy alone is not
71: sufficient to determine multi-output vs single-output.
72: 
73: If multi-output: use the direct `Indicator` interface approach (like FRAMA, CoG).
74: If single-output: use `LineIndicator` embedding/inheritance.
75: 
76: ---
77: 
78: ## Component Mapping
79: 
80: ### OhlcvComponent to Component Triple
81: 
82: MBST uses a single `OhlcvComponent` for OHLCV bars only. Zpano uses three separate
83: component types: `BarComponent`, `QuoteComponent`, `TradeComponent`.
84: 
85: | MBST `OhlcvComponent` | Zpano `BarComponent` | Go Constant | TS Enum |
86: |---|---|---|---|
87: | `ClosingPrice` (default) | `Close` (default) | `entities.BarClosePrice` | `BarComponent.Close` |
88: | `OpeningPrice` | `Open` | `entities.BarOpenPrice` | `BarComponent.Open` |
89: | `HighPrice` | `High` | `entities.BarHighPrice` | `BarComponent.High` |
90: | `LowPrice` | `Low` | `entities.BarLowPrice` | `BarComponent.Low` |
91: | `MedianPrice` | `Median` | `entities.BarMedianPrice` | `BarComponent.Median` |
92: | `TypicalPrice` | `Typical` | `entities.BarTypicalPrice` | `BarComponent.Typical` |
93: | `WeightedPrice` | `Weighted` | `entities.BarWeightedPrice` | `BarComponent.Weighted` |
94: | `Volume` | `Volume` | `entities.BarVolume` | `BarComponent.Volume` |
95: 
96: ### Default Component Handling
97: 
98: - **MBST default:** `OhlcvComponent.ClosingPrice` — set in `Indicator` base class constructor.
99: - **Zpano default:** `BarClosePrice` / `BarComponent.Close` — aka `DefaultBarComponent`.
100: - **Some indicators override the default** (e.g., CoG defaults to `MedianPrice` instead of
101:   `ClosingPrice`). Check the MBST constructor signature for non-default values.
102: 
103: When converting, QuoteComponent and TradeComponent are **always new** (MBST doesn't have
104: them). Use the zpano defaults (`DefaultQuoteComponent`, `DefaultTradeComponent`) when the
105: params zero-value/undefined is provided.
106: 
107: ### Non-Default Bar Component Rule
108: 
109: If the MBST indicator uses a non-default `OhlcvComponent` (anything other than
110: `ClosingPrice`), the zpano constructor must resolve the zero-value to that component
111: instead of `DefaultBarComponent`. This causes `ComponentTripleMnemonic` to include the
112: component in the mnemonic even for default parameters.
113: 
114: **Go example (CoG defaults to MedianPrice):**
115: ```go
116: bc := params.BarComponent
117: if bc == 0 {
118:     bc = entities.BarMedianPrice  // NOT entities.DefaultBarComponent
119: }
120: ```
121: 
122: **TS example:**
123: ```typescript
124: const bc = params.barComponent ?? BarComponent.Median;  // NOT DefaultBarComponent
125: ```
126: 
127: ---
128: 
129: ## Constructor Conversion
130: 
131: ### MBST Constructor Pattern
132: 
133: ```csharp
134: public CenterOfGravityOscillator(int length = 10,
135:     OhlcvComponent ohlcvComponent = OhlcvComponent.MedianPrice)
136:     : base(cog, cogFull, ohlcvComponent)
137: {
138:     if (1 > length) throw new ArgumentOutOfRangeException(argumentLength);
139:     this.length = length;
140:     // ...
141:     moniker = string.Concat(cog, "(", length.ToString(...), ")");
142: }
143: ```
144: 
145: ### Zpano Conversion Rules
146: 
147: 1. **Parameters become a struct/interface** — not individual constructor args.
148: 2. **Validation stays** — translate `ArgumentOutOfRangeException` to `error` (Go) / `throw Error` (TS).
149: 3. **`moniker` becomes `mnemonic`** — built using `ComponentTripleMnemonic` for the triple.
150: 4. **`description` is auto-generated** — typically `"<Full Name> " + mnemonic`.
151: 5. **`base(name, description, component)` call is dropped** — zpano doesn't have a base
152:    class constructor chain for this.
153: 6. **Component function resolution** — create `barFunc`/`quoteFunc`/`tradeFunc` in constructor.
154: 
155: ---
156: 
157: ## Algorithm Conversion
158: 
159: ### The Only Method That Matters
160: 
161: In MBST, `Update(double sample)` contains the entire algorithm. All other `Update`
162: overloads (`Update(Scalar)`, `Update(Ohlcv)`) are pure delegation boilerplate.
163: 
164: **Only convert `Update(double sample)`.** The entity-level update methods are generated
165: by `LineIndicator` embedding (for single-output) or written manually using a simple
166: `updateEntity` helper (for multi-output).
167: 
168: ### C# to Go/TS Algorithm Translation
169: 
170: | C# | Go | TS |
171: |---|---|---|
172: | `double.NaN` | `math.NaN()` | `Number.NaN` |
173: | `double.IsNaN(x)` | `math.IsNaN(x)` | `Number.isNaN(x)` |
174: | `double.Epsilon` | `math.SmallestNonzeroFloat64` | `Number.MIN_VALUE` |
175: | `Math.Abs(x)` | `math.Abs(x)` | `Math.abs(x)` |
176: | `Array.Copy(src, srcIdx, dst, dstIdx, len)` | Manual loop or `copy()` | Manual loop |
177: | `lock (updateLock) { ... }` | `s.mu.Lock(); defer s.mu.Unlock()` | Drop (single-threaded) |
178: 
179: ### Priming Logic
180: 
181: MBST `primed` field is set inside `Update()` after enough samples.
182: Convert directly — zpano uses the same `primed` boolean pattern.
183: 
184: ### NaN Guard
185: 
186: MBST typically has `if (double.IsNaN(sample)) return sample;` at the top of `Update`.
187: Preserve this — it's important for correctness.
188: 
189: ---
190: 
191: ## Multi-Output Indicators
192: 
193: ### MBST: Facade Pattern
194: 
195: MBST exposes multiple outputs via facade classes:
196: 
197: ```csharp
198: // In MBST indicator:
199: public LineIndicatorFacade ValueFacade =>
200:     new LineIndicatorFacade(cog, moniker, cogFull, () => IsPrimed, () => Value);
201: public LineIndicatorFacade TriggerFacade =>
202:     new LineIndicatorFacade(cogTrig, ...moniker..., cogTrigFull, () => IsPrimed, () => Trigger);
203: ```
204: 
205: ### Zpano: Output Array
206: 
207: Zpano replaces facades with an `Output` array (Go `core.Output` / TS `IndicatorOutput`):
208: 
209: **Go:**
210: ```go
211: func (s *X) updateEntity(time time.Time, sample float64) core.Output {
212:     output := make([]any, 2)
213:     cog := s.Update(sample)
214:     trig := s.valuePrevious
215:     if math.IsNaN(cog) { trig = math.NaN() }
216:     output[0] = entities.Scalar{Time: time, Value: cog}
217:     output[1] = entities.Scalar{Time: time, Value: trig}
218:     return output
219: }
220: ```
221: 
222: **TS:**
223: ```typescript
224: private updateEntity(time: Date, sample: number): IndicatorOutput {
225:     const cog = this.update(sample);
226:     let trig = this.valuePrevious;
227:     if (Number.isNaN(cog)) { trig = Number.NaN; }
228:     const s1 = new Scalar(); s1.time = time; s1.value = cog;
229:     const s2 = new Scalar(); s2.time = time; s2.value = trig;
230:     return [s1, s2];
231: }
232: ```
233: 
234: ### Output Enum
235: 
236: Each multi-output indicator gets a per-indicator output enum:
237: 
238: **Go:** `centerofgravityoscillatoroutput.go`
239: ```go
240: type Output int
241: const ( Value Output = iota; Trigger )
242: ```
243: 
244: **TS:** `center-of-gravity-oscillator-output.ts`
245: ```typescript
246: export enum CenterOfGravityOscillatorOutput { Value = 0, Trigger = 1 }
247: ```
248: 
249: ### Metadata for Multi-Output
250: 
251: Each output gets its own entry in `Metadata().Outputs`:
252: 
253: ```go
254: Outputs: []outputs.Metadata{
255:     { Kind: int(Value), Type: outputs.ScalarType, Mnemonic: s.mnemonic, Description: s.description },
256:     { Kind: int(Trigger), Type: outputs.ScalarType, Mnemonic: s.mnemonicTrig, Description: s.descriptionTrig },
257: },
258: ```
259: 
260: Facade-specific mnemonic patterns (like `cogTrig(10)`) are preserved as the Trigger
261: output mnemonic.
262: 
263: ---
264: 
265: ## Metadata Conversion
266: 
267: | MBST | Zpano |
268: |---|---|
269: | `Name` (e.g., `"cog"`) | `Metadata().Type` — use the registered `core.IndicatorType` enum |
270: | `Moniker` (e.g., `"cog(10)"`) | `Metadata().Mnemonic` — includes component triple |
271: | `Description` (e.g., `"Center of Gravity oscillator"`) | `Metadata().Description` — typically `"Full Name " + mnemonic` |
272: 
273: ### Register the Indicator Type
274: 
275: Before implementing, register the indicator in:
276: - **Go:** `go/indicators/core/type.go` — add enum constant, string, `String()`, `MarshalJSON`, `UnmarshalJSON`
277: - **TS:** `ts/indicators/core/indicator-type.ts` — add enum member
278: 
279: ---
280: 
281: ## What to Drop
282: 
283: ### From the Implementation
284: 
285: | MBST Element | Action |
286: |---|---|
287: | `[DataContract]`, `[DataMember]` | Drop — no serialization |
288: | `Reset()` method | Drop — zpano indicators are immutable |
289: | `ToString()` override | Drop — debug formatting |
290: | `lock (updateLock)` | Go: replace with `sync.RWMutex`. TS: drop entirely |
291: | `Update(Scalar)`, `Update(Ohlcv)` | Drop — handled by `LineIndicator` or `updateEntity` |
292: | `Update(double, DateTime)` | Drop — convenience overload |
293: | Facade properties (`ValueFacade`, `TriggerFacade`) | Drop — replaced by Output array |
294: | `OhlcvComponent` property setter | Drop — component is immutable after construction |
295: | C# regions (`#region ... #endregion`) | Drop — organizational noise |
296: | XML doc comments (`/// <summary>`) | Convert to Go doc comments / JSDoc |
297: 
298: ### From Tests
299: 
300: | MBST Test | Action |
301: |---|---|
302: | `ToStringTest()` | Drop |
303: | `SerializeTo()` / `SerializeFrom()` | Drop |
304: | `SerializationTest()` | Drop |
305: | Facade-specific tests | Drop (test via Output array instead) |
306: | `Reset()` tests | Drop |
307: | `[TestMethod]` / `[TestClass]` | Convert to Go `Test*` / TS Jasmine `describe`/`it` |
308: | `Assert.IsTrue(x == y)` | Go: require assertions. TS: Jasmine `expect(x).toBe(y)` |
309: 
310: ---
311: 
312: ## Test Conversion
313: 
314: ### Reference Data
315: 
316: MBST tests typically include large arrays of reference data (from TA-Lib Excel
317: simulations). **Preserve this data exactly** — it's the ground truth for numerical
318: verification.
319: 
320: Typical MBST test structure:
321: ```csharp
322: private readonly List<double> rawInput = new List<double> { ... };  // 252 entries
323: private readonly List<double> expected = new List<double> { ... };  // expected output
324: ```
325: 
326: Convert to:
327: - **Go:** `var` block with `[]float64` slices
328: - **TS:** `const` arrays of `number`
329: 
330: ### Test Categories to Convert
331: 
332: 1. **Output value tests** — Feed `rawInput`, compare against `expected` values.
333:    Use tolerance comparison: Go `require.InDelta(t, expected, actual, 1e-10)`,
334:    TS `expect(Math.abs(actual - expected)).toBeLessThan(1e-10)`.
335: 
336: 2. **IsPrimed tests** — Verify `IsPrimed()` is false during warmup and true after.
337: 
338: 3. **Metadata tests** — Verify `Metadata()` returns correct type, mnemonic, description, outputs.
339: 
340: 4. **Constructor validation** — Verify invalid params (e.g., `length < 1`) produce errors.
341: 
342: 5. **UpdateEntity tests** — Test `UpdateScalar`, `UpdateBar`, `UpdateQuote`, `UpdateTrade`
343:    with a few samples to verify entity routing works.
344: 
345: 6. **NaN handling** — Verify that NaN input produces NaN output without corrupting state.
346: 
347: ### Test for Multi-Output
348: 
349: For multi-output indicators, test **both** (all) outputs against reference data:
350: ```go
351: // After feeding sample:
352: output := indicator.UpdateScalar(scalar)
353: cogScalar := output[int(Value)].(entities.Scalar)
354: trigScalar := output[int(Trigger)].(entities.Scalar)
355: require.InDelta(t, expectedCog, cogScalar.Value, tolerance)
356: require.InDelta(t, expectedTrig, trigScalar.Value, tolerance)
357: ```
358: 
359: ### MBST Test Data with Separate High/Low Arrays
360: 
361: Some MBST tests provide raw high and low arrays separately, then compute median price
362: in the test. In zpano tests, either:
363: - Pre-compute the median values and store as a single input array, or
364: - Feed `Bar` entities with high/low set and test via `UpdateBar`.
365: 
366: The CoG tests use a pre-computed `rawInput` array of median prices (already `(high + low) / 2`).
367: 
368: ---
369: 
370: ## Worked Example: CenterOfGravityOscillator
371: 
372: ### Source Analysis
373: 
374: ```csharp
375: // MBST: extends Indicator directly, implements ILineIndicator
376: // Has Value + Trigger facades = multi-output
377: // Default OhlcvComponent: MedianPrice (non-default!)
378: public sealed class CenterOfGravityOscillator : Indicator, ILineIndicator
379: ```
380: 
381: ### Decision: Multi-output, direct Indicator interface
382: 
383: Because CoG has two outputs (Value, Trigger) and extends `Indicator` directly (not
384: `LineIndicator`), it uses the direct `Indicator` interface pattern in zpano.
385: 
386: ### Key Conversion Points
387: 
388: 1. **Default component**: `OhlcvComponent.MedianPrice` -> `BarMedianPrice` / `BarComponent.Median`.
389:    Not the framework default, so it always appears in the mnemonic (`, hl/2`).
390: 
391: 2. **Facades dropped**: `ValueFacade` and `TriggerFacade` replaced by Output array
392:    `[Scalar{cog}, Scalar{trigger}]`.
393: 
394: 3. **Output enum created**: `Value = 0`, `Trigger = 1`.
395: 
396: 4. **Algorithm**: `Update(double)` converted line-by-line. `Calculate()` helper preserved.
397:    `Array.Copy` replaced with explicit loop.
398: 
399: 5. **Priming**: Requires `length + 1` samples. First `length` fill the window; at index
400:    `length - 1` the initial CoG is computed and stored as `valuePrevious`; at index
401:    `length` the indicator becomes primed.
402: 
403: 6. **Mnemonic**: `cog(10, hl/2)` for default params. The `, hl/2` comes from
404:    `BarMedianPrice` being non-default relative to `DefaultBarComponent` (which is
405:    `BarClosePrice`).
406: 
407: 7. **Tests**: 252-entry reference data preserved. Serialization/ToString/Reset tests dropped.
408:    Added entity update tests, metadata tests, NaN tests.
409: 
410: ### Files Produced
411: 
412: **Go (5 files):**
413: - `centerofgravityoscillatorparams.go` — `Params` struct with `Length`, `BarComponent`, `QuoteComponent`, `TradeComponent`
414: - `centerofgravityoscillatoroutput.go` — `Output` enum: `Value`, `Trigger`
415: - `centerofgravityoscillatoroutput_test.go` — Output enum string tests
416: - `centerofgravityoscillator.go` — Main implementation (273 lines)
417: - `centerofgravityoscillator_test.go` — Tests with 252-entry data (659 lines)
418: 
419: **TS (4 files):**
420: - `center-of-gravity-oscillator-params.ts` — `CenterOfGravityOscillatorParams` interface
421: - `center-of-gravity-oscillator-output.ts` — `CenterOfGravityOscillatorOutput` enum
422: - `center-of-gravity-oscillator.ts` — Main implementation (227 lines)
423: - `center-of-gravity-oscillator.spec.ts` — Tests with 252-entry data
424: 
425: ---
426: 
427: ## Bar-Based Indicators (Non-LineIndicator Pattern)
428: 
429: Some indicators require bar data (high, low, close) rather than a single scalar component.
430: These indicators implement the `Indicator` interface directly without using `LineIndicator`
431: embedding/inheritance. Example: **TrueRange**.
432: 
433: ### Key Differences from LineIndicator
434: 
435: | Aspect | LineIndicator | Bar-Based (e.g., TrueRange) |
436: |---|---|---|
437: | Base type | Embeds `LineIndicator` (Go) / `extends LineIndicator` (TS) | Implements `Indicator` directly |
438: | Core method | `Update(float64)` / `update(number)` — single scalar | `Update(close, high, low float64)` — multiple values |
439: | Bar handling | Extracts one component via `barComponentFunc` | Extracts H, L, C directly from bar |
440: | Scalar/Quote/Trade | Routed through component function | Use single value as H=L=C substitute |
441: | Params | Typically has `Length`, component fields | May be empty (parameterless) |
442: | Components | `BarComponent`, `QuoteComponent`, `TradeComponent` | None — bar fields accessed directly |
443: 
444: ### Go Pattern
445: 
446: ```go
447: type TrueRange struct {
448:     mu            sync.RWMutex
449:     previousClose float64
450:     value         float64
451:     primed        bool
452: }
453: 
454: func (tr *TrueRange) Update(close, high, low float64) float64 { ... }
455: func (tr *TrueRange) UpdateSample(sample float64) float64 {
456:     return tr.Update(sample, sample, sample)
457: }
458: func (tr *TrueRange) UpdateBar(sample *entities.Bar) core.Output {
459:     output := make([]any, 1)
460:     output[0] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: tr.Update(sample.Close, sample.High, sample.Low)}
461:     return output
462: }
463: func (tr *TrueRange) UpdateScalar(sample *entities.Scalar) core.Output {
464:     v := sample.Value
465:     output := make([]any, 1)
466:     output[0] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: tr.Update(v, v, v)}
467:     return output
468: }
469: ```
470: 
471: ### TS Pattern
472: 
473: ```typescript
474: export class TrueRange implements Indicator {
475:     public update(close: number, high: number, low: number): number { ... }
476:     public updateSample(sample: number): number {
477:         return this.update(sample, sample, sample);
478:     }
479:     public updateBar(sample: Bar): IndicatorOutput {
480:         const scalar = new Scalar();
481:         scalar.time = sample.time;
482:         scalar.value = this.update(sample.close, sample.high, sample.low);
483:         return [scalar];
484:     }
485: }
486: ```
487: 
488: ### Test Data Extraction
489: 
490: For large test datasets (e.g., 252-entry TA-Lib arrays), extract data **programmatically**
491: from the C# test file using a Python script rather than manual transcription:
492: 
493: ```python
494: import re
495: pattern = rf'readonly List<double> {name} = new List<double>\s*\{{(.*?)\}};'
496: # Remove C-style comments before extracting numbers
497: body = re.sub(r'/\*.*?\*/', '', body)
498: ```
499: 
500: This avoids transcription errors that can be very hard to debug.
501: 
502: ---
503: 
504: ## Composite Indicators (Indicator-inside-Indicator Pattern)
505: 
506: Some indicators internally create and use other indicator instances. Example: **AverageTrueRange**
507: creates an internal **TrueRange** instance and delegates bar processing to it.
508: 
509: ### Key Points
510: 
511: - The inner indicator is a private field, created in the constructor
512: - The outer indicator calls the inner indicator's `Update()` method and processes the result
513: - Both Go and TS follow the same pattern: import the inner indicator's package and instantiate it
514: - The outer indicator manages its own priming state independently of the inner indicator
515: - In Go, the inner indicator's mutex is separate — no nested locking issues because `Update()`
516:   on the inner indicator acquires/releases its own lock before the outer lock is held
517: 
518: ### Go Example (AverageTrueRange)
519: 
520: ```go
521: import "zpano/indicators/welleswilder/truerange"
522: 
523: type AverageTrueRange struct {
524:     mu        sync.RWMutex
525:     trueRange *truerange.TrueRange  // internal indicator instance
526:     // ... other fields
527: }
528: 
529: func NewAverageTrueRange(length int) (*AverageTrueRange, error) {
530:     return &AverageTrueRange{
531:         trueRange: truerange.NewTrueRange(),
532:         // ...
533:     }, nil
534: }
535: 
536: func (a *AverageTrueRange) Update(close, high, low float64) float64 {
537:     trueRangeValue := a.trueRange.Update(close, high, low)  // delegate to inner
538:     // ... apply Wilder smoothing to trueRangeValue
539: }
540: ```
541: 
542: ### TS Example (AverageTrueRange)
543: 
544: ```typescript
545: import { TrueRange } from '../true-range/true-range';
546: 
547: export class AverageTrueRange implements Indicator {
548:     private readonly trueRange: TrueRange;
549: 
550:     constructor(length: number) {
551:         this.trueRange = new TrueRange();
552:     }
553: 
554:     public update(close: number, high: number, low: number): number {
555:         const trueRangeValue = this.trueRange.update(close, high, low);
556:         // ... apply Wilder smoothing to trueRangeValue
557:     }
558: }
559: ```
560: 
561: **Important:** Call the inner indicator's `Update()` **before** acquiring the outer lock in Go,
562: or call it inside the lock if the inner indicator uses its own separate mutex (as TrueRange does).
563: In the ATR implementation, `trueRange.Update()` is called inside the outer lock — this works
564: because TrueRange's mutex is independent.
565: 
566: ### Composing a Multi-Output Inner Indicator
567: 
568: When the inner indicator is itself multi-output (implements `Indicator` directly and
569: returns a tuple), the outer indicator typically wants to **re-expose some of the inner
570: outputs alongside its own**. Example: **SineWave** composes **DominantCycle** and
571: publishes 5 outputs: `Value`, `Lead`, `Band` (its own) + `DominantCyclePeriod`,
572: `DominantCyclePhase` (forwarded from the inner).
573: 
574: Rules:
575: 
576: 1. **Instantiate the inner using the `Params` constructor**, not `Default`, so the outer
577:    can forward parameters correctly (estimator params, `alphaEmaPeriodAdditional`,
578:    components, etc.).
579: 2. **Resolve component defaults in the outer first**, then pass the **resolved** values
580:    explicitly to the inner. This keeps mnemonics aligned across outputs — e.g., if the
581:    user overrides `BarComponent = Median`, **both** `sw(0.330, hl/2)` and the inner's
582:    `dcp(0.330, hl/2)` carry the suffix. If you skip this, the inner resolves its own
583:    defaults and the two mnemonics drift apart.
584: 3. **Call the inner's `Update()` before acquiring the outer lock** (inner manages its
585:    own mutex).
586: 4. **Forward outputs by index** into the outer's output tuple — don't re-compute. The
587:    outer's `updateEntity` assembles all N `Scalar`/`Band` entities from the inner's
588:    period/phase plus its own Value/Lead/Band.
589: 5. **Keep a single top-level `Mnemonic` / `Description`** on the outer's `Metadata`
590:    (the primary output's). Each forwarded inner output carries its own per-output
591:    mnemonic unchanged.
592: 
593: ```go
594: // Go — inside newSineWave, after resolving bc/qc/tc to non-zero defaults:
595: dc, err := dominantcycle.NewDominantCycleParams(&dominantcycle.Params{
596:     AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional: p.AlphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
597:     EstimatorType:            p.EstimatorType,
598:     EstimatorParams:          p.EstimatorParams,
599:     BarComponent:             bc, // resolved, not p.BarComponent
600:     QuoteComponent:           qc,
601:     TradeComponent:           tc,
602: })
603: ```
604: 
605: ```ts
606: // TS — inside the private SineWave constructor:
607: this.dominantCycle = DominantCycle.fromParams({
608:     alphaEmaPeriodAdditional: params.alphaEmaPeriodAdditional,
609:     estimatorType: params.estimatorType,
610:     estimatorParams: params.estimatorParams,
611:     barComponent: bc, quoteComponent: qc, tradeComponent: tc,
612: });
613: ```
614: 
615: ### Band Output Semantics When Wrapping MBST's `Band`
616: 
617: MBST's `Band(DateTime, firstValue, secondValue)` carries no upper/lower semantic — it's
618: just a pair. When a merged zpano indicator emits a `Band` output, the **wrapping
619: indicator must choose** which value becomes `Upper` and which becomes `Lower`.
620: 
621: Convention: **primary/fast value → `Upper`, secondary/slow value → `Lower`**. This
622: matches MAMA (`Upper: mama, Lower: fama`) and SineWave (`Upper: value, Lower: lead`).
623: Document the choice in a short comment above the `Band{}` construction so reviewers
624: don't have to cross-reference MBST to confirm ordering.
625: 
626: ---
627: 
628: ## Volume-Aware Indicators (UpdateWithVolume Pattern)
629: 
630: Some indicators require both a price sample and volume. Since `LineIndicator` only
631: supports `Update(float64)` / `update(number)`, these indicators need special handling.
632: 
633: **Example: MoneyFlowIndex (MFI)**
634: 
635: ### Pattern
636: 
637: 1. **Embed `LineIndicator`** as usual (single scalar output).
638: 2. **Add `UpdateWithVolume(sample, volume float64)`** as the real computation method.
639: 3. **`Update(sample)`** delegates to `UpdateWithVolume(sample, 1)` (volume=1 fallback).
640: 4. **Shadow/override `UpdateBar`** to extract both price (via `barFunc`) AND volume from
641:    the bar, then call `UpdateWithVolume`.
642: 
643: ### Go: Shadow `UpdateBar`
644: 
645: ```go
646: // MoneyFlowIndex shadows LineIndicator.UpdateBar to extract volume.
647: func (s *MoneyFlowIndex) UpdateBar(sample *entities.Bar) core.Output {
648:     s.mu.Lock()
649:     defer s.mu.Unlock()
650:     price := s.barFunc(sample)
651:     v := s.updateWithVolume(price, sample.Volume)
652:     output := make([]any, 1)
653:     output[0] = entities.Scalar{Time: sample.Time, Value: v}
654:     return output
655: }
656: ```
657: 
658: The shadowed `UpdateBar` on the concrete type takes precedence over `LineIndicator.UpdateBar`
659: when called on `*MoneyFlowIndex` directly or through the `Indicator` interface.
660: 
661: ### TS: Override `updateBar`
662: 
663: ```typescript
664: public override updateBar(sample: Bar): IndicatorOutput {
665:     const price = this.barFunc(sample);
666:     const v = this.updateWithVolume(price, sample.volume);
667:     const scalar = new Scalar();
668:     scalar.time = sample.time;
669:     scalar.value = v;
670:     return [scalar];
671: }
672: ```
673: 
674: ### Default BarComponent
675: 
676: MFI defaults to `BarTypicalPrice` / `BarComponent.Typical` (not `BarClosePrice`),
677: matching the C# default of `OhlcvComponent.TypicalPrice`.
678: 
679: ### Mnemonic
680: 
681: MFI uses `mfi(LENGTH)` — no component suffix, matching C# behavior.
682: 
683: ### Testing Volume-Aware Indicators
684: 
685: Test with two datasets:
686: 1. **Real volume** — `updateWithVolume(price, volume)` against expected values.
687: 2. **Volume=1** — `update(price)` (which uses volume=1) against a separate expected dataset.
688: 3. **UpdateBar test** — Feed `Bar` entities with real OHLCV data, verify first computed
689:    value matches the real-volume expected data.
690: 
691: ---
692: 
693: ## Test Data from Julia/CSV Reference (No C# Tests)
694: 
695: Some MBST indicators lack C# unit tests but have Julia reference implementations and CSV
696: test data files. Example: `SuperSmoother` has `ehlers_super_smoother.jl` and
697: `test_3-3_Supersmoother.csv`.
698: 
699: ### Key Differences: MBST vs Julia Priming
700: 
701: - **MBST priming:** Seeds previous filter values to the first sample value on count==1.
702: - **Julia priming:** Seeds previous filter values to zero (array initialized to zeros).
703: - After sufficient samples (~30-60), both converge to the same values.
704: 
705: ### CSV Test Data Strategy
706: 
707: 1. **Go tests:** Read the CSV file at test time using `encoding/csv`. Reference the CSV
708:    via a relative path (e.g., `../../../../mbst-to-convert/.../file.csv`).
709: 2. **TS tests:** Embed a representative subset (e.g., first 200 rows) directly in the
710:    spec file as `const` arrays (CSV reading at test time is more complex in TS).
711: 3. **Skip early rows:** Due to priming differences, skip the first N rows (e.g., 60)
712:    where MBST and Julia outputs diverge.
713: 4. **Tolerance:** Use a generous tolerance (e.g., 2.5) to account for:
714:    - CSV data rounded to 2 decimal places
715:    - Different priming initialization (MBST seeds to first sample, Julia to zero)
716:    - Convergence lag in the early samples
717: 5. **Julia test reference:** Check the Julia test file (`*_test.jl`) for the skip count
718:    and rounding precision used in the original Julia validation.
719: 
720: ---
721: 
722: ## Merging Multiple MBST Indicators Into One Zpano Indicator
723: 
724: Some MBST indicators are closely related (share a common internal computation) but are
725: exposed as **separate** C# classes. In zpano they should be merged into a **single
726: multi-output indicator** to avoid duplicated state/warm-up and simplify the API.
727: 
728: **Example: MBST `DominantCyclePeriod` + `DominantCyclePhase` → zpano `DominantCycle`** with
729: three outputs: `RawPeriod`, `Period` (EMA-smoothed), `Phase`.
730: 
731: ### When to Merge
732: 
733: Merge when all of the following hold:
734: 1. The MBST classes each own their own `HilbertTransformerCycleEstimator` (or other
735:    expensive internal state) but conceptually **compute the same thing**.
736: 2. They share the same primary parameter(s) (e.g., `alphaEmaPeriodAdditional` and
737:    estimator params).
738: 3. Users typically want multiple outputs together (reading phase requires the period
739:    anyway).
740: 
741: ### Pattern
742: 
743: - **One class, N outputs** — the indicator implements `Indicator` directly (MAMA-style),
744:   owns `barFunc`/`quoteFunc`/`tradeFunc`, and has an `updateEntity` helper emitting N
745:   `Scalar` entities (or `Band`, etc.) in the fixed output order.
746: - **One estimator instance**, one priming flag, one warm-up period for the whole
747:   indicator. The `primed` transition seeds all derived state in one shot.
748: - **`Update(sample)` returns a tuple** (Go: multi-return `(a, b, c)`, TS:
749:   `[a, b, c]` tuple). Each output has its own mnemonic; the indicator's top-level
750:   `Mnemonic()` returns the "primary" output's mnemonic (e.g., Period, not RawPeriod).
751: - **Output enum** lists all N outputs. Go: `RawPeriod Output = iota + 1, Period, Phase,
752:   outputLast`. TS: numeric enum with explicit `= 0, = 1, = 2`.
753: - **Single entry in `core.IndicatorType`** — one `DominantCycle` constant, not three.
754: 
755: ### Constructor Flavors
756: 
757: Follow MAMA: provide `NewDominantCycleDefault` / `static default()` and
758: `NewDominantCycleParams` / `static fromParams()`. A private constructor does the actual
759: work. Avoid length-vs-smoothing-factor duplication when the indicator has only a single
760: scalar parameter — use one `fromParams` flavor.
761: 
762: ### Merging on Top of Another Merged Indicator
763: 
764: The MBST indicators you're merging may themselves each instantiate an already-merged
765: zpano indicator (e.g., `SineWave`, `SineWaveLead`, `SineWaveBand` each own a
766: `DominantCyclePeriod`/`DominantCyclePhase` pair — already merged in zpano as
767: `DominantCycle`). In that case:
768: 
769: - **Don't duplicate the inner estimator in the outer.** Compose the already-merged
770:   inner indicator (see "Composing a Multi-Output Inner Indicator" above).
771: - **Forward the inner's relevant outputs** as additional outputs of the outer, so users
772:   don't need to instantiate both. SineWave's 5 outputs = its own 3 (Value/Lead/Band) +
773:   2 forwarded from DominantCycle (Period/Phase).
774: - **Reuse the inner's reference test arrays** where MBST's tests happen to embed them
775:   (MBST's SineWave test file contains the same `dcPeriod`/`dcPhase` arrays as the
776:   DominantCycle tests — port them once into the outer's `_test`).
777: 
778: ---
779: 
780: ## Warm-Up Period Defaults When Wrapping an HTCE
781: 
782: The `HilbertTransformerCycleEstimator` has two different default warm-up values depending
783: on how it's created:
784: 
785: | Code path | Default `WarmUpPeriod` |
786: |---|---|
787: | MBST C# constructor when caller passes `0` | `MaxPeriod * 2 = 100` |
788: | zpano HTCE internal auto-default (Go/TS) when caller passes `0` | `smoothingLengthPlus3HtLength ≈ 25` |
789: 
790: **This means the zpano HTCE primes earlier by default than MBST.** When porting an MBST
791: indicator that wraps HTCE and relies on the MBST default (`warmUpPeriod = MaxPeriod * 2`),
792: the zpano indicator's default factory **must explicitly set `warmUpPeriod: 100`** in the
793: estimator params — otherwise the port primes ~75 samples earlier and diverges from the
794: MBST reference test data.
795: 
796: ```go
797: // Go: inside NewDominantCycleDefault
798: &hilberttransformer.CycleEstimatorParams{
799:     SmoothingLength:           4,
800:     AlphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: 0.2,
801:     AlphaEmaPeriod:            0.2,
802:     WarmUpPeriod:              100, // NOT 0 — MBST default is MaxPeriod*2
803: }
804: ```
805: 
806: ```typescript
807: // TS: inside static default()
808: estimatorParams: {
809:     smoothingLength: 4,
810:     alphaEmaQuadratureInPhase: 0.2,
811:     alphaEmaPeriod: 0.2,
812:     warmUpPeriod: 100, // NOT omitted — MBST default is MaxPeriod*2
813: }
814: ```
815: 
816: When writing tests that call `NewDominantCycleParams(...)` with arbitrary estimator
817: params, passing `WarmUpPeriod: 0` is fine — the HTCE auto-default kicks in and the test
818: isn't comparing against MBST reference data anyway (it's comparing mnemonic shape, error
819: handling, etc.).
820: 
821: ---
822: 
823: ## Handling Structurally-Divergent Reference Data
824: 
825: Occasionally the reference data you're validating against comes from an implementation
826: that is **structurally different** from MBST's — e.g., TA-Lib Excel templates that
827: produce output from bar 0 by smoothing through zeros, while the MBST port follows a
828: strict priming convention (NaN until HTCE primed, then seed-EMA).
829: 
830: When this happens, an exact match is **algorithmically impossible** for the early portion
831: of the output series. Both implementations converge later because each subsequent EMA
832: step shrinks the seed error by `(1-α)`.
833: 
834: ### Detection
835: 
836: - You've ported the algorithm line-by-line from MBST/C# and the code is correct.
837: - Early output values diverge from reference by a lot (e.g., 20+ units); late values
838:   match to 1e-5 or better.
839: - Error decays geometrically, not randomly.
840: 
841: ### Test Strategy
842: 
843: 1. **Add a `settleSkip` constant** (tuned empirically) — the index past which
844:    convergence is tight enough to assert.
845: 2. **Sanity-check earlier indices** — assert output is finite / non-NaN, but skip the
846:    value comparison.
847: 3. **Use a realistic tolerance** (e.g., `1e-4`) that accommodates both residual seed
848:    error at `settleSkip` and late-series floating-point accumulation.
849: 4. **Document the rationale in a block comment** above `settleSkip` — future maintainers
850:    must understand this is algorithmic, not a port bug.
851: 
852: ```go
853: const (
854:     skip       = 9   // TradeStation convention — skip first N bars.
855:     settleSkip = 177 // Samples required for EMA to converge past structural reference mismatch.
856: )
857: 
858: for i := skip; i < len(input); i++ {
859:     _, period, _ := dc.Update(input[i])
860:     if math.IsNaN(period) || i < settleSkip {
861:         continue
862:     }
863:     if math.Abs(expPeriod[i]-period) > tolerance {
864:         t.Errorf(...)
865:     }
866: }
867: ```
868: 
869: ### Phase/Angle Comparisons — Modulo 360
870: 
871: If the reference produces phase values **outside** the MBST port's `(-90°, 360°]` range
872: (e.g., 639.09°, -42.95°), you cannot compare directly. Use a `phaseDiff` helper that
873: computes the shortest signed angular difference modulo 360:
874: 
875: ```go
876: func phaseDiff(a, b float64) float64 {
877:     d := math.Mod(a-b, 360)
878:     if d > 180 {
879:         d -= 360
880:     } else if d <= -180 {
881:         d += 360
882:     }
883:     return d
884: }
885: ```
886: 
887: Then assert `math.Abs(phaseDiff(expected, actual)) < tolerance`.
888: 
889: Same helper applies in TS, using `(a - b) % 360` (JavaScript's `%` is remainder, which
890: preserves the sign of the dividend — same semantics as `math.Mod` for this purpose).

(End of file - total 890 lines)
</content>
```

---
